# Pathways for professionals

Regardless of your education or background, we’ll meet you where you are.

## Find your next step

### Your story matters here

You don’t need a degree to build a meaningful career with us—your skills and experiences are valued and amplified. We’re here to help you grow and develop through hands-on learning, 500+ training programs, and benefits, like tuition assistance and childcare.

### You are supported here

Connect with a global network of peers, mentors, and leaders who support your continued growth. Whether you’re exploring new directions, returning to the workforce, or looking to broaden your expertise, our Global Mentoring Program makes it easy to connect with a mentor from almost anywhere in the world.

### Your impact multiplies here

Contribute to innovative projects and initiatives that make a difference—within our firm and across the communities we serve. As a professional at JPMorganChase, your work has real-world influence on the future of banking and beyond.

## Inside the programs

### Emerging Talent Software Engineers

A technical and professional development program for individuals new to software engineering that welcomes applicants from non-traditional pathways, like coding bootcamps and community college. Gain practical skills and experience in software engineering alongside peers in a supportive cohort, while adding value as an entry-level member of an engineering team.

[Learn more](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/emerging-talent-software-engineers-fulltime "Emerging Talent Software Engineers")

### Experienced Software Engineer Hiring

A streamlined path to full-time, experienced software engineering roles. Successful candidates are matched to teams and opportunities based on their strengths, interests, and skills. With openings across the globe, this offering provides a direct and supportive route into software engineering positions.

[Learn more](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/software-engineer-cohort "software engineer cohort")

### Military & Athletes Programme

A program offering specialised support for service members and professional athletes transitioning to corporate life in the UK and beyond. Leverage your leadership and problem-solving experience in a corporate environment with dedicated mentorship and resources.

[Learn more](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/military-uk "military uk")

### ReEntry Program

A 15-week paid fellowship program designed for professionals ready to relaunch their careers after being away from the workforce for two or more years. Participants receive support as they navigate skills sessions, on-the-job training, coaching, and developmental experiences, all aimed at helping them transition back into the workplace and prepare for a long-term career.

[Learn more](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/reentry-program "reentry program")

# Asia Analyst Development Program

Get exposure to a range of the firm's key lines of business and establish a holistic view of how we run our global business. You will add value to high-priority projects, while enhancing your core competencies.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

Your day-to-day responsibilities will vary based on your placement, which can be within any division across front to back office, such as Investment Banking, Markets, Operations, Finance, Product Management, Client Services, Legal, and Compliance.

You’ll analyze and present key data, work on projects to improve our products and offerings, and find ways to best reach our customers. By building your professional skills and experiences, you will be able to advance your career with numerous possibilities.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We expect motivated top talent with a strong desire and passion for financial services. We’re looking for exceptional communicators with the desire to drive continuous improvement. The vision and initiative of developing innovative solutions that make the most of our resources will be essential. Your excellent project management and problem-solving skills will also be required.

#### **Key skills**

We seek individuals eager to learn and develop new skills by being open to assignments in various parts of the bank/business. We are looking for strong team players who demonstrate flexibility and eagerness to work in a fast-paced, collaborative environment. Your excellent interpersonal and communication skills will be critical to your success in this program. You should be able to identify the important information from a large pool of data and use that to develop innovative solutions, while maintaining a strong attention to detail.

### What we offer

You’ll have the opportunity to make meaningful contributions while developing your professional expertise in a dynamic team environment supporting trade support, middle office, banking operations, or other areas in the Commercial & Investment Bank.

#### **On-the-job experience**

Working on a collaborative team, you will learn how our businesses operate. You will be contributing to high-priority initiatives and delivering solutions to make real impact. You will be exposed to both revenue and non-revenue-generating divisions from front, middle to back office roles throughout the program.

#### **Training**

The rotational program offers an unique opportunity to sharpen your technical, analytical, and leadership skills. During the internship, you will be placed into a team across our front, middle, or back office, receive hands-on training, and gain insights into how our businesses come together.

#### **Career progression**

Leveraging the experience you acquire throughout the program, it is designed to help you develop general management and operational skills, giving you flexibility in your career direction.

# Global Finance & Business Management Analyst Program

Deepen your understanding of the firm, while learning how we operate, generate revenue, and manage expenses when you join our global finance and business management team. You’ll support specific lines of business or corporate functions, working on projects that directly affect the firm’s bottom line alongside top-tier professionals.

Because you’ll be analyzing results and providing insights to business leaders to help make decisions, we welcome your perspectives from day one. You’ll combine sophisticated analysis methods, accurate reporting, and financial discipline to ensure the firm is capable of navigating all types of market environments, while maintaining a fortress balance sheet.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

While your responsibilities will vary based on the role, you’ll help generate meaningful analysis and reporting, coordinate and collaborate with partners across the firm on strategic initiatives, generate budgets and forecasts, and contribute to important projects across businesses. Start gaining the skills you need for a wide-ranging and rewarding career that can take you in any direction.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We are looking for highly motivated individuals with a passion for producing financial analyses to help drive strategic business decisions and the adaptability to work on many different types of projects. If you have excellent leadership, interpersonal, and communications skills and thrive working in a collaborative, dynamic environment, then this is the role for you.

#### **Key skills**

You should have a basic understanding of financial statements, accounting procedures, internal and external reporting, and variance analysis. We’re looking for those with well-rounded academic backgrounds and a strong interest in financial management and data and analytics. Those who are analytical, curious leaders with a passion for critical thinking and working as part of a community to shape the future will enjoy the many opportunities available here.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll spend the summer working with a team on various initiatives that drive key business results, such as preparing external reporting to meet requirements, gathering internal and external data, financial budgeting, or forecasting.

#### **Training**

The program begins with a one-day orientation. You’ll then have access to various senior executives via our speaker events, mentors, and training programs to increase your technical and soft skills.

#### **Career progression**

After completing the program, highly motivated analysts may be offered an opportunity for a continued employment with the firm.

# Marketing Leadership Development Program

We’re looking for applicants with a passion for marketing, a creative mindset, and the strategic ability to turn insights into actionable results. You’ll be part of a cross line of business program that is integral to the future of marketing at the firm and will give you the skills to build your career at JPMorganChase.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

The program starts with hands-on training, followed by three eight-month-long rotations across our lines of business and corporate marketing team. You’ll build your foundational knowledge, professional skills, and experiences in the following areas: Marketing Analytics, Strategy and Execution, and Brand/Creative.

You’ll work on highly strategic projects, including supporting the delivery of effective plans to drive brand awareness and engagement, engaging with creative partners to support the development and delivery of impactful marketing programs, and using data to support decision-making.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued Qualities & Skills:**

Excellent project management and problem-solving skills, exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, and aptitude to digest large amounts of information and use it to develop innovative solutions with strong attention to detail.

### What we offer

In this two-year program, with three 8-month rotations, you will have the opportunity to learn from and collaborate with experts to help define and manage our brands, conduct market research, and drive customer acquisition and engagement.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You will collaborate with our partners to produce marketing and promotional materials, oversee external vendors and agencies, and measure the impact and effectiveness of marketing campaigns.

#### **Training**

Over the course of the program, you’ll have access to development training, networking, business deep dives, and one-on-one mentorship.

#### **Career progression**

Throughout your rotations, we’ll provide ongoing support to prepare and empower you to take the next step in your marketing career. Upon successfully completing the program, you’ll have the opportunity to transition to a new role within our Marketing organization.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/marketing-fulltime-inside-program.jpg)

# Design Development Program Internship

Start your career by joining our Experience Design and Research teams to learn, grow, and contribute to JPMorganChase’s portfolio of work.

This summer internship will guide you as you create dynamic visual solutions, research the needs of our users, and drive design thinking on matters that add value to our business and have real-life impact on our customers.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

During your summer internship, you will have the opportunity to design and redesign product experiences under the guidance and direction of user experience leads, participate in user research to better understand user needs and behaviors, and engage in usability testing.

You’ll focus on ideation and prototyping to explore different design options, landing on unexpected solutions.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for passionate, dynamic, and motivated individuals with applicable undergraduate majors and an interest in user experience design, interaction design, or user research.

We are looking for people who are open to collaborating with others and demonstrate excellent teamwork skills.

#### **Key skills**

We’re looking for individuals who demonstrate early development in adjacent skills such as user research, UX writing, and prototyping.

### What we offer

Learn and develop your skill set in user design or user research as a member of our Experience Design team.

This summer internship will guide you as you create dynamic visual solutions, research the needs of our users, and drive design thinking on matters that add value to our business and have real-life impact on our customers.

#### **On-the-job experience**

From the start, you’ll have the support you need to succeed. Dive head-first and gain hands-on experience creating innovative solutions that make a difference for our customers, clients, and employees.

Our managers receive extensive training on how to help interns make the most of their program. You’ll have clear priorities and frequent check-ins throughout the internship to make sure you’re achieving your goals.

Interns are encouraged to take time to explore, shadowing other teams and networking with various people. We offer social events and other activities in our business resource groups to help you expand your professional network across our lines of business.

#### **Training**

You'll work on teams with peers and experienced designers and researchers to grow your skills, share ideas, and innovate with our technology community all over the world. We provide deeper insight into what it means to work here through networking events, senior speaker sessions, and peer mentorship programs.

#### **Career Progression**

Those who successfully complete the program may be offered a position in our full-time [Design Development Program](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/design-dev-fulltime "Full time").

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/design-dev-summer.jpg)

### What you'll do

During your summer internship, you will have the opportunity to design and redesign product experiences under the guidance and direction of user experience leads, participate in user research to better understand user needs and behaviors, and engage in usability testing.

You’ll focus on ideation and prototyping to explore different design options, landing on unexpected solutions.

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for passionate, dynamic, and motivated individuals with applicable undergraduate majors and an interest in user experience design, interaction design, or user research.

We are looking for people who are open to collaborating with others and demonstrate excellent teamwork skills.

#### **Key skills**

We’re looking for individuals who demonstrate early development in adjacent skills such as user research, UX writing, and prototyping.

Learn and develop your skill set in user design or user research as a member of our Experience Design team.

This summer internship will guide you as you create dynamic visual solutions, research the needs of our users, and drive design thinking on matters that add value to our business and have real-life impact on our customers.

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/career-edyou#main)

# Career.edYOU Academy

Career.edYOU Academy is a selective, virtual educational program designed for college sophomores in the United States interested in a career at JPMorganChase. This program will deliver an immersive experience introducing you to internship opportunities in finance and technology, while providing insight into the recruiting process. This experience will prepare you for summer internships!

**Registration is closed**

2024 Career.edYOU Academy highlights include:

- 910





sophomore students participated in the program

- 246





colleges and universities represented among participants

- 99%





of survey respondents felt better prepared for the interview process[1](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/career-edyou#footnote-1)

- 100%





of survey respondents would recommend Career.edYOU Academy to a friend[1](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/career-edyou#footnote-1)


## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You will receive a syllabus of virtual sessions that will need to be completed in sequential order over the course of the semester. In these live virtual sessions, we will introduce you to our firm, culture, lines of business, and interview preparation. You will have the opportunity to connect virtually with the recruiting team and other employees at the firm to grow your network.

Your participation will be tracked to ensure you have completed the program and marked for early consideration. The more engaged you are in this virtual program, the better prepared you will be for the application process.

This virtual program is designed for college sophomores studying in the United States.

We are looking for students who are willing to engage each session’s content and presenters, dedicated to completing the program, and eager to leverage the knowledge gained throughout the semester-long program.

In order to maximize your virtual experience, we are looking for students eager to learn and engage in the sessions. You must be motivated and willing to connect with others and grow your network. These skills are essential for success within this exploratory program.

# Innovate for Impact Case Study Competition

The Innovate for Impact Case Study Competition puts you at the center of the action. You’ll work in teams of four to tackle a hypothetical business challenge, gaining insights into three of our Corporate Functions Early Careers programs. Along the way, you’ll build industry knowledge and strengthen your communication and analytical skills—preparing you for success in our internship and full-time opportunities.

Registration is closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

As a participant, you’ll form a team of four students from your university to collaborate with on a business challenge. You’ll gain insights into our businesses, connect with senior leaders, and develop skills to prepare you for future opportunities at the firm.

The competition consists of a single round, with participants working closely with case study mentors. The first half is virtual, followed by an invitation for teams to present live, in person, to JPMorganChase business representatives. You’ll also have the opportunity to learn more about our internship programs, in particular:

- [Corporate Analyst Development Program](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/cadp-summer-analyst)
- [Global Finance and Business Management Analyst Program](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/finance-summer-analyst)
- [Internal Audit Analyst Program](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/internal-audit-summer-internship)

### Who we're looking for

#### **Desired qualities**

We’re seeking college sophomores studying in the United States who are interested in our Corporate Functions Early Careers programs. Participants are motivated, clear communicators, and analytical thinkers. If you are curious, focused, enjoy collaboration, and like solving new challenges, this is the opportunity for you.

#### **Key skills**

We value strong problem-solving skills and adaptability in dynamic environments. The ideal participant is proactive, responsive, and excels in fast-paced, collaborative settings. Effective time management, prioritization, and communication skills are essential. We are especially interested in students considering full-time opportunities in Columbus, OH, or Newark/Wilmington, DE after graduation.

### What we offer

#### **Introduction**

Over three months, you’ll attend virtual sessions designed to build your skills and introduce you to JPMorganChase. Each participant is assigned two mentors for guidance throughout the competition.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll work in teams to analyze a hypothetical business challenge, gaining insights into three of our Corporate Functions internship programs. This experience will help you strengthen your industry knowledge, communication, and analytical skills, and identify which program best fits your interests and goals.

#### **Training**

Teams that complete the curriculum will be invited to present live to a panel of judges and JPMorganChase business representatives.

#### **Career progression**

Participants who perform well may be considered for future internship opportunities.

# Grow with us

Through a wide range of opportunities to learn and grow in your career, we're supporting the future success of our greatest asset: you.

### Career mobility

We’re proud to be one of the largest financial institutions in the world, and there are many possibilities to shape your career and explore new business areas, job functions and geographic locations. Our rich history spans back over 200 years , and you can play a pivotal role in building our future.

### Lasting connections

Develop meaningful connections with colleagues across the globe by engaging in a diverse range of opportunities. These include our Business Resource Groups, which promote leadership, growth and networking connections.

### Learning opportunities

Whether you’re beginning an internship or have years of experience, your learning never stops. You’ll have access to training, mentorships and more—all designed to help you build the skills you need for every stage of your career.

### Paths to leadership

There are many ways to become a leader. We offer opportunities and resources to help you refine your skills and grow into leadership roles that fit your career path.

# Human Resources Analyst Development Program

Our people are our greatest asset, and investing in attracting, retaining, and developing them is a top priority. Working in Human Resources is more than payroll and benefits—we have programs to recruit and train our talent, as well as help them build fulfilling, rewarding careers. We’re constantly adding new initiatives to create engaged employees who are developing new skills. Joining our community means impacting millions of people and making sure we have the right talent to innovate for the future.

Applications are currently closed

## Apply now



Please note, only locations where this program is currently open will be displayed. If you're not able to find your desired location, please follow us on Facebook or Linkedin for program application updates.


## Inside the program

### What you'll do

While your day-to-day responsibilities will vary depending on the function and line of business, you’ll make a direct impact on our employee programs. Projects may include contributing to our industry-leading performance management, training and development programs; working on recruiting initiatives, learning how to build successful teams, and working with business partners on strategy, planning, and project management.

You’ll analyze and present data to senior business leaders, help with program management, and work with businesses to improve training. By owning projects right from the beginning, you’ll refine your skills while building your professional network across the firm through mentorships and working with senior leaders.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for highly motivated, analytical, and passionate people who will challenge the way we do things and aren’t intimidated to provide feedback so we can improve. You should be flexible, adaptable, and enjoy working with people. We’re always looking for innovative ways to improve our processes and engage employees, so bring your best ideas.

#### **Key skills**

Those with strong project management, communication, interpersonal, and strategic thinking skills will do well on our teams. You should be collaborative, open to new challenges, a natural problem-solver, and take ownership of your work.

### What we offer

Experience the breadth of opportunities in human resources at one of the world’s largest financial services firms. This two-year rotational program will develop your core skills, and help you make connections that advance your career.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll contribute to initiatives involving compensation, recruiting, performance management, employee relations, and more. Your work might include creating and presenting data packages to senior leaders and collaborating on compensation and succession plans.

#### **Training**

You’ll begin with a two to three-week induction training, which provides an overview of human resources and the firm’s business lines, and professional and technical skills development. You’ll benefit from continued training and attend speaker and networking events.

#### **Career progression**

After successfully completing the program, highly motivated analysts will be on track for increased responsibilities and career growth opportunities. You’ll be able to explore opportunities within Human Resources, either in a corporate group or an individual line of business.

# Software Engineer Program   (12 Month Placement)

You'll benefit from a multi-billion dollar annual investment in technology, working in one of the world’s biggest tech companies. You’ll work in an open, collaborative, and supportive culture, where our agile teams are constantly innovating, learning new skills, and are at the forefront of developing new technologies and solutions.

With the scale of our business, you could impact millions of consumers, thousands of enterprise clients , and 300,000+ employees. We’re committed to advancing your career helping you acquire new skills, opportunities, and a global network of support that will help you take your career in any direction imaginable.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

As a 12-month industrial placement intern, you’ll help build and optimize digital applications and systems that support millions of customers, clients, and employees worldwide. The industrial placement will begin with an induction training covering our tech strategies, products, and systems, as well as an overview of our technology community.  You will work in an agile team with peers and experienced software engineers to grow your skills, share ideas and innovate with our global technology community. You’ll gain deeper insight into what it means to work here through networking events, senior speaker sessions, and peer-mentorship programs.

### Who we're looking for

**Valued qualities**

We are seeking well-rounded, enthusiastic, and motivated individuals, particularly those majoring in Computer Science and/or Engineering, who are eager to make a direct impact on our business from day one. Regardless of your background, we value candidates with a strong interest in financial services who are attuned to industry-wide technology trends and best practices, and who possess a thirst for learning, growth, and innovation.

Collaboration is at the heart of our teams, so we are looking for candidates with excellent teamwork skills and demonstrated leadership abilities. You should be able to work effectively in large, collaborative teams to achieve organizational goals and be passionate about fostering an innovative culture.

**Key skills**

We are looking for applicants who possess a foundational knowledge of programming languages, such as Python, JavaScript, Java, C++, or C#, along with expertise in databases, data structures, and algorithms. Ideal applicants will have experience across the entire software development life cycle (SDLC) and exposure to agile methodologies, including continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), application resiliency, and security. We value emerging knowledge of software applications and technical processes within specialized disciplines, like cloud computing, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and mobile technologies. Additionally, a solid understanding of software skills, including business analysis, development, maintenance, and software improvement is essential for success in our dynamic environment.

### What we offer

This industrial placement will give you the opportunity to learn about our technology business and develop your career. You will work on projects that deliver real solutions for our customers clients, and businesses. No matter if you’re working on payment solutions or trading algorithms around the world, you’ll see tangible results from your work.

**On-the-job experience**

Dive headfirst into creating innovative solutions that make a difference for our customers, clients, and employees. Our program is designed to make sure you’re supported and learning new skills. You’ll have clear priorities and projects where you’ll be able to make a difference across our business and add value from the start.

You are encouraged to take time to explore, shadowing other teams and networking with various people. You'll be fully integrated into our technology community with the opportunity to attend social events and tech talks, interact with senior leaders, and more.

**Training**

You’ll begin with a comprehensive firmwide orientation, followed by an induction training   to teach you about our businesses, build upon your knowledge of development methodologies, and also develop your professional skills.

You’ll have access to continuous training to build your technical and business skills. We’ll cover topics ranging from cybersecurity to presentation skills to further your career development. Our teams are dedicated to your support and advocacy throughout the ten weeks of the program.

**Career progression**

Top performers may receive the opportunity to join us for a full-time role in the [Software Engineer Program](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/software-engineer-fulltime "Software Engineer Program") upon graduation.

### Where to learn more

Explore life at JPMorganChase with this free & self-paced virtual experience. To learn more and register, visit the [Software Engineering](https://www.theforage.com/simulations/jpmorgan/advanced-software-engineering-r0fm "Forage") page on Forage.

\*Registration or completion of Forage virtual experience programs is optional and will not impact consideration or hiring decisions.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/technology-12-month-analyst.jpg)

### What you'll do

As a

# Chase Digital Development Programme

We know that people want great value combined with an excellent experience, from a bank they can trust. That's why we're revolutionizing mobile banking, by creating seamless digital journeys that our customers will love. For us, that means keeping ourselves customer-obsessed and always being open to trying new things. Above all, it's about working with people who are passionate about building the bank of the future.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/chase-digital-development-programme-fulltime#first-apply-now-link)

## Apply now



Please note, only locations where this program is currently open will be displayed. If you're not able to find your desired location, please follow us on Facebook or Linkedin for program application updates.


- [**2026 Chase Digital Development Programme – Full-time Analyst (London)** \\
\\
Deadline: February 15, 2026\\
\\
Europe, Middle East, & Africa\\
\\
London\\
\\

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

Our programme gives you the tools and experience you need to become a skilled Product Manager, by empowering you to harness insights and help us build innovative customer experiences in a full-time, two-year programme. Your time will be made up of three eight-month rotations, so you can see what goes into developing intuitive experiences, from start to finish.

As a Product Manager, you'll get involved with every part of the process. From coming up with fresh ideas, penning strategies and iterating designs with our developers, to implementing features with our engineers, and looking at marketing, data and servicing with our customer experience team – you'll see exactly what it takes to get genius ideas into the hands of our customers.

### Who we're looking for

We understand that everyone's unique – and that diversity of thought, experience, and backgrounds is what makes a good team great. By bringing people with different points of view together, we can represent everyone and truly reflect the communities we serve. Specifically for this programme, we're looking for graduates in their final year, or who graduated less than a year ago.

Fintech is a fast-paced, ever-evolving world. So, we like working with people who are adaptable, open to new things, and don't mind making mistakes. To help us tackle problems together, we also look for skills like critical thinking and problem-solving, as well as having a curious mindset and being a great communicator.

So that you can get the most out of the programme, having a motivated, resourceful spirit is key, as well as a hunger to learn more about technology and financial services. If you're a graduate with fresh ideas and an entrepreneurial mindset, we'd love to hear from you.

### What we offer

#### **Collaborative culture**

By joining the Chase Digital Development Programme, you'll be part of a team that’s friendly, committed, hard-working, and growth-orientated. Over two years, we'll show you how to build new products and journeys, all while balancing business innovation with what our customers need—and making our mark as an exciting player in fintech.

#### **International growth**

Since we launched a digital bank in the UK, we're planning our next phase of growth, including developing new products and expanding into new markets – so it's an incredibly exciting time to jump on board.

#### **Career development**

You'll work with teams across lots of different areas of the business, and have access to a huge range of skill sets, leadership styles, and opportunities to learn.

Through this network, we'll help you evolve your professional skills with mentorship, training, and plenty of hands-on experience. You'll also spend time learning from your peers as you support each other to grow.

#### **Long term opportunities**

After the programme, we'll work together to find the best fit for you. We're currently expanding our European footprint – so if you have a second language, be sure to let us know.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/chase-digital-development-programme-fulltime.jpg)

### What you'll do

Our programme gives you the tools and experience you need to become a skilled Product Manager, by empowering you to harness insights and help us build innovative customer experiences in a full-time, two-year programme. Your time will be made up of three eight-month rotations, so you can see what goes into developing intuitive experiences, from start to finish.

As a Product Manager, you'll get involved with every part of the process. From coming up with fresh ideas, penning strategies and iterating designs with our developers, to implementing features with our engineers, and looking at marketing, data and servicing with our customer experience team – you'll see exactly what it takes to get genius ideas into the hands of our customers.

Are you interested in designing and coding a next-generation web application used by millions of users every day? Do you wish to help us scale to over a billion users?  If you have answered a resounding “YES!” for all the above, then we may have a position for you! 

The Outlook Engineering team designs and implements services to support different Outlook clients which have hundreds of millions of active users. We are building the next generation of Co-Pilot and Artificial Intelligence (AI) driven productivity features in email to help everyone on the planet to do more and stay organized. And we are looking for a Software Engineer II who is passionate about creating best-in-class intelligent user experiences and eager to join a team that moves fast, is data-driven, and cares deeply about our customers. You will be part of a passionate and creative team working to modernize the way people collaborate and communicate. This is a great opportunity to prove yourself and accelerate your career growth.  

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond. 



Responsibilities
  • Works with appropriate stakeholders (e.g., program manager, technical lead) to determine user requirements for a set of features. Begins to leverage a variety of feedback channels to incorporate insights into future designs or solution fixes. Incorporates appropriate continuous feedback loops measuring customer value, usage patterns, and other actionable metrics of value.
  • Contributes to processes for the architecture of a product/solution feature and helps to create proposals for architecture by testing design hypotheses and helping to refine code plans, with technical leadership from others. Provides initial reactions and input to architects. Begins to own solutions for architecting of solutions, following technical leadership as applicable. Contributes to the development of design documents for designs or User Stories with oversight, and determines the technology that will be leveraged and how it will interact. Escalates and shares findings from investigations with the team and owns some design decisions. Helps to assure system architecture meets security and compliance requirements and expectations.
  • Creates and implements code for a product, service, or feature, reusing code as applicable. Writes and learns to create cod

# Our benefits

![Photo of employee with child](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/life/Benefits-ImageTextComponent1.jpg)

- ### Support that goes beyond the workplace




At Microsoft, doing your best work begins with being empowered to live your best life. That belief drives everything we offer—from world-class benefits that support your physical, mental and emotional, and financial wellbeing, to a culture built around growth, purpose, and care.



A belief in balance and purpose guides how we support employees. Across regions and roles, employees are supported with benefits that reflect what matters most—wellbeing, flexibility, and growth. We offer generous time off, parental leave, wellbeing programs, and performance-based compensation, including competitive pay, bonuses, and stock awards, reflect a commitment to helping people thrive in all aspects of life.



But thriving isn’t just about what you receive—it’s about what you experience. Here, curiosity is encouraged, bold ideas are celebrated, and learning never stops. Mentorship and coaching are part of how we work, and growth happens through connection, experimentation, and shared ambition.



No matter where you are or what role you’re in, the support to grow, the space to learn, and the care to thrive are always within reach.


## How we work (Microsoft's hybrid workplace)

- * * *




Flexibility that fuels progress



- * * *




Flexibility defined




- Flexibility at Microsoft isn’t one‑size‑fits‑all. Where appropriate, leaders and teams set their in‑office cadence based on the work, role, and location, and we publish worksite expectations in every job posting for clarity. That flexibility fuels strong collaboration and accelerates decision-making while balancing customer needs and business outcomes.







The focus isn’t on where you work, it’s on enabling individual and team progress and being there for you at every stage. We accomplish this with a commitment to:







  - Digital‑first collaboration across time zones and work styles
  - Team rhythms that build trust (regular check‑ins and agreements)
  - Wellbeing support to help you thrive at work and in life

- Transparency matters. We believe clarity helps you make confident career decisions. That’s why every Microsoft job posting includes details about where and how the work happens – so you know what to expect before you apply.







  - Work site — Required in‑office days at a Microsoft‑designated location (e.g., “0 days / week in-office – remote” to “Fully on‑site”), based on role, primary residence, and organization‑specific guidelines; shown in the job posting. Examples of work site fields in a job posting:

0 days / week in-office – remote

1 day / week in-office

2 days / week in-office

3 days / week in-office

4 days / week in-office

Fully on‑site

  - Work location — City/region/country where the role can be based. “Multiple locations” may indicate several options within a listed country. Check the Work site field for in‑office expectations.

  - Work hours — Hours vary by role and team; align with your manager and teammates to support collaboration and outcomes.

# Microsoft Aspire

- ### From Campus to Career: The Microsoft Aspire Experience




Are you ready to take the first steps into your career and put your talents to work in the real world? Do you want to play a vital part in the business success of a high-tech global leader? Would you like to experience an inspiring world class program? If so, then the Microsoft Aspire Experience is for you.



Microsoft Aspire is a 1+ year learning and development journey designed for new full-time employees from undergraduate, graduate, MBA, PhD, MSSA, or LEAP backgrounds once you’ve started in your role at Microsoft. This global program, where enrollment is automatic once you are hired and start at Microsoft from a qualifying program, is designed to help you kick start your career at Microsoft, offering unique programs and events designed to accelerate your growth, build your network, and discover opportunities.



We understand the importance of working in a role where your talents will shine, your interests will grow, and your passions will inspire your peers. Join us and begin your Microsoft journey today.

- ### Pursuing passions and making a difference




Our Datacenter pathways are crafted to help individuals like YOU from diverse backgrounds step into exciting, hands-on technician careers that make a global impact. These pathways open doors to opportunities you may not have initially considered, introducing you to one of today’s most dynamic industries. To thrive in a technician role in a Datacenter, we recommend those with experience in IT, electrical, mechanical, HVAC, maintenance, logistics management—or anyone with a strong interest in learning and growing in a hands-on technical environment. Each pathway offers a unique experience, all focused on unlocking new opportunities for your future. Discover how your skills can lead to a meaningful career in datacenter operations and join us in powering the world forward.

# Microsoft HR Rotation Program (HR2P)

![Two employees working on a laptop.](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/hrrotation/02_HRrotation_MediaText.png)

- ### Empowering our exceptional university HR talent to achieve more.




HR2P (formerly known as HR Trax) has existed for over 20 years at Microsoft. It starts with a 12-week internship experience followed by an opportunity to return for our 3-year rotation program. The experience will foster learning and growth in the HR profession and help you jumpstart your career in HR.


- ### Explore HR careers at Microsoft




Our 3-year HR Rotation Program, known as HR2P, provides you the opportunity to grow your human resource skills in all aspects of HR and build community with your cohort. Through this program, you will build your HR capabilities, expand your skill-set, sharpen your business acumen while allowing you to explore HR careers at Microsoft.

# Hiring Process FAQs

## Your success starts with clarity

We’re glad you’re exploring opportunities with us! This FAQ section is built to give you straightforward, helpful information about every stage of your candidate experience. Because when you have clarity, you’re empowered to succeed. We are committed to making your experience transparent and supportive – so you can focus on what matters most: thriving in your career journey.

- Getting Started

- Search & Apply

- Profile & AI

- Processes & Accommodations

- Feedback & Support

- New Recruiting System


![](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/system-icons-do-not-delete-/Vector.png)

Getting StartedSearch & ApplyProfile & AIProcesses & AccommodationsFeedback & Support New Recruiting System

- ### What is required to create a profile on Microsoft Careers Site?











You’ll need either your preferred personal email address or your LinkedIn login details. You’ll also be asked to answer a few questions about your preferences. Finally, you can upload your resume—which will be parsed to create your profile (and can be updated anytime)—or manually enter your basic contact details.

- ### How do I create a Microsoft Careers site profile and what does it allow me to do?











Click Sign in, select Create a new account, and follow the prompts. Creating a profile allows you to:







  - View personalized job recommendations based on your skills and experience

  - Set up job alerts

  - Save jobs

  - Apply for open jobs

  - Complete tasks related to your recruitment activities if you advance in the process

- ### How is parsing used when I upload my resume?











When you upload your resume, the system uses parsing to extract details such as name and contact information, skills, work experience, education, and certifications. These elements are automatically populated into your profile to save time. It is critical that you review and confirm the information extracted from your profile to ensure that it accurately reflects your information, skills, and experience.

- ### What can I expect when I join the Microsoft Talent Network?











Joining the Microsoft Talent Network is a quick way to stay connected and receive job alerts without committing to a full profile. Here’s what you can expect:







  - **Receive job alerts:** Receive email notifications about roles that match your skills and experience.

  - **Profile visibility to recruiters:** Your profile and resume can be viewed by Microsoft recruiters for relevant roles—even before you apply.

  - **Stay connected:** Receive emails from Microsoft Recruiting, including updates about events, news and career opportunities.

- ### How do I search and apply for open jobs through the Microsoft Careers site?











You can search for jobs with or without creating a profile by navigating to the Search page and entering a keyword, location, job ID, or using filters to narrow results.







Creating a profile on the Microsoft Careers site unlocks richer personalization, editing options, and deeper engagement with recruiters.







To apply for a job, you’ll need to create a profile if you haven’t already, confirm you meet the required qualifications, complete all required questions, and submit your application.

- ### How frequently are job recommendations updated?











Job recommendations are refreshed regularly as new roles are posted and when you update your profile. The platform continuously reviews your information and available jobs to keep recommendations current and relevant.

- ### If I was referred for a job by a Microsoft employee, where do I see that information and what do I need to do?











Sign in to your profile and navigate to Action Center > Applications > Referrals. There, you’ll see de

# Transparency FAQ

- Platform Overview

- Join the Talent Network

- Career Site Profile

- Job Search and Recommendations

![](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/system-icons-do-not-delete-/Vector.png)

Platform OverviewJoin the Talent NetworkCareer Site ProfileJob Search and RecommendationsResponsible AI and ComplianceData Privacy & SecurityTroubleshooting and Support

- ### What is the Microsoft Careers Site?











The Microsoft Careers Site is Microsoft’s AI-powered job marketplace where people who are interested in working at Microsoft can find jobs that match their skills and experience and learn more about Microsoft’s culture.

- ### What can the Microsoft Careers Site do?











The Microsoft Career Site has a range of functions that make it easier to align the most qualified applicants for available opportunities. To take full advantage of the Microsoft Career Site, submit your resume to your created profile (however there is no obligation to do this).







You can click on “Join talent network” at the top of the page or “Sign in” to submit your resume. Using these options creates a profile so that recruiters at Microsoft can see your information and reach out to you about company updates, open positions, and recruiting events.







Creating a profile will also activate AI job recommendations so that you can see which open positions are a “good” or “strong” match for your skills and experience. Recruiters can also see your highlighted skills and attributes relevant to open positions and may reach out to you and invite you to apply. A strong or good recommendation does not guarantee or imply any specific outcome for a candidate's application to any role.







The best way to create a profile is by using the “Sign In” option to set up a login so that you can view and edit your profile information, create job alerts, and update your settings. Creating a login also allows you to input your location, so job recommendations will be filtered to show jobs near you.

- ### Do I need to upload my resume/CV?











If you go to the Careers page without uploading your resume, you can search for jobs by keyword and location, or you can use the filters at the top of the page to narrow down the list by things like job title or profession. If you don’t submit a resume, the AI features will be limited to using your IP address to infer your location so that jobs nearby will be presented to you first, and using your search terms and clicks to show you jobs that match your search patterns.

- ### What are the AI features in the Microsoft Careers Site?











**Resume parsing:** When you upload your resume, an AI parser extracts your contact information, education, and work experience and populates it into your talent network profile, saving you time. Be sure to always check the information extracted to your profile to ensure that it accurately reflects your information, skills, and experience.







**Skill inference:** Based on skills you mention in your resume, your work experience, and your education, the AI tool will extract and infer the skills you have and add them to your profile. These extracted \[and inferred skills\] are visible to you when you log into your profile, and you are encouraged to edit those skills if you feel they do not reflect your skills and experience.







**Job recommendations:** The AI tool will compare the skills, experience, and education from your profile with requirements for open positions and show you jobs that are a “good” or “strong” match for your profile. A strong or good recommendation does not guarantee or imply any specific outcome for a candidate's application to any role. You can o

# How we hire

![Employees talking](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/fy26/How%20we%20Hire_Guided%20by%20Our%20Values.jpg)

- ### Guided by our values, focused on your future




The way we hire reflects who we are, and that means creating an open and transparent hiring experience. Along your journey, we’ll guide you through the process, how to prepare, and when to expect your next update.



Our hiring is built to:



  - Create consistency. Structured interviews and consistent framework help us evaluate real skills against the role’s requirements.

  - Live our values. We look for respect, integrity, accountability, and growth mindset. It’s how we work with each other and our customers.

  - Prepare you for success. Access tips and resources to help you shine in interviews.

  - Value your time. We aim for clear timelines, few surprises, and support when you need it.

  - Evaluate skills and shared principles. We consider how you’ll learn, collaborate, and contribute to impact across teams.

  - Support accessibility. We provide accommodations and accessibility support throughout the recruitment process. [Learn more or request support](https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/accessibility.html "Learn more or request support opens in same tab").

  - Prepare you to excel. After you are hired, we guide your onboarding and equip you with tools to thrive in your new role.

## The recruiting lifecycle at Microsoft

- ### Explore open jobs











Searching for your next role at Microsoft should feel simple and empowering. Here’s how you can make the most of our tools:



  - **Get personalized recommendations** enabled by AI, based on your skills and experiences.

  - **Stay informed** by creating job alerts once you’ve set up your Microsoft Careers profile—so you never miss an opportunity.

  - **Search** by keywords, location, or job ID—and apply filters to narrow down results. Save jobs for later if you’re not ready to apply today.

**Personalize your job search with AI:**

Use one of these two methods:

**Option 1:  Search**

For single session job searches, you may upload your resume on the **Search** page to see personalized job recommendations based on your skills and experience. Your resume details will be purged from the system within 24 hours.

Note: If you return to the site after 24 hours, you’ll need to re-upload your resume. To apply for a job, you’ll need to create a profile.

**Option 2: Create a profile**

Click “Sign in”, navigate to “Create a new account” and follow the steps provided. By creating a profile, you can:

  - View personalized job recommendations

  - Set up job alerts

  - Apply for open roles

You can unlock richer personalization by registering for a Microsoft Career site login, which will give you access to enhanced profile editing options and deeper engagement opportunities with our recruiters

**How AI recommendations work**

When you upload your resume or create a profile, we use **AI-powered job recommendations** to show you jobs that are a “good” or “strong” match based on your skills and experience. You’ll see a **match indicator** on job postings. This shows how closely your skills, title, and work experience align with the role. It’s meant to guide you, not decide for you. Use it as a reference point but trust your judgement when choosing the right job to apply for. And remember, this indicator doesn’t guarantee any outcome for your application. These recommendations help identify roles that you might be interested in, so you can spend less time searching and more time finding opportunities that fit.

For additional information refer to the Microsoft Careers Site [Transparency FAQs](https://careers.microsoft.com/

# Legal policies

## Diversity and inclusion at Microsoft

Microsoft believes that a diverse and inclusive workplace leads to better products and services for our customers. As our workforce evolves to reflect the growing diversity of our communities and marketplace, the ability to leverage different perspectives for innovation and problem solving becomes increasingly important. Diversity and inclusion are foundational to a One Microsoft culture, fueling our business growth by allowing us to attract, retain, and develop the best talent from around the world. We address diversity and inclusion through clear, measurable strategies aligned to the priorities of the Microsoft CEO and senior leadership team.

https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/diversityandinclusion

Learn more about diversity and inclusion at Microsoft

[Learn more about diversity and inclusion at Microsoft](https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/diversityandinclusion)

## Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) policy

Microsoft maintains a work environment free from discrimination, one where employees are treated with dignity and respect. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling Microsoft’s commitment to equal employment opportunity. Microsoft does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by and consistent with applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We adhere to these principles in all aspects of employment, including recruitment, hiring, training, compensation, promotion, benefits, social and recreational programs, and discipline. In addition, it is the policy of Microsoft to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances where a particular employee works. For more information about reasonable accommodation, [visit the Accessibility page](https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/accessibility "Accessibility page opens in the same tab").

[View the US equal employment opportunity poster](https://www.eeoc.gov/sites/default/files/2022-10/22-088_EEOC_KnowYourRights_10_20.pdf "US equal employment opportunity poster opens in a new tab")

# Early in profession hiring tips

![Photo of students talking](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/urupdates/StudentInerview-RecentGrad.png)

- Through our interview process, we aim to discover your base knowledge in the area of focus, evaluate critical thinking skills, assess how you solve problems and your ability to learn. You'll have an initial interview and, if selected, a second final interview. We aim to discover your strengths to pair you with the right team.







Our interview process varies depending on the role you're applying for. For some of our openings you may be asked to write code, share a creative portfolio or project, or provide examples of your work in other ways.  Interview preparation and details are communicated to candidates when they are invited to interviews to help set you up for success.


- **Virtual interviews**



All our interviews are virtual. To help you prepare, please [see tips for virtual interviewing](https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/hiring-tips/virtual-interviewing "Virtual interviewing page opens in the same tab").



You can also find further recommendations for virtual interviewing from Kelsea, University Recruiting Account Manager and Chase, Programming Engineer when you [watch this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fRozLeshPiA "Virtual interview tips video opens in a new tab").







**Virtual Interview Day**



  - 1:1 meetings with Microsoft employees
  - A break is incorporated between interviews
  - Prepare for multiple types of questions, both behavioral and functional/technical, focused on your experience and skills that could translate to the role for which you are interviewing
  - Your recruiter can help you understand the specific timeline of when you can expect to hear back regarding the hiring decision.

![](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/05-LWT-Carousel1of4.png)

You can also find further recommendations for virtual interviewing from Kelsea, University Recruiting Account Manager and Chase, Programming Engineer when you [watch this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fRozLeshPiA "Virtual interview tips video opens in a new tab").

  - A break is incorporated between interviews
  - Prepare for multiple types of questions, both behavioral and functional/technical, focused on your experience and skills that could translate to the role for which you are interviewing

Watch the videos below to help you prepare for your interview. Many of our roles will have different interview objectives, so we have created role specific videos to help you understand the process and prepare better.

# Interview tips for all roles

If advancing in the process, the next interview steps vary based on the role you're applying for. Be prepared to demonstrate how you meet the qualifications of the job by sharing specific examples from your past or ideas about how you would accomplish a specific task. Be sure to share how skills you have gained throughout your career will translate to the role for which you are interviewing. For some of our openings you may be asked to write code, share a creative portfolio, or provide examples of your work in other ways. You’ll meet with a few people—some potential teammates and some cross-functional colleagues—for up to an hour each. The hiring team gets a chance to get to know you—and you get a chance to get to know the team. Post-interview, your recruiter can help you understand the specific timeline of when you can expect to hear back regarding the hiring decision.

Our interview process is currently entirely virtual. For more specific guidance, you can read [how to prepare for a virtual interview.](https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/hiring-tips/virtual-interviewing)

## Before your interview

- ### Do your research











Don't stop at understanding the position you’re interviewing for. You should understand what's happening in the tech industry, what Microsoft is doing well, and how we can make ourselves stand out from the competition. Make sure to prepare questions to ask during your interview. This is also a chance for you to make sure that the opportunity is what you are looking for.

- ### Know our competencies











The interviews will include competency-based questions, as well as questions about your resume. Keep the core competencies in mind. Think of examples you can share or incorporate into your conversation. These are our core competencies:





  - **Collaboration**. Communicating effectively within the team and across teams.

  - **Drive for results**. Working tenaciously to deliver on commitments, constantly seeking bigger challenges, holding yourself and others accountable.

  - **Customer focus**. Our mission at Microsoft is to empower every person and every organization to achieve more.


  - **Influencing for impact**. Successfully persuading and influencing others with effective communication.

  - **Judgment**. Effectively scoping complex problems and using business acumen to make knowledge-based decisions.

  - **Adaptability**. Ability to deal with ambiguous and uncertain situations or problems with agility.


- ### Accelerate our culture











At Microsoft, we believe that living our culture is key to achieving our mission. Selecting people who have the right skills and mindset is important, not only for the company we are today, but also for the company we aspire to be.




Our growth-mindset culture is designed to drive innovation and collaboration. Being a learn-it-all instead of a know-it-all fosters curiosity and prompts us to be inclusive and seek new and diverse perspectives.




For our interview process, we focus on these key aspects of our culture:





  - **Growth mindset**. True learners who can acquire new skills rapidly and connect with others to help bring big ideas to life.

  - **Diverse and inclusive**. Being open to learning about your own biases and changing behaviors accordingly. Value differences, seek them out, and invite them in.

  - **One Microsoft**. Actively seeks collaboration with others and looks for common ground across differences, contributes to and builds on success of others, and prioritizes for customer and team within their role.


  - **Customer obsession**. The ability to learn about customers' perspectives, show empathy to their experiences, and incorporate these insights into their decisions and deliverables.

  - **Values*

# Cloud Supply Chain Rotational Program

![Photo of intern working on laptop at home](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/lpupdates/Score_S1_MSC24-Canada-SMB-Getty-1341217663-rgb.jpg)

- ### SCORE (Supply Chain Operations Rotation Experience) is the rotation program for university hires entering Microsoft’s Cloud Supply Chain organization.




SCORE is a three-year career experience designed to provide emerging talent with an unparalleled journey through end-to-end cloud supply chain operations. Over three one-year rotations, participants will partner with and learn from our teams while building skills and supply chain acumen to accelerate their career growth. During this experience, participants can expect:



  - Meaningful roles in supply chain operations, planning and other critical parts of the business
  - A robust support network of leaders, managers, mentors and dedicated SCORE Program Managers
  - Targeted learning and developmental opportunities throughout the three-year journey including Lean Six Sigma belt certifications
  - Networking opportunities, volunteering, and community engagement
  - End-to-end cloud supply chain operations visibility and dedicated time with leadership

The SCORE program is also offered in our India Center of Excellence.

- ### Microsoft Cloud Supply Chain Internship




The US Cloud Supply Chain Internship is a 12-week program exclusively for students enrolled full-time at a college or university. As a Supply Chain Planner intern, you'll become part of a team within the Cloud Supply Chain organization, gaining insight into a future career at Microsoft. Throughout the program, interns can look forward to:



  - Opportunities across all Supply Chain operations groups
  - Attending LEAN training and receiving your White Belt certification
  - Dedicated time with leadership
  - An annual Intern Project Fair to showcase your work
  - Training sessions and networking opportunities
  - A primary pathway into the full-time SCORE rotation program

The Cloud Supply Chain Internship is also offered in our India Center of Excellence for an 8-week experience and in Taiwan for a 12-week experience.

![Photo of employees working togehter ](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/lpupdates/SCORE_MSC24-ASEAN-meeting-Getty-1138451175-rgb.jpg)

- ### Microsoft Cloud Supply Chain Internship




The US Cloud Supply Chain Internship is a 12-week program exclusively for students enrolled full-time at a college or university. As a Supply Chain Planner intern, you'll become part of a team within the Cloud Supply Chain organization, gaining insight into a future career at Microsoft. Throughout the program, interns can look forward to:



  - Opportunities across all Supply Chain operations groups
  - Attending LEAN training and receiving your White Belt certification
  - Dedicated time with leadership
  - An annual Intern Project Fair to showcase your work
  - Training sessions and networking opportunities
  - A primary pathway into the full-time SCORE rotation program

The Cloud Supply Chain Internship is also offered in our India Center of Excellence for an 8-week experience and in Taiwan for a 12-week experience.

![](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/media-text--3-2-/lpupdates/Score_%20Vert%20tab2_SUR25-Pro-11thEdition-Photo-1600-RGB.jpg)

- ### Who should apply?




We are looking for bachelor’s students and new grads who resonate with Microsoft’s mission and are excited and passionate about a career in Supply Chain. Here are some preferred skills and qualifications for both the internship and full-time program:



  - One or more internship experiences in a supply chain-related role
  - Familiarity

- ### Get inside the Microsoft intern experience




Every year, thousands of university students from every corner of the world join Microsoft. You bring your aspirations, talent, potential—and excitement for the journey ahead. Our internship experience brings interns from around the world together to connect, collaborate and co-create virtually to make a difference and impact the world.


## Internship Programs

- ![Photo of intern working](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/students/EIP_Discovery%20Program%20clickable%20cards%20GCS%20FY25-02.png)





### Discovery Program





The Discovery Program is a unique opportunity for students who are passionate about technology and want to make a positive impact on the world and society. This four-week internship is open to rising first-year students college students, based in Redmond, Washington or Atlanta, Georgia. Participants will work on a real project, learn from Microsoft employees and mentors, and explore different career paths in tech.

- ![Photo of Microsoft interns talking](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/students/EIP_Explore%20program%20clickable%20cards%20GCS%20FY25-01.png)





### Explore Program





Created for first- and second-year college students, Explore Microsoft is a 12-week summer internship that lets you try out different software engineering roles.

- ![Photo of Microsoft interns](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/students/EIP_phdmbajd%20clickable%20cards%20GCS%20FY25-03.png)





### PhD, MBA, JD





If you're pursuing a PhD, MBA, or JD and want to explore what it's like to work at Microsoft, look no further. We host PhD, MBA, and JD interns throughout our teams. Our PhD, MBA, and JD interns are active, contributing members to our community. No matter what route you take, come work on meaningful, innovative proj

# How to prepare for a virtual interview

- ### Prior to your virtual interview











**If required, sign the Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)**. Certain teams require you to sign an NDA prior to your interviews. If you have been asked to do this, please review the document in your Action Center and sign to acknowledge that you agree to the terms. If you have not been asked to do this, there is no action for you.



**Set up the teleconferencing technology**. All interviews are held via Microsoft Teams and/or in the case of some technical interviews, via a different 3rd party virtual platform. Your recruiting contact will inform you of the platform(s) used.



**If your interview is confirmed to be on Microsoft Teams:**



  - In preparation for your upcoming virtual Teams interview, [download the Teams App](https://teams.microsoft.com/dl/launcher/launcher.html?url=%2f_%23%2fl%2fmeetup-join%2f19%3ameeting_Mjc0NmUwYzQtZWUxZS00OTE1LWJlZWEtMDBlYzBhZTMzNTc3%40thread.v2%2f0%3fcontext%3d%257b%2522Tid%2522%253a%252272f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47%2522%252c%2522Oid%2522%253a%252294cf4c1e-5216-4a94-8207-16a5420f2ea2%2522%257d%26anon%3dtrue&type=meetup-join&deeplinkId=1d08ec18-6c7b-48f8-a86c-f2c39f28e1c6&directDl=true&msLaunch=true&enableMobilePage=true) to your computer. If you are unable to install Teams, there is an option to open Teams in your web browser; however, we recommend using the App where possible as this ensures that you have access to all the features. Please note the preferred browsers are Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome.
  - Test that the Teams App is working. You can join, leave, and re-join a Teams Meeting anytime.
  - Your recruiting contact will send a Microsoft Teams meeting ID# prior to your interview.

**If your interview is confirmed to be on a different 3rd party platform:**

  - You will be sent an email from your recruiting contact with specific instructions to install and test the platform prior to your interview.
  - As a back-up, we recommend [downloading the Teams App](https://teams.microsoft.com/dl/launcher/launcher.html?url=%2f_%23%2fl%2fmeetup-join%2f19%3ameeting_Mjc0NmUwYzQtZWUxZS00OTE1LWJlZWEtMDBlYzBhZTMzNTc3%40thread.v2%2f0%3fcontext%3d%257b%2522Tid%2522%253a%252272f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47%2522%252c%2522Oid%2522%253a%252294cf4c1e-5216-4a94-8207-16a5420f2ea2%2522%257d%26anon%3dtrue&type=meetup-join&deeplinkId=1d08ec18-6c7b-48f8-a86c-f2c39f28e1c6&directDl=true&msLaunch=true&enableMobilePage=true) as well.

**Check the rest of your technology setup.** Confirm that your webcam and microphone are allowed and work properly in your chosen browser. Test your internet connection to ensure it is strong and working. If using headphones, make sure they are connected and selected prior.

**If needed, request accommodations**. If you need any accommodations or are concerned about Microsoft Teams or other third-party virtual platforms not being fully accessible for you, please [submit an accessibility request](https://apply.careers.microsoft.com/careers/forms/create?templateId=yx2LlRBJ7wq&fullPageResponse=1&hl=en-US "Support request form opens in a side panel"). We will reach out to discuss how to best support you.

**Prepare for your virtual interview as for any other.** [Explore our interview tips page](https://careers.microsoft.com/v2/global/en/hiring-tips "Interview tips page opens in the same tab") to help you get ready.

- ### On the day of your interview











  - Conduct your interview in a well-lit and quiet environment that is as free of any distractions as possible. With many of us having to work from home while taking care of our families, we understand that quiet space can be challenging to find, for you as well as for the interviewers. While we encourage you to find a space where you can focus and n

# India

At Microsoft India you can find exciting careers ranging from research and global delivery to technical support, cloud services and solutions, product development and everything else in-between. Our teams are enabling organizations across India to accelerate digital transformation. We're present across five locations: Bengaluru, Gurugram, Hyderabad, Mumbai, and Noida. Come and team up with the people who are empowering others to achieve more.

## Life at Microsoft India

Be creative. Be inspired. Be valued. Be here.


- ![Photo of India](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/india/India-1.jpg)





### Microsoft empowers its employees with the flexibility to optimize work-life balance





At Microsoft, we’re constantly finding ways to accommodate different work styles as the path to helping everyone do their best work. For this, it’s important to blend work and life at levels that suit your needs. To experience benefits that enable you to do more with time.

- ![Photo of group of Microsoft employees](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/india/India-2.jpg)





### A hybrid work strategy by Microsoft for the new era of flexible work





Hybrid work requires a new operating model and strategy that encompasses flexible work policy, inclusive space design and innovative technology solutions. The modern workplace needs to meet employee expectations, connect a more distributed workforce, and provide tools to create, innovate and work together to solve business problems.

- ![Photo of Microsoft employee](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/india/India-3.jpg)





### How communities at Microsoft enable employees to be more





Microsoft offers close to 40 community groups across the company for career development, support, networking opportunities, mentoring, community participation, product input, and assistance in activities that prove cultural awareness. You can connect with others, exchange information on common interests and hobbies and make the most of your experience here.

- ![Photo of Microsoft employee ](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/india/India-4.jpg)





### Wellbeing means physical, emotional, and financial support at Microsoft





Microsoft offers close to 50 community groups across the company for career development, support, networking opportunities, mentoring, community participation, product input, and assistance in activities that promote cultural awareness. You can connect with other, exchange information on common interests and hobbies and make the most of your experience here.

- ![Photo of Microsoft employees](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/india/India-5.jpg)





### How Microsoft's approach to diversity and inclusion is driving change





Every day, we at Microsoft strive to create an inclusive environment that brings the power of diversity to life. We expect each employee to play an active role in creating environments where people of diverse backgrounds are excited to bring all of who they are and do their best work.

- ![Photo of Microsoft employee](https://careers.microsoft.com/content/dam/microsoftcareersvnext/en/images/clickablecards-3-1-/india/India-6.jpg)





### How Microsoft Springboard enables women to reboot their careers after a break





It can be challenging for women to make re-entry into the tech industry after taking a career break. Microsoft's flagship program, Springboard, is a platform that makes w
