Johnson & Johnson is hiring for a **Sr. Supplier Quality Engineer – Shockwave Medica** l to join our team located in **Santa Clara, CA.**

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe health is everything. Our strength in healthcare innovation empowers us to build a world where complex diseases are prevented, treated, and cured, where treatments are smarter and less invasive, and solutions are personal. Through our expertise in Innovative Medicine and MedTech, we are uniquely positioned to innovate across the full spectrum of healthcare solutions today to deliver the breakthroughs of tomorrow, and profoundly impact health for humanity. Learn more at [https://www.jnj.com/.](https://www.jnj.com/)

Fueled by innovation at the intersection of biology and technology, we’re developing the next generation of smarter, less invasive, more personalized treatments. Ready to join a team that’s pioneering the development and commercialization of Intravascular Lithotripsy (IVL) to treat complex calcified cardiovascular disease. Our Shockwave Medical portfolio aims to establish a new standard of care for medical device treatment of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease through its differentiated and proprietary local delivery of sonic pressure waves for the treatment of calcified plaque.

**Position Overview**

The Sr. Supplier Quality Engineer performs work with general supervision to provide and implement solutions to broad problems and accomplishes complex assignments. The scope of responsibilities includes supporting manufacturing operations, managing suppliers, and working with cross-functional groups to address quality issues. The Sr. Supplier Quality Engineer participates in decisions related to scope of work, risk management, process improvements, material disposition, and the implementation of revised product specifications.

**Essential Job Functions**

_Supplier Management_

- Partner with Procurement, R&D, Operations, Quality, and Suppliers in the timely investigation, resolution, and prevention of supplier quality issues which could disrupt business operations
- Monitor and analyze supplier performance metrics, identify improvement opportunities, and communicate supplier risks and/or opportunities to the next management level
- Plan, schedule, execute, report, and follow-up on supplier audits
- Initiate, issue, review, and approve Supplier Corrective Action Reports (SCARs)
- Drive corrective actions for supplier processes by driving suppliers to true root cause analysis and corrective and preventive actions
- Own, manage, and drive to completion Supplier Change Assessments (SCAs)
- Participate in component qualification change projects in collaboration with key business partners and the supplier’s applicable functional groups
- Compile, analyze, and summarize supplier performance data for periodic management reviews

_Manufacturing Support_

- Review, investigate, disposition, and drive to completion Non-conforming Reports (NCRs) and Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPAs)
- Initiate, review, and approve Document Change Orders (DCOs)
- Provide technical support and guidance to the Quality Assurance group
- Support internal and third-party audits (FDA, Notified Body)
- Participate in Material Review Board (MRB) meetings

_Manufacturing Line Transfers_

- Partner with cross-functional teams to define requirements and generate documentation for the transfer of manufacturing lines to Contract Manufacturers (CMs)
- Plan and provide on-site and off-site training to CMs on product inspection and testing
- Act as the primary Quality point of contact with CMs, and provide remote and off-site support o

title: Ruby’s fight to change how we think about mental health | Johnson & Johnson Careers
raw_text:
[Skip to main content](https://www.careers.jnj.com/en/employee-stories/culture/ruby-s-fight-to-change-how-we-think-about-mental-health/#content)

![Screenshot 2025 03 04 At 12.45.26 PM](https://www.careers.jnj.com/media/bm1nwj4g/screenshot-2025-03-04-at-124526-pm.png?width=1600&height=900&v=1db8cfaf2d1a5e0&rmode=crop&quality=75)

[Culture](https://www.careers.jnj.com/en/employee-stories/culture/) March 26, 2021

# Ruby’s fight to change how we think about mental health

Share

- [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3a%2f%2fwww.careers.jnj.com%2fen%2femployee-stories%2fculture%2fruby-s-fight-to-change-how-we-think-about-mental-health%2f)
- [Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.careers.jnj.com%2fen%2femployee-stories%2fculture%2fruby-s-fight-to-change-how-we-think-about-mental-health%2f&text=Ruby%E2%80%99s%20fight%20to%20change%20how%20we%20think%20about%20mental%20health&via=JNJNews)
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3a%2f%2fwww.careers.jnj.com%2fen%2femployee-stories%2fculture%2fruby-s-fight-to-change-how-we-think-about-mental-health%2f)

President of The American Society of Hispanic Psychiatry. Immigrant and innovator. When Ruby says, “I’ve dedicated my life to elevating the conversation about mental health,” she means it. Follow her path to Johnson & Johnson to find out how she’s translating her lifelong passion into meaningful action today.

### “Why psychiatry?”

Once upon a time, that was a question Ruby got asked a lot, primarily by family members. Five of Ruby’s older siblings are doctors. None of them are psychiatrists.

“They would always ask me, ‘Why, Ruby, why are you going into this field?’ And the answer is complicated, but it has to do with the fact that these patients need a lot of help, and that it’s about much more than just medicine. It’s about support, compassion, community, culture, knowledge—all of these things and on so many different levels.”

In the medical community as in society at large, Ruby has long noted and been frustrated by the lack of transparency and dialogue around mental health issues. “It’s comparatively easy to talk about the heart or the lungs or the skin,” she pointed out. “But the brain is an organ, too! It’s a part of the body. So why aren’t we talking about schizophrenia or bipolar disorder in the same way or with the same frequency?”

Part of the answer, according to Ruby, may have to do with the fact that there are as yet no known “biomarkers” in psychiatry—that is, measurable substances whose presence in the body could be used to indicate a mental health condition like depression.

That’s actually something Ruby and others are working on right now. But in the short term, she’s leading the way with some pretty remarkable research involving cutting-edge applications of AI.

“We used all of these AI tools to analyze more than one million digital conversations that touched on mental health, looking at the differences between, for example, Hispanic versus non-Hispanic conversations, among a lot of other things,” Ruby recounted.

“And one of the things really that stood out to me is that Hispanics generally don’t talk about depression as a disease. We talk about it like it’s something that’s a part of us, part of our lives—you know, ‘I must be going through postmenopausal depression’ or ‘I guess this is adolescence.’ Like it’s a part of us. And I think a lot of that ties back to trauma. A lot of Hispanic people have been through traumatic events in their own lives, or else they’ve inherited stories and memories of trauma from their parents.”

Whatever findings the research ultimately yields, it’s an interesting new approach and one that Ruby describes as “cultural psychiatry.” It has also driven home at least one takeaway for her already: “We definitely must continue to embrace new technology-driven approaches in the field of psychiatry.”

### Creating a new canon, paying tribute to the old

Coping with the direct and indirect costs of COVID-19 has been immensely challenging, but Ruby also warns we might still be underestimating the long-term consequences of the global pandemic—at least in one crucial respect.

“I truly believe what we’re going through right now is only the first pandemic,” she explained. “The second pandemic is going to be psychological. This experience has been very traumatic for many people, and that isn’t being fully acknowledged or addressed.”

For her part, she’s certainly staying busy. During COVID-19, notably, she completed (and recently published!) her first non-academic book: “Quijotes de la Psiquiatria,” a compendium of anecdotes and stories drawing on the lives of more than 100 Hispanic psychiatrists.

“The reality is that these remarkable psychiatrists played an important role in shaping the development of my field,” she said. Appropriately, then, Ruby’s title, while alluding to Miguel de Cervantes’s famous hidalgo, recasts his journey in an interesting new light, as well.

Ruby explained: “Looking at all of these amazing psychiatrists, I decided that they’ve all been a little bit like Quixotes. They were chasing these professional dreams that probably seemed crazy to the people around them at the time.” Of course, what made those aspirations “crazy” was nothing more than a fantasy reinforced by society: that things like language, ethnicity and skin color were destiny. And quite unlike the exploits of the fictional Quixote, however, the chronicles of flesh-and-blood pioneers in psychiatry like George I. Sanchez, Martha Bernal and others are not widely known or celebrated. So Ruby’s collection is a welcome entry.

Her book pays homage to Cervantes’ in another sense, as well: She wrote it in Spanish. (Thanks to the success of the first printing, a second edition, including an English-language translation, is forthcoming.)

“I truly never dreamed that I would write a book in my lifetime,” she mused. “I’ve written many articles for ac

Terakhir Diperbarui: \[27 Agustus 2024\]

Johnson & Johnson menghargai privasi Anda serta berkomitmen terhadap pengelolaan, penggunaan, dan perlindungan informasi pribadi yang bertanggung jawab. Kebijakan Privasi Karier ini (“Kebijakan”) menjelaskan praktik kami sehubungan dengan informasi mengenai Anda yang dikumpulkan secara online atau offline oleh Johnson & Johnson Services, Inc., yang berlokasi di Amerika Serikat, atau afiliasi lain dari Johnson & Johnson Family of Companies (secara bersama-sama disebut dalam Kebijakan ini sebagai “Johnson & Johnson” atau “kami”), sehubungan dengan lamaran pekerjaan Anda dan peluang karier mendatang di Johnson & Johnson. Semua laman situs web terkait tempat Anda mengakses Kebijakan ini secara bersama-sama disebut dalam Kebijakan ini sebagai “Situs Karier”.

**INFORMASI PRIBADI YANG KAMI KUMPULKAN**

**Informasi yang Anda Berikan**

Kami mengumpulkan informasi pribadi dari Anda sehubungan dengan lamaran pekerjaan Anda, komunikasi, dan peluang karier mendatang di Johnson & Johnson.

Informasi yang kami kumpulkan meliputi:

- **Informasi Kontak:** nama, alamat, nomor telepon, alamat email, dan informasi kontak lainnya
- **Materi Lamaran:** CV, résumé, dan surat pengantar
- **Pengalaman:** pekerjaan sebelumnya, pengalaman praktis, dan pengalaman terkait lainnya
- **Pendidikan:** pendidikan, termasuk tingkat pendidikan, jenis, mata kuliah, gelar, ijazah dan sertifikat, serta institusi
- **Jabatan yang Diinginkan:** jabatan, peran, dan peluang yang diinginkan, dan jika ada, jabatan yang ditawarkan
- **Keterampilan:** pengetahuan, keterampilan, bahasa, dan kompetensi lainnya
- **Sertifikasi:** lisensi, izin, dan sertifikasi profesional dan lainnya yang berkaitan dengan pekerjaan yang dimiliki
- **Perincian Referensi:** informasi yang Anda berikan terkait dengan referensi karakter dan pekerjaan
- **Informasi Akun Online:** nama pengguna dan kata sandi untuk mengakses Situs Karier, pengidentifikasi lamaran, alamat protokol internet (IP), dan pengidentifikasi perangkat yang dapat dikumpulkan secara otomatis
- **Preferensi Komunikasi:** metode dan bahasa komunikasi pilihan
- **Informasi Acara:** pembatasan terkait makanan, detail perjalanan dan akomodasi, identifikasi yang diterbitkan pemerintah untuk mengakses salah satu lokasi fisik kami, dan detail lain yang khusus untuk wawancara, pertemuan, atau acara perekrutan tertentu
- **Izin Kerja:** Status izin kerja dan visa terkait atau informasi imigrasi lainnya
- **Informasi Pemeriksaan Konflik:** Informasi mengenai hubungan pribadi dan keanggotaan dewan eksternal untuk perusahaan non-J&J, penunjukan, peran, dan informasi lainnya yang mungkin menimbulkan kemungkinan konflik kepentingan dengan Johnson & Johnson, hanya jika diminta, terkait dengan kandidat terpilih
- **Informasi Lainnya:** Setiap informasi lainnya yang Anda pilih untuk diberikan kepada kami (misalnya, preferensi ketenagakerjaan, kesediaan untuk melakukan relokasi, gaji saat ini, gaji yang diinginkan, penghargaan, atau keanggotaan profesional)

Sebagaimana diizinkan oleh hukum yang berlaku, kami dapat mengajukan pertanyaan mengenai asal ras/etnis, gender, dan disabilitas (“ **Informasi Keragaman**”), untuk pemantauan kesetaraan peluang kerja. Kami juga dapat meminta keterangan tentang rekam jejak kriminal (“ **Rekam Jejak Kriminal**”)menyusul penawaran ketenagakerjaan bersyarat, jika diizinkan oleh hukum yang berlaku. Kecuali diminta secara khusus atau diwajibkan oleh hukum, kami meminta agar Anda menghindari mengirimkan informasi yang dapat dianggap sebagai informasi sensitif dalam lamaran Anda. Informasi sensitif termasuk data mengenai ras, agama, etnis, kewarganegaraan atau asal negara, usia, identitas gender, kehidupan atau praktik seksual maupun orientasi seksual, status pernikahan, informasi medis atau kesehatan (termasuk status disabilitas), informasi genetik atau biometrik, keyakinan politik atau filosofi, keanggotaan partai politik atau serikat buruh, status veteran, foto, informasi pemeriksaan latar belakang, data peradilan, seperti rekam jejak kriminal, a

title: Global Market Access Summer Intern Program | Johnson & Johnson Careers
raw_text:
[Skip to main content](https://www.careers.jnj.com/en/student-opportunities/internships/global-market-access-summer-intern-program/#content)

# Global Market Access Summer Intern Program

![MA Internship](https://www.careers.jnj.com/media/sesgyk0t/ma-internship.webp?width=1048&height=1048&v=1db889d1860c640&width=1048&height=1048&rmode=crop&quality=75)

## Accelerate your career and your impact

Market access professionals tackle pressing healthcare access challenges to deliver essential therapies and treatments to patients. By leading bold innovations and strategies here, we can ultimately impact patients’ lives for the better.

The Global Market Access Summer Intern Program occurs between May and August, running for 10 to 12 weeks. You’ll explore the process of bringing innovative therapies to life as you build your Market Access-related skills.

**The opportunity**

During this internship, you can:

- Shape strategies that deliver bold pharmaceutical innovations and medications to patients
- Gain real-world experience working on high visibility assignments across the product cycle from concept to commercialization
- Learn how we generate evidence to demonstrate the value of our pharmaceutical products to key stakeholders
- Ensure patients have access to our products and maintain treatment affordability
- Work on projects that focus on Health Economics, Pricing, Patient-Reported Outcomes, Market Access Analytics, or Health Policy
- Drive projects across one or more of the following therapeutic areas: Infectious Diseases and Vaccines, Cardiovascular, Metabolism and Pulmonary Hypertension, Retina, Neuroscience, Oncology, and Immunology

**Who we're looking for**

Market Access is a career path for talented minds that are deeply curious, smart and strategic.

Ideal applicants are:

- Currently pursuing an MBA, MA, MS, MPH, PharmD, Ph.D. in Economics, Epidemiology, Psychology, Public Health, Pharmacy, Health Outcomes Research, Health Services Research, or other market access related degree
- Self-motivated, team-focused individual with strong communication and analytical skills
- Driven to learn about market access, product development and evidence generation to demonstrate the value of our pharmaceutical products to key stakeholders

![Cover Market Access Summer Intern Page 1](https://www.careers.jnj.com/media/vsfdiuxg/cover-market-access-summer-intern-page-1.webp?width=1024&height=768&v=1dc6e7fb8e946f0&rmode=crop&quality=75)Play video

## Inside the program

Creating life-changing treatments and vaccines goes beyond the incredible work undertaken at Johnson & Johnson. It is the Market Access professionals who provide the vital link between R&D and commercial operations, shaping the economic, clinical and patient-reported evidence that will be used to provide eventual access to our innovative medicines. Our Market Access organization leverages this valuable evidence to meet the needs of key stakeholders like government payers and private insurance companies so that our treatments can be made available to patients across the globe.

# Associate Clinical Account Specialist (ACAS)

We want you to write your own success story, spark bold innovations and become an expert at what truly interests and inspires you. When you set out on your path with the power of the world’s largest and most diversified healthcare products company behind you, you’ll uncover opportunities to meaningfully impact the lives of people everywhere.

![ACAS Mast](https://www.careers.jnj.com/media/hcqd5ykv/acas-mast.jpg?width=1048&height=1048&v=1db4644ade8d7c0&width=1048&height=1048&rmode=crop&quality=75)

## The program

Our Associate Clinical Account Specialist (ACAS) role is a full-time, hands-on, high-impact opportunity with Biosense Webster. You'll gain valuable experience in clinical settings, act as the "eyes" of physicians during arrythmia ablation procedures and help patients with cardiac arrythmias live healthier, longer lives by delivering breakthrough MedTech innovation. Along the way, you'll also build trusting partnerships with physicians, electrophysiologists, electrophysiology lab teams and other stakeholders across the U.S. To excel in this role, you may be required to travel locally, regionally and nationally.

![ACAS 1](https://www.careers.jnj.com/media/eo5ddniw/acas-1.jpg?rxy=0.5136855791091739,0.22776889386984445&width=1024&height=768&v=1db4645761d96e0&rmode=crop&quality=75)

## The opportunity

**Opportunities available throughout the U.S. (all locations recruit locally)**

- Ongoing hiring throughout the year
- Six-to-nine-month training program
- Those who complete the training program are eligible for promotion into a Clinical Account Specialist (CAS) role

**Are you ready to make a difference?**

Biosense Webster, Inc. is the global leader in the science of diagnosing and treating heart rhythm disorders. The company established its leadership in electrophysiology with the development of the first real-time, 3D cardiac mapping and navigation technology, as well as the first electrophysiology catheter. The introduction of the company’s CARTO® 3 System in 2009 revolutionized 3D mapping technology by increasing the accuracy, speed and efficiency of the cardiac ablation procedure.

![JJ Photo Redwoodcity 2505](https://www.careers.jnj.com/media/w0gjlu5u/jj_photo_redwoodcity_2505.jpg?width=1024&height=768&v=1db4c8fd955ba30&rmode=crop&quality=75)

## Who we’re looking for

Our ideal candidates have strong technical skills, strategic mindsets and exceptional leadership, communication and interpersonal capabilities. They should be able to thrive in highly complex, global work environments and enjoy taking initiative, setting priorities and exceeding goals.

Program requirements (must meet at least one of the following):

- A minimum of a bachelor’s degree by December 2024
- A minimum of two years of related professional experience (engineering in a procedural hospital setting) with an associate degree
- A minimum of one year of electrophysiology mapping experience with an associate degree
- Have graduated from an accredited academic program or industry prep school with a focus on electrophysiology and a bachelor’s degree
- Exiting a branch of the U.S. military with an associate degree
- Priority given to candidates with bachelor’s or master’s degree in one of the following STEM fields: biology, biochemistry, cardiac function and interventional technology, engineering (biomedical, computer science, chemical, electrical, mechanical and general engineering), health sciences and kinesiology
- Ability and willingness to travel within the U.S. up to 40%, including overnights locally, regionally and nationally
- Legal authorization to work in the U.S. without requiring sponsorship for employment visa status now or in the future (we do not accept OPT/CPT)
- Geographic flexibility with potential for relocation
- A valid driver’s license and clean driving record

# Five Career Development Tips for Manufacturing Employees

Share


Whether you're just starting out or have years of manufacturing experience, career development in our manufacturing facilities is not only possible, it’s powerful. Here are some practical tips to help you grow your career, make an impact, and shape your future at work.

### **1\. You Don’t Need All the Skills Right Away**

Every expert started as a beginner. In production roles, many skills are learned on the job. What matters most is showing up ready to learn and take initiative.

### **2\. Find a Mentor**

Look for someone whose career path inspires you. Ask questions, observe how they work and learn from their experience. Mentors can help you navigate challenges and open doors to new opportunities.

### **3\. Talk to Your Manager About Your Goals**

Your manager can’t support your career aspirations if they don’t know what they are. Whether you’re interested in leadership, certifications, or trying something new, it is worth it to start the conversation.

### **4\. Share Your Ideas for Improvement**

You know your workspace better than anyone. If you see a way to improve safety, efficiency or morale—speak up. Your ideas can lead to real change and show your leadership potential.

### **5\. Try New Ways of Doing Things**

Innovation isn’t just for engineers. Whether it’s a better way to organize tools or streamline a process, small changes can make a big impact. Stay curious and open to new approaches.

Your career is in your hands. Start where you are, use what you have and build toward where you want to go. [Learn more.](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/life-at-caterpillar/)

# Sr. Parts Information Analyst

| Location | [Chennai](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Chennai), [Tamil Nādu](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Tamil%20N%C4%81du), [India](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?country=India) |
| Date Posted | Monday, February 2, 2026 |
| Apply By | Tuesday, February 10, 2026 |
| Contract Type | Full time |
| Job Type | Regular |
| Requisition ID | R0000346667 |

## Description

**Career Area:**

Product Support

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

The Technical Information Solutions (TIS) team within Product Support & Logistics Division (PSLD) creates and delivers technical parts and service information accessible via SIS and SIS2GO critical for the repair and service of Caterpillar products. Over 300,000 dealer and customer users depend on the information created, managed, and delivered by this team to identify and order Cat parts.

**Job Summary**

In TIS, the Parts Information Specialist Creates, validates, and revises technical parts information in support of New Product Introduction (NPI), Continuous Process Improvement, and Revision project timelines, managing projects to ensure parts manuals are completed on-time and accurately.

**What You Have:**

- Review day-to-day work completed by parts information staff for accuracy. Provide appropriate coaching to improve performance.

- Train and mentor new staff on how to create, validate, and revise technical parts information.

- Develop and maintain parts information standards.

- Participate in and lead process improvement efforts.

- Communicate the importance of customer needs/expectations to parts information creation team consistently.

**What You Will Get:**

Technical Excellence: Demonstrated knowledge of Caterpillar product structure, engineering, and parts information databases, including EDS, NPR, PRWB and Windchill (ACM) authoring system.

Meets or exceeds performance expectations for parts information production and quality. Develops and provides solutions to significant technical challenges. Helps others solve technical or procedural problems or issues.

Service Excellence: Provides effective responses to parts information requests and interactions with a sense of urgency and positive action. Acts on parts information improvement opportunities and supports the delivery of high-quality parts information to customers.

Effective Communications: Delivers helpful feedback that focuses on behaviours without offending the recipient. Listens to feedback without defensiveness and uses it for own performance effectiveness. Independently makes oral presentations and written reports needed for own work. Leads meetings and teams effectively.

Data Gathering & Analysis: Participates in gathering and analysing parts information business data. Utilizes data to identify problems and reach a conclusion, goal, or judgment. Identifies and reports problems that arise in the data collection process.

Collaboration & Engagement: Ability to establish and maintain healthy working relationships with peers and other relevant stakeholders.

Problem Solving: Identifies and documents specific problems and resolution alternatives. Examines a specific problem and understands the perspective of everyone involved. Works with all stakeholders to address critical issues and resolve major problems.

Caterpillar is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status; however, as a global company, Caterpillar offers many job opportunities outside of India which can be found through our employment website at [www.caterpillar.com/careers](http://www.caterpillar.com/careers).

**What You Will Get:**

Our goal at Caterpillar is for you to have a rewarding career. Our teams are critical to the success of our customers who build a better world. Here you earn more than just wage, because we value your performance, we offer a total rewards package that provides day one benefits along with the potential of a variable bonus. Additional benefits include paid annual leave, flexi leave, medical and insurance (prorated based upon hire date).

**Final Details:**

Please frequently check the email associated with your application, including the junk/spam folder, as this is the primary correspondence method. If you wish to know the status of your application – please use the candidate log-in on our career website as it will reflect any updates to your status.

If you are interested in joining our team, please apply using an English version of your CV. We look forward to meeting you!

It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as an exhaustive summary of all responsibilities, duties and effort required of employees

With over 1.5 million connected assets worldwide, our teams use data, technology, advanced analytics, telematics, and AI capabilities to help our customers build a better, more sustainable world.

**Job Summary:**

Oversees project management, planning, and organization to enable the implementation of multiple and complex eCommerce and digital marketing initiatives, including platform enhancements, campaign execution, and marketing technology integrations by leveraging enterprise project management, roadmap, workflow and AI-assisted productivity tools (e.g. Azure DevOps, Aha!, MS Project, Adobe Workfront, Copilot).

**What You Will Do**:

- Managing the day-to-day activities of eCommerce and digital marketing projects, including overseeing project scope, responsibilities, timelines, and dependencies.

- Developing contingency and mitigation plans for campaign, platform, data, or vendor‑related risks.

- Directing and motivating cross‑functional teams (marketing, product, engineering, vendors, and agencies).

- Evaluating and managing deliverables to ensure business, brand, and performance expectations are achievable, defined, and met.

- Monitoring project outcomes for significant deviations in schedule, or performance KPIs, ensuring adherence to quality standards and processes.

- Leading stakeholder communication and expectation management across marketing, product, engineering, data, and business leadership.


**What You Will Have**:

**Planning: Tactical and Strategic**

Knowledge of effective digital portfolio, roadmap and program planning techniques and the ability to contribute to operational (short term), tactical (1–2 years), and strategic (3–5 years) planning in support of overall eCommerce and digital marketing objectives.

Level: Extensive Experience

- Develops, refines, and communicates tactical plans for assigned eCommerce and digital marketing initiatives.

- Provides the appropriate level of detail to support channel, platform, and growth strategy planning.

- Demonstrates the value of aligning campaign‑level execution with broader digital and business strategy.

- Ensures attention to dependencies across marketing, product, engineering, data, business and vendor deliverables.

- Plans for allocation of resources in line with business goals, marketing priorities, and technology constraints.

- Ensures planning processes are integrated with the overall business roadmap.


**Project Management**

Knowledge of project management practices; ability to plan, organize, monitor, and control eCommerce and digital marketing initiatives using organizational strategies, modern delivery frameworks, and workflow and project management tools.

Level: Extensive Experience

- Oversees end‑to‑end delivery to ensure initiatives launch on time and meet expectations.

- Reports dependencies, conflicts, and milestone status to project leadership and stakeholders.

- Supports and enables team adoption of project management and collaboration tools.

- Ensures project issues, blockers, risks, and decisions are documented and escalated appropriately.

- Develops templates and frameworks for planning and communication governance (planned and ad-hoc) with internal teams, agencies, and external partners.

- Revises work plans to ensure intermediate milestones are achievable.

- Facilitates regular status and working sessions with project teams.

- Tracks and reports overall project progress and delivery health, using schedules, roadmaps, scorecards, and dashboards.


**Decision Making and Critical Thinking**

Knowledge of structured decision‑making approaches; ability to analyze complex scenarios, apply specific frameworks and make informed recommendations.

Level: Extensive Experience

- Differentiates assum

# Career Growth with Integrity: Lavanya's Top Tips for Success

Share


With 16+ years of professional experience in various domains of Human Resources, Lavanya has seen it all - from welcoming new hires to watching them reach great heights. She believes that a true career progression is built on personal and professional values. Here are her top five tips to help you climb the career ladder while staying true to yourself:

1. Build the right skills while retaining your personality: As work and life evolve, adapt and pursue the necessary skills to grow but never forget the core personality that defines who you are.
2. Be transparent: As a leader in Talent Acquisition, I always emphasize integrity. Authenticity is the key for the employees and potential talent within any organization to create success.
3. Pay it forward: Be the coach who guides others just as your mentor guided you.
4. Build the right network: Network with leaders who are visionaries in enhancing the talent ecosystem. Networking should be intentional, to make the most of everyone’s time.
5. Stay humble: Humility and positivity in challenging situations help keep the focus on doing the work that matter.

“Staying humble and sticking to your roots will help build you as a person as you grow in your career. Growth is not about becoming high-headed but becoming a servant leader,” says Lavanya.

Be a part of an organization that embraces integrity and puts people first, check out our open positions here.

[Career blogs](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/life-at-caterpillar/career-blogs/)

# The Power of Communication: How Nithya Turned Conversations into Career Growth

Share


In today’s fast-paced workplace, technical skills often take center stage. But ask any successful leader, andthey’ll tell you—communication is the real game changer. For Nithya, Parts Information Manager at Caterpillar’s Chennai office, this truth shaped her journey from a shystudent  to a leader managing a team of 17.

Nithya began her career as a Parts Information Specialist in 2016. Her early role involved providingaccurate and accessible information about Parts to our dealers and customers. But instead of sticking to the basics, she looked for ways to add value. One of her key contributions was digitizing the parts manual for a new product, which made it easier for customers to identify the right components and significantly improved overall technician efficiency. Her proactive approach earned recognition and opened doors to coaching opportunities with leaders across different segments. These conversations taught her a vital lesson: listening to customers and voicing your perspective can transform your career trajectory.

![](https://careers.caterpillar.com/media/5uwfyu0z/img_1405.jpg?rmode=max&width=521&height=391&hmac=25568a19bbaf27ce513b464d600ae052a25bb041113612795493c13353d47a37)

Growing up in an all-girls school in a remote town, Nithya onceshied away from speaking up. Today, she emphasizes that communicationisn’tjustaboutpolished sentences—it’s about adding value to conversations and improving the way we work, even by 1% every day.

Nithya believes communication drives collaboration, sparks innovation, and builds trust. She shares five practical tips for making workplace conversations more effective:

1\. **Context is Key:**Make sure your listener has the right background information to prevent confusion and allow for quick decision-making.

2\. **Embrace Mistakes:**Don’t let fear of imperfect sentences hold you back. Opening up is the first step toward meaningful dialogue.

3\. **Listen Before You Respond:**Active listening helps you understand the real issue before jumping to solutions.It’s the foundation of empathy and problem-solving.

4\. **Be Inclusive:**Pass the mic. Encourage diverse perspectives in meetings and discussions. Inclusion fuels creativity and better outcomes.

5\. **Turn Off Subtitles:**In virtual meetings, try turning off captions occasionally. It challenges you to listen actively and adapt, improving your communication skills over time.

![](https://careers.caterpillar.com/media/f3qj5h1e/image4.jpeg?rmode=max&width=621&height=466&hmac=b9006e9f4151a392dd59c4b7a10810b5f3ccc1d8bdda1c14b728db3929bbd566)

> “When the right voices go unheard, we lose valuable ideas, creativity, and new ways of working that impact not only individual growth but the success of the entire organization.”

![](https://careers.caterpillar.com/media/tymjtyrn/swe.jpg?rmode=max&width=743&height=383&hmac=7a47e566a4b5e82f7467d0717aab526624c894b5577fbcbc57a3904ef99c5d15)

> “Show up, listen, and speak up. Communication isn’t just a soft skill—it’s a growth strategy. It helps individuals build confidence, teams collaborate better, and organizations innovate faster”

[Career blogs](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/life-at-caterpillar/career-blogs/)

# Building Blocks for Success: The Power of a Career Development Plan

Share


Feeling lost or stagnant in your own career? You're not alone. Many people lack a clear direction, unsure how to leverage their strengths and interests. But unlike a confusing map, your career path doesn't need to be rigid. You can navigate it strategically with a career plan.

Jessyca K. is a human resource professional with 10+ years of experience in roles ranging from Talent Acquisition Lead, Human Resources Manager, and, currently, Talent Management. She thrives from partnering with customers to solve business problems and is passionate about how these solutions impact people's lives. Check out her tips on developing your career.

### **Step 1: Reflection**

Just like exploring a new city, you need to understand your starting point before venturing out. Reflection helps you gather your thoughts and gain clarity on:

- **Values:** What's important to you in your work?
- **Interests:** What topics or areas of the business draw you in?
- **Skills:** What technical and soft skills do you possess? Skills are transferrable, how can you use them in different areas or roles?
- **Strengths:** What are you naturally good at?
- **Development Opportunities:** Are there skills you'd like to acquire to reach your goals?

**Reflecting on Alignment:**

Your career path should complement your life. Consider:

- **Purpose:** What impact do you want to make with your work?
- **Priorities:** Work-life balance, compensation, or a specific work environment?
- **Personal Circumstances:** Family obligations, commute time, or relocation needs.

### **Step 2: Explore**

After you’ve taken some time to **REFLECT** on your skills, abilities, and aspirations – take those notes and explore a variety of development opportunities. There are three main ways we learn and develop – [read about them here](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/life-caterpillar/life-at-caterpillar/building-a-better-career-through-a-career-development-plan/ "Building a Better Career through a Career Development Plan").Some examples are taking on a stretch assignment to strengthen a skill, identifying a mentor in a different area of the business or mentoring another employee to enhance leadership skills.

### **Step 3: Plan**

A common question asked in career discussions is, "Where do you want to be in your career in 5, 10 or 15 years?" Would this question give you pause? Remember, you own your career! Career planning puts you in charge as you prepare for the future by identifying the necessary skills needed to navigate your career journey, whether your aspirations are to increase your performance and impact in your current role or to explore other potential roles.

- Now that you havereflected on your current situation andexplored the opportunities to develop, it’s time to connect both and identify your focus areas.
- Consider your desired outcomes, such as a promotion or a new skill, to create development objectives like completing a leadership course or improving your technical skills.
- Be specific about determining actions, such as attending training sessions or taking on new projects, and success indicators, like receiving positive feedback or achieving specific performance metrics.
- Once you have a plan, it's important to share it with a leader, mentor, or peer for their input. This not only shows your commitment to your development but also allows others to understand and support your goals. In your plan, commit to reasonable next steps and a timeline to check in, ensuring that your career planning is a collaborative and valued process.

### **Step 4: Act**

- Initiate and experiment
- Ask for feedback
- Evaluate outcomes and adjust as needed
- Remember, it’s a journey, the cycle is continuous

Now that you know the steps to building a strategic career plan, get ready to put them to use! [Find open roles with Team Caterpillar.](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/ "Find Open Jobs")[Find Open Jobs](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/ "Find Open Jobs")

**Career Area:**

Students and Graduates

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**¡Ven y forma parte de nuestro gran equipo de trabajo!**

**Ciudad de México en Zona Santa Fe**

Estamos buscando un Practicante del área de Marketing – Ventas el cual tendrá la oportunidad de dar el siguiente paso en su educación más significativa a través de la experiencia práctica en un entorno empresarial internacional. Va a transformar el aprendizaje académico en soluciones de negocio mientras se desarrolla en proyectos reales.

**Actividades:**

- Elaboración y análisis de reportes de KPI’s, tendencias de mercado y analíticas comerciales
- Soporte en la elaboración de presentaciones ejecutivas
- Estudios de mercado sobre la competencia y competitividad de productos
- Ayudar a los representantes de ventas y gerentes de cuentas internacionales con proyectos de ventas especiales
- Análisis de campañas y contenido digital
- Soporte en logística de eventos, presentaciones y estudios de mercado
- Territorio de enfoque: México & Centroamérica
- Productos de enfoque: Equipo de Construcción, Minería e Infraestructura

**Requisitos Indispensables:**

- Carreras: Ingenierías, Mercadotecnia, Finanzas, Comercio o Informática
- Inglés: Intermedio/Avanzado
- Paquetes: Word, Excel & Power Point (nivel medio/alto).

**Requisitos Deseables:**

- Otros Idiomas: Portugués/ Chino
- Habilidades: Comunicación, Data Mining, Data Analytics, Sentido de Negocio, Retail, Trabajo bajo presión

**Importante:**

- Lugar de Trabajo: Cd de México **(Zona Santa Fe)**
- Al menos 1 e ideal 2 años para terminar carrera
- Expectativa de al menos 40 hrs/ semanales laborales
- Esquema **% presencial**

**Detalles finales:**

Verifique con frecuencia el correo electrónico asociado con su aplicación, incluida la carpeta de correo basura/spam, ya que este es el método principal de correspondencia. Si desea conocer el estado de su solicitud, utilice el inicio de sesión de candidato en nuestro sitio web de carreras, ya que reflejará cualquier actualización de su estado.

En esta empresa no solicitamos pruebas de embarazo, ni pruebas de VIH como requisitos para el ingreso, permanencia o ascenso en el empleo.

[Career blogs](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/life-at-caterpillar/career-blogs/)

# How to Ace Your Virtual Interview

Share


About five years ago, Susan T. helped launch Caterpillar’s Virtual Recruiting Team to support Early Talent recruiting for Engineering. This innovative team leverages technology to engage with and assess applicants from schools where we don’t have an on-campus presence. The result? A broader, more diverse candidate pool.

Drawing from Susan’s 28 years of experience with Caterpillar, here are her team’s top tips to help you shine in your next virtual interview.

### 1\. First Impressions Start with Your Setup

- Turn your camera on: We will have ours on! It helps make the experience feel more like meeting in person.

- Test your tech: Check your camera and mic beforehand. Headphones are fine and can reduce background noise. Make sure you speak clearly into the microphone.

- Double-check lighting: Make sure your face is well-lit and clearly visible.

- Dress the part: Dress professionally and appropriately for the role you’re interviewing for, even for a virtual interview. A suit probably isn’t required for an internship interview, but maybe skip the bathrobe (yes, we’ve seen that!).

- Mind your background: A tidy space makes a better impression than an unmade bed or broken blinds.


### 2\. Presence Matters

- Engage with body language: Nod, smile, and use hand gestures to show you’re listening

- Look at the camera: It helps simulate eye contact. Adjust your setup if needed.


### 3\. Prepare Your Interview Content

- Expect behavioral questions: We’ll ask for examples of things you’ve done – use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to answer. [You can find more information on this method here.](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/life-at-caterpillar/life-at-caterpillar/how-to-ace-your-caterpillar-interview/ "STAR Method Prep")

- Have 5-6 “stories” ready: Think of projects or experiences that highlight your skills, decision-making and learning. Recall the situation details, what the assignment was and what was challenging about it; what your role was and how you made decisions about what to do; what you actually did, how things turned out, and what you learned. If you have these stories fresh in your mind (or on notes that you can see without seeming distracted or like you’re reading off a card) you should be able to answer our interview questions.

- Match your “stories” to the job: Identify which skills each story demonstrates and categorize them according to the skills that your interviewers will be looking for. Especially for a technical role, find examples that show technical ability.


### 4\. Practice Out Loud

- Talk it through: Explain your projects to a family member or friend outside your field. It’ll help you clarify and simplify your message to ensure you are comfortable and can explain it to an interviewer.

- Use Technical terms: Where applicable, don’t be afraid to use appropriate technical terminology in your examples. If your interviewer is from a different area of expertise, you may have to explain in more detail, but it’s important to represent your knowledge.


### 5\. Most Importantly: Be YOURSELF

- Let your personality and passion shine through: Smile, be enthusiastic and show us what excites you. Did you love that internship project? Were you excited to contribute to a product? We want you to be enthusiastic about your work! Show us what you really enjoyed working on.

- Don’t forget that interviewing is a two-way street: This is your chance to see if we’re the right fit for you.

- Remember, Interviewers are human too! A smile goes a long way to make a connection – especially when the interaction is limited to that little square computer screen.


Virtual interviews can be just as effective as in person. With the right preparation, presence, and mindset, you can make a lasting impression from behind the screen.

At Caterpillar, we’re always looking for passionate, curious, and driven individuals to join our team. Whether you're just starting your career or looking to take the next step, we encourage you to explore our open roles and apply what you’ve learned here. [Your next opportunity might just be one click – and one great virtual interview – away.](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/ "Search and Apply for Jobs")

# Condition monitoring advisor

| Location | [Bangalore](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Bangalore), [Karnātaka](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Karn%C4%81taka) / [Chennai](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Chennai), [Tamil Nādu](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Tamil%20N%C4%81du), [India](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?country=India) |
| Date Posted | Tuesday, February 3, 2026 |
| Apply By | Thursday, February 12, 2026 |
| Contract Type | Full time |
| Job Type | Regular |
| Requisition ID | R0000347788 |

## Description

**Career Area:**

Product Support

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**JOB PURPOSE:**

To aggregate, correlate and create Caterpillar customer and dealer recommendations using the 5 elements of condition monitoring in order to provide critical suggested actions to help customers lower owning and operating costs, improve availability, and reduce warranty costs. These recommendations will improve customer loyalty, increase enterprise market share, and grow parts and service sales, while reducing the customer's costs through equipment management efficiency.

**JOB RELATED ACCOUNTABILITY:**

- Provides equipment management services for at least 300 Product Link assets, 50 VIMS assets, or some combination of both.

**JOB DUTIES:**

Condition Monitoring Advisors (CMAs) will monitor the elements of S•O•SSM fluid analysis, equipment inspections, electronic data, repair history and site conditions to provide relevant recommendations as indicated by the data. This includes the following essential duties and responsibilities:

• Aggregate and correlate the five elements of condition monitoring, where all are available.

• Maintains working knowledge for each of the condition monitoring five elements with subject matter expertise in at least 3 elements.

• Provides equipment recommendations to Caterpillar dealers and customers.

• Uses enabling software tools & systems to prioritize and analyze condition monitoring data inputs.

• Extracts or is provided data from the appropriate business systems including customer, dealer and contractor sources.

• Provides information for equipment management lead tracking and resolution.

• Prepares standard written reports of recommendations for customers and their Caterpillar dealers on a monthly basis.

• Facilitates ongoing customer meetings with Caterpillar dealers, product groups, JSS and others as needed.

• Communicates verbal recommendations as required to customers and their Caterpillar dealers.

• Builds collaborative relationships & acts as a consultant with equipment management personnel.

**BACKGROUND/EXPERIENCE:**

6+ years in product support and/or engineering fields, with demonstrated on-machine troubleshooting experience. Strong customer service/contact experience, communication, and organizational skills are essential to success in this position. Possess the ability to work and provide direction in a team environment, and work with diverse groups of customers and business partners. Position requires initiative and ability to resolve issues either individually or through appropriate team guidance. A degree in engineering, business, marketing and/or equivalent application experience is highly desired. Working knowledge of engines, power train, hydraulic systems, Product Link™, VIMS, inspection process, equipment lifecycle planning, S•O•SSM Services, and the Service Information System is desired.

Relocation is available for this position.

**Posting Dates:**

February 3, 2026 - February 12, 2026

Caterpillar is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants of any age are encouraged to apply

## Description

**Career Area:**

Technology, Digital and Data

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Caterpillar is embarking on a **bold digital transformation to revolutionize our dealers’ systems** \- seamless, intelligent, and designed for the future. We are developing a scalable, modern ERP solution for Caterpillar dealers, built on Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Azure Integration Services. This next-generation platform will leverage pre-configured business processes, optimized workflows and AI-powered automation to streamline operations, accelerate digital capability deployment, and elevate customer experience across our global dealer network.

We are seeking a delivery-focused **Regional Deployment Manager** **(APD)** to lead a team of Deployment Consultants supporting Caterpillar Dealers and their selected System Integrator to deploy our Dealer ERP Template and the Annual Deployment Plan for Dealers with in the Asia Pacific region. This high-impact role is part of a newly launched strategic initiative and will serve as a delivery orchestrator, aligning teams to program objectives, managing risks and dependencies, and ensuring consistent, high-quality outcomes through scaled Agile practices. The Annual Deployment Plan supports the selected required and pilot deployments for all dealers, this role will lead Deployment Consultants that will work with dealer to gain commitment to deliver requested deployments and liaison with dealers oversee the successful deployment completions on time.

**The Regional Deployment Manager Will Do:**

- Managing the day-to-day activities of existing Caterpillar Deployment consultants and their ongoing Dealer Deployment projects both for **ERP Cat Dealer Template** and **Annual Deployment Plan (ADP),** including overseeing project responsibilities and schedules. Developing contingency plans for potential risks.


- Organizing and coordinating teams who work on diverse aspects of digital programs around the Caterpillar Dealership.


- Carry “Voice of the Dealer” into the Product and Engineering team for future deployment and dealer needs on MS Dynamics 365, and to Caterpillar Product owners for other Caterpillar Deployment Projects within the Annual Deployment Plan (ADP)


- Serving as an collaborative leader and liaison, developing deep working relationships between Caterpillar Dealers, System Integrators and Caterpillar product and engineering teams and Caterpillar DSD and Digital leaders.


- Facilitating meetings and discussions with Caterpillar Dealer, Cat Digital & DSD leaders to share continual update and full transparency of status on deployment projects for ERP & ADP.


- Lead and mentor existing team members to foster a high-performing Agile culture


- Identifying, escalating, and tracking risks, issues and cross-team dependencies so deployment projects run to committed time schedules and expected quality.


- Managing the Azure DevOps environment to support efficient deployment projects and accurate recording of Caterpillar Dealer Deployment needs for success project completion.


- Be a strong deployment SME voice in regular product and steering committee meetings and stakeholder events for the Cat Dealer ERP Template and other Cat Digital Products.


- Give strategic leadership to your deployment team and set working priorities.


- Achieve Enterprise set Deployment targets for each calendar year.


- Develop existing teams talent and skillsets in MS Dynamics 365 and supporting deployment tools such as Mavim, Leapworks, etc.


- Manage team T&E budget.


- Limited Travel required. (Up to 25% of working time)


​

**What You Will Have: Extensive experience in..**

- **ERP & Systems Knowledge** **(MS Dynamics 365 focus)**


- Hands-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 in a product, process or deployment leadership role.


- Understanding of ERP configuration, security, and integrations with related systems.


- Awareness of Microsoft release cycles and ability to assess new features fo

# Produktionsplaner Motorenproduktion (m/w/d)

| Location | [Kiel](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Kiel), [Schleswig Holstein](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Schleswig%20Holstein), [Germany](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?country=Germany) |

## Description

**Career Area:**

Human Resources

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Produktionsplaner Motorenproduktion** **(m/w/d)**

Bei Caterpillar engagieren wir uns für unsere Kunden, die mit unseren Produkten, Dienstleistungen und Lösungen eine bessere und nachhaltigere Welt aufbauen. Wir verstehen und zeigen ihnen, warum sie sich für uns entscheiden sollten. Wir erkunden neue Ideen und Chancen – wir entwickeln Innovationen, um die Durchbrüche zu entdecken, die für das Wachstum von morgen notwendig sind. Dabei sind wir [ein Team](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mYIDne7Q5ek&list=PLwAXbfivFg0jmiNqqgFLeQJpRB_i-UVln&index=39) – wir entwickeln und liefern erstklassige Produkte, Dienstleistungen und Lösungen, die der Konkurrenz überlegen sind. Erfahren Sie mehr über das [Caterpillar-Erlebnis](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GvCYWtWARlk&list=PLwAXbfivFg0jmiNqqgFLeQJpRB_i-UVln&index=25).

Caterpillar Inc. ist der weltweit führende Hersteller von Bau- und Bergbaumaschinen, Off-Highway-Dieselmotoren und Erdgasmotoren, industriellen Gasturbinen und dieselelektrischen Lokomotiven. Wir haben eine spannende Gelegenheit für eine/n **Produktionsplaner Motorenproduktion (m/w/d)** inunserem Large Power Systems Team, um die Produktion großer Caterpillar-Motoren an unserem **Standort in Kiel** zu unterstützen.

**Das ist Ihr Verantwortungsbereich bei uns:**

- Verantwortung für die Erstellung, Steuerung und laufende Anpassung der Produktionspläne unter Berücksichtigung von Kapazitäten, Materialverfügbarkeit und Terminvorgaben


- Aktives Management von Planabweichungen, Engpässen und Terminrisiken im Produktionsbetrieb


- Analyse von Effizienz, Qualität und Termintreue der Produktionsprozesse sowie Ableitung geeigneter Verbesserungsmaßnahmen


- Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Bereichen wie Vertrieb, Engineering, Montage, Prüfstand und Logistik


- Weiterentwicklung von Planungslogiken, Steuerungsprozessen und relevanten Planungsparametern


- Überwachung von Produktionsbeständen, Maschinenstillständen und Kapazitätsauslastung zur Sicherstellung stabiler Abläufe


- Erstellung kundenspezifischer oder projektbezogener Auftragslisten sowie Einarbeitung technischer Änderungen oder Neuentwicklungen


- Mitarbeit in bereichsübergreifenden Abstimmungs- und Eskalationsformaten zur kurzfristigen Produktionssicherung


- Direkter Beitrag zur Verbesserung von Liefertreue, Durchlaufzeiten und Bestandsstabilität


**Das sollten Sie mitbringen:**

- Mehrjährige Erfahrung in der Produktionsplanung oder -steuerung in einem industriellen Fertigungsumfeld


- Sehr gute Kenntnisse in der Produktions- und Kapazitätsplanung sowie im Umgang mit Planungstools und ERP-Systemen, insbesondere Materialstammdaten und Stücklisten


- Technisches Verständnis und sicherer Umgang mit Konstruktionszeichnungen


- Erfahrung im Forecasting und in der Ableitung von Produktionsbedarfen aus Absatz- und Kundenanforderungen


- Ausgeprägte Analyse- und Problemlösungskompetenz sowie die Fähigkeit, Entscheidungen unter Zeitdruck zu treffen


- Sicheres Prozessverständnis und Erfahrung in der Optimierung und Weiterentwicklung bestehender Abläufe


- Hohe Kommunikationsfähigkeit und Durchsetzungsstärke im Umgang mit internen und externen Schnittstellen


- Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse sowie gute Englischkenntnisse


- Abgeschlossene technische oder kaufmännische Ausbildung, gerne ergänzt durch eine Weiterbildung – oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation


- Hoher Anspruch an Präzision, Sorgfalt und zuverlässige Arbeitsweise


**Ihre Benefits – was wir Ihnen bieten:**

- Abwechslungsreiche und herausfordernde Aufgaben


- Hervorragende Verdienstmöglichkeiten


- Sonderzahlungen wie Urlaubsgeld, Weihnachtsgeld, etc.


- 30 Tage Urlaub / Arbeitszeitkonto


- Betriebliche Altersvorsorge


- Fahrrad-Leasing / Jobticket / Wellbeing Allowance


**Hinweis**

Diese Position erfordert Ihre Präsenz in unserem Werk in Kiel und ist nicht für Remote- oder Hybrid-Arbeit geeignet.

Ein gültiger Arbeitsnachweis oder die Staatsbürgerschaft eines EU-25/EFTA-Landes ist für diese Stelle erforderlich.

**Über Caterpillar**

Caterpillar Inc. ist der weltweit führende Hersteller von Bau- und Bergbaumaschinen, Off-Highway-Diesel- und Erdgasmotoren, industriellen Gasturbinen und dieselelektrischen Lokomotiven. Seit über 100 Jahren helfen wir unseren Kunden, eine bessere und nachhaltigere Welt aufzubauen, und engagieren uns

# Engineering

Caterpillar engineers conceptualize ideas that become real-world products and solutions — and make progress possible around the globe.

## A career in Engineering

Our engineering team is made up of problem solvers, innovators, and go-getters from every discipline, including chemical, electrical, material, mechanical, and metallurgical. Their work touches virtually every area of our company, from product design to software development.

We encourage our engineers to explore various opportunities within our organization through our rotational program, and provide a variety of professional growth and volunteer opportunities, like STEM outreach in local schools to help inspire the next generation of engineers.

## Electrification

We solve problems for our customers across industries, like oil and gas, mining, electric power, construction and more. Experts in fields ranging from battery and fuel cell technology to energy policy work together to push the boundaries of electrification and make innovative energy solutions a reality for data centers.

We're best known for our iron, but we've been a leader in innovative technology for more than 30 years, too. Our autonomous vehicles are working on three continents have traveled hundreds of millions of kilometers. See where we’ve been — and where you can help us go in the future.

[Human Resources](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/career-areas/human-resources/)

# Auxiliar de Recursos Humanos

| Location | [Acuna](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Acuna), [Coahuila](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Coahuila), [Mexico](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?country=Mexico) |
| Date Posted | Wednesday, February 4, 2026 |
| Apply By | Monday, February 9, 2026 |
| Contract Type | Full time |
| Job Type | Fixed Term - Temporary (Fixed Term) |
| Requisition ID | R0000348185 |

## Description

**Career Area:**

Human Resources

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Acerca del puesto**

Apoya a la empresa ejecutando tareas administrativas y de atención al cliente en el área de Recursos Humanos.

**Qué harás:**

- Llevar a cabo el proceso de contratación digital del personal de nuevo ingreso.
- Capacidad para implementar tareas, proceso o proyectos relacionados con Recursos Humanos.
- Contribuye a la excelencia operativa en Recursos Humanos ejecutando de manera eficiente y precisa los procesos relacionados con el cumplimiento normativo laboral, la nómina, la gestión de ausencias, el control horario, la administración de beneficios y la gestión de datos del personal.
- Proporciona una excelente experiencia de servicio al ejecutar los procesos dirigidos al personal.
- Identifica proactivamente oportunidades para mejorar la experiencia del personal, la calidad, la rapidez y/o el costo de los procesos de Recursos Humanos.
- Apoya el mantenimiento de una base de datos de conocimientos de Operaciones de Recursos Humanos y la recopilación y presentación de métricas operativas de Recursos Humanos.

**Escolaridad Requerida:**

Carrera Universitaria terminada (Licenciatura en Recursos Humanos, Licenciatura en Administración, Ingeniería en Gestión Empresarial o un campo relacionado)

**Habilidades y experiencias requeridas:**

**Se detallan algunas de las habilidades más importantes que son requeridas en esta posición:**

**Mejora de Procesos de Negocio** **:**

- Dominio en el análisis y manejo de métricos de recursos humanos
- Capacidad analítica y de resolución de problemas para identificar áreas de mejora y desarrollar soluciones efectivas.

**Captura de información:**

- Manejo intermedio de herramientas del paquete Microsoft Office.
- Capacidad para identificar, capturar y documentar información relevante de una manera auditable, organizada y comprensible.

**Políticas, Normas y Procedimientos de Recursos Humanos:**

- Conocimiento de las políticas, prácticas y procedimientos de Recursos Humanos.
- Capacidad para mantener un alto nivel de confidencialidad y la atención al detalle.

**Consideraciones para los mejores candidatos:**

- Conocimiento en el sistema Workday.
- Conocimiento mínimo de 1 a 2 años en puestos similares
- Ingles intermedio
- PowerBI

**Información adicional:**

- **Esta es una vacante temporal por 12 meses**
- Esta vacante es 100% presencial y la locación es Cd. Acuña, Coahuila

**Detalles finales**

Revise con frecuencia el correo electrónico asociado con su solicitud, incluida la carpeta de correo no deseado/spam, ya que este es el método de correspondencia principal. Si desea conocer el estado de su solicitud, utilice el inicio de sesión de candidato (a) en nuestro sitio web de carreras, ya que reflejará cualquier actualización de su estado.

**El Código de conducta de Caterpillar establece que seleccionaremos y ubicaremos a al personal según sus calificaciones para el trabajo a realizar, sin distinción de raza, religión, origen nacional, color, género, identidad de género, orientación sexual, edad o discapacidad. En esta empresa no solicitamos pruebas de embarazo, ni pruebas de VIH como requisitos para el ingreso, permanencia o promoción laboral.**

**Posting Dates:**

February 4, 2026 - February 9, 2026

Caterpillar is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants of any age are encouraged to apply

Not ready to apply? Join our [Talent Community](http://flows.beamery.com/caterpillarinc/talcom).

# Calibrador/Calibradora de Herramientas Especializado

| Location | [Nuevo Leon](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Nuevo%20Leon), [Nuevo León](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Nuevo%20Le%C3%B3n), [Mexico](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?country=Mexico) |
| Date Posted | Thursday, February 5, 2026 |
| Apply By | Wednesday, February 11, 2026 |
| Contract Type | Full time |
| Job Type | Regular |
| Requisition ID | R0000346859 |

## Description

**Career Area:**

Operations

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

PROPÓSITO GENERAL:

El titular es responsable del armado de herramientas de corte (PA) con calidad y eficiencia, es responsable de la entrega de turno, de dejar los pendientes en el pizarrón de pendientes, de mantener el área limpia, ordenada. Es responsable de cuidar la herramienta manual y de reportar si alguna herramienta manual está dañada.

II. FUNCIONES:

1\. Utilizar su EPP completo según la hoja de trabajo standard de su estación y mantenerlos en buenas condiciones, así como usarlos siempre que esté dentro de la planta, y reportara toda condición insegura que vea dentro de la planta, y la reportar al supervisor.

2\. Realizar el mantenimiento preventivo de su máquina de calibración ZOLLER de acuerdo con el programa de TPM, y reportara de inmediato cuando se detecte alguna condición insegura que involucre la ZOLLER, así como reportar algún desperfecto o daño en el hardware o software de la máquina de calibración ZOLLER

3\. Realizar el llenado del check list de inicio seguro turno (capa 0), así como notificar de inmediato al supervisor si se encuentra algún detalle que no cumpla con el arranque seguro, también es responsable de arreglar si está dentro de su conocimiento, la condición o acto o reportarlo al supervisor, y no arrancar hasta no arreglar la condición o acto inseguro.

4\. Mantener el área delimitada en plano dentro de los conceptos de orden y limpieza establecidos por 5´s.,también será el responsable de reportar al supervisor si los compañeros del turno anterior no están dejando los el área limpia y ordenada de acuerdo a la normar de las 5´s, también se responsabilizara que dentro del paro de los 15 minutos antes de terminar el turno deberán dejar el área limpia, ordenada y sin basura, también cuando el colaborador le toque en el turno de noche se encargara de trapear el área los días Miércoles y Viernes.

5\. Llevar gráficas de control estadístico para controlar su proceso y herramientas. Uno de los colaboradores tendrá que responsabilizarse del llenado del tablero de hora por hora, y el colaborador que ande en el turno de tarde, se encargara de realizar la sumatoria de la producción de los turnos, noche, día y tarde, para ver si se llegó a la meta diaria de producción, si se llega a la meta, deberá repintar la columna del tablero en la parte de producción diaria con color verde, y con color rojo si no se llega a la meta de producción.

6\. Cumplir los lineamientos requeridos por CPS en la función de su puesto.

7\. Participar e implementar proyectos e ideas de mejora dentro y fuera del área de trabajo que ayuden a alcanzar y mejorar métricos establecidos de seguridad, calidad, productividad y costo

8\. Identifica problemas de calidad, personal, seguridad y producción y los comunica inmediatamente al Gerente de sección.

9\. Cuenta con métricas para un área determinada, es responsable de la precisión de la métrica para cada operación y de que los informes sean presentados en forma oportuna.

10\. Monitorea y audita la métrica de PQVC en el nivel de celda de su área, por ejemplo, controla el desempeño comparando con el Trabajo Estándar, los puntos de control significativos y los indicadores del área.

11\. Reúne datos y actualiza los tableros de métricos según sea necesario

III. MÁQUINAS Y EQUIPOS INVOLUCRADOS:

Computadora y equipos de levante, Equipo de calibración zoller.

IV. HERRAMIENTAS UTILIZADAS:

Herramientas de medición como: micrómetros, vernier, Herramientas manuales como martillos, Llaves españolas, llaves allen, pinzas mecánicas, torquímetros, vernier, flexómetro.

V. ESCOLARIDAD / HABILIDADES REQUERIDAS

Técnico en Máquinas y Herramientas con 2 año de experiencia en armado de herramientas de Corte, operación de centros de maquinado, conocimientos de Interpretación de planos, conocimiento de herramientas de corte, tolerancias geométricas, proyectos de mejora, alineación de rimas en centros de maquinado, capacidad de realizar set up de numero de parte, programación básica de CNC, conocimiento de rectificado, conocimiento de hand lap, responsabilidad, liderazgo, trabajo en equipo, honestidad, sentido de urgencia, trabajo bajo presión, habilidad de comunicación, Liderazgo y habilidad de solución de problemas.

ETTCA - MACHOTE

NOTA: Interesados e inter

# Machine Repair Mechanic - 2nd or 3rd Shift

| Location | [Mapleton](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?location=Mapleton), [Illinois](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?region=Illinois), [United States Of America](https://careers.caterpillar.com/en/jobs/?country=United%20States%20Of%20America) |
| Date Posted | Tuesday, December 9, 2025 |
| Apply By | Monday, March 2, 2026 |
| Contract Type | Full time |
| Job Type | Regular |
| Requisition ID | R0000339451 |

## Description

**Career Area:**

Operations

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Large Power Systems Division (LPSD):**

We design and manufacture the most comprehensive lineup of large diesel and natural gas engines in the industry, as well as engine components and powertrains.

As a Machine Repair Mechanic at our Mapleton Foundry, you will inspect equipment and plan repairs for service required. You are also responsible for checking blueprints, diagrams, or drawings for repairs or installations when necessary and do lay-out work and fabrication. Also the use of various hand tools and equipment to dismantle, repair, replace and reassemble parts and equipment is required. You will assemble and align gears, bearings, cams, and shafts involving the application of press, sliding and running fits. While operating machinery such as drill presses, lathes, mills, grinders, power saws, and cutting & threading machines.

**Caterpillar has exciting opportunities for Machine Repair Mechanics at our Mapleton Facility! These are 2nd or 3rd shift hourly positions. Pay starts at $35.81/hr. plus $1.00 per hour shift differential. These Full Time positions offer benefits including health insurance, paid time off, paid holidays, 401K etc.**

**What You Will Do (including but not limited to):**

- Performs maintenance pipefitting on all equipment

- Changes, sets up, and adjusts equipment as required

- Lubricates or directs others to lubricate equipment

- Uses all common and precision checking equipment, laser equipment, and portable power tools Installs, repairs, and maintains pumps

- Uses Hydraulic equipment, Metal cutting and forming equipment, Compressors, valves, turbines, steam delivery systems and Material handling equipment


**Basic Qualifications:**

- Successfully pass a 2-hour skills exam

- 3-5 years maintenance experience


**Physical Qualifications (with or without reasonable accommodation):**

- Be able to work near high voltage electricity, molten metal, heavy equipment, various gases, moving machinery, high above the floor and in a congested or confined areas


**Top Candidates Will Have:**

- Strong mechanical background

- Machine shop experience

- Technical degree/diploma

- Must be able to weld, cut, solder and braze


**What You Will Get:**

- **Safety in words and actions.** Safety is a core value at Caterpillar. We encourage and commit to safety in all processes that we do. We strive to provide a safe and secure work environment for all our employees.

- **A rewarding career.** Our teams are critical to the success of our customers who build a better world. Here you earn more than just wage. Because we value your performance, we offer a total rewards package that provides:

- Starting salary is $35.81/hr. plus $1.00 per hour shift differential

- Medical, dental, and vision coverage

- Paid time off plan (prorated based on start date)

- 401k savings plan

- Wellness programs


**Additional Information:**

- Position is located in Mapleton, Illinois

- This position is on 2nd or 3rd shift

- No relocation reimbursement is offered


**Please ensure you frequently check the email account you provided on your application, including the junk/spam folder, as this is the primary correspondence method in our recruiting process.**

#LI

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other.
