### **Maintenance Electrician – 1st Shift**

**Location:** South Milwaukee, WI

**Shift: 1st** Shift (Full-Time)

**Bonus:** $2,000 Sign-On Bonus

**Pay Progression:**

• Step 1: $39.53/hr

• Step 2: $40.75/hr

• Step 3: $41.96/hr

**Overview:**

Caterpillar is hiring a **Maintenance Electrician** to support our manufacturing operations in **South Milwaukee, WI.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

At our South Milwaukee facility, you’ll be part of a team that builds some of the largest and most powerful mining machines in the world. This facility specializes in the production of Cat® Electric Rope Shovels (ERS) used in mining operations around the world.

**Key Responsibilities:**

- Troubleshoot and repair electrical systems on CNC machines, PLC-controlled equipment, and relay logic systems
- Maintain and repair machine tools and equipment from manufacturers including Ingersoll, DMG Mori, Liebherr, G&L, Pietro Carnaghi, and Pama
- Work with encoder systems including Heidenhain and Inductosyn

- Support electrical maintenance on cranes, hoists, conveyors, furnaces, welding equipment (Miller and Lincoln), paint booths, and heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems
- Perform preventive and predictive maintenance to reduce downtime and improve equipment reliability
- Read and interpret electrical schematics, ladder logic diagrams, and machine tool prints

**Required Qualifications:**

- Minimum of 3 years of experience in electrical maintenance of industrial machinery or successful completion of a field-related technical program
- Experience with CNC and PLC systems including Fanuc, Siemens, and Allen Bradley
- Strong troubleshooting skills with relay logic and programmable controllers
- Hands-on experience with AC/DC analog and digital drives
- Basic mechanical aptitude
- Possess one of the following:
  - Wisconsin Electrician License
  - State-indentured Journeyman Electrician or Electronic Technician
  - Associate Degree in electrical or electronics
  - Union Journeyman Card
  - Equivalent military experience

**Preferred Qualifications:**

- Experience with heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems
- Experience with welding equipment and industrial automation
- Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment
- Willingness to work overtime to support production needs
- Familiarity with safety protocols and lockout/tagout procedures

**Compensation and Benefits:**

- Hourly pay based on experience (including a $1.00/hour shift differential): Step 1 – $37.83, Step 2 – $39.00, Step 3 – $40.17
- $2,000 sign-on bonus is available
- Benefits effective on date of hire:
  - Health, dental, vision, and life insurance
  - 401(k) with 6% company match
  - Paid vacation, holidays, parental leave, military duty, jury duty, and bereavement leave
  - Employee assistance program and adoption support
  - Safety boot reimbursement and company-provided PPE including prescription safety glasses
  - Company-provided uniforms, lockers, showers, and access to a self-service canteen

**Additional Info**

- Full-time, 40 hours/week
- No relocation assistance
- Pre-employment drug screen and medical evaluation required
- Please check your email (including spam/junk folders) for updates

Safety is a core value at Caterpillar. We owe it to our employees and families to work in a safe and secure workplace. All candidates for employment must successfully pass a preemployment drug screen for prohibited substances as well as a medical evaluation.

$29.29 - $40.35

## Description

**Career Area:**

Finance

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**It's Your Career. Make it Matter.**

At Caterpillar, you build what matters—whether it’s the career you want, crucial work skills, strong relationships, or new digital technologies. There are big jobs to be done worldwide—building critical infrastructure, bolstering local economies, meeting the demand for energy—and that work is powered by our people.

Every single Caterpillar employee moves our company forward. Piece by piece, part by part, person by person. Together we create sustainable, world-changing solutions that impact lives around the globe. Join our winning team and help build a better world.

**About The Role**:

A Finance Analyst in the Finance Leadership Development Program will perform entry-level finance assignments and develop knowledge of Caterpillar’s procedures, philosophy, customers, dealers, organization, and financial practices.

**What You Will Do**

Responsibilities may include, but are not limited to:

- Our Finance Professionals deliver a wide range of financial management directions and services to a variety of business partners including suppliers, subsidiaries, business units, dealers, and customers.
- The Finance Analyst role will allow you opportunities in a variety of finance-related areas.
- Managing multi-currency cash flows.
- Managing foreign exchange/commodity risk.
- Execute an investment strategy for our multi-billion-dollar retirement fund.
- Analyze the financial strength of suppliers and work with our worldwide dealers to improve their financial results.

**What You Will Need**

**Required Qualifications:**

- Bachelor’s or Master’s degree at 4-year accredited university/college in one of the following degree programs: Finance, Economics or other Business or Technology major with a minimum 18 hours of Finance courses.
- Minimum 3.0/4.0 GPA (both Finance GPA & Cumulative GPA).
- Candidates must complete their degree before the hire date.

**Desired Qualifications:**

- This position requires 12 months or less of professional experience in this field (excluding internships)

**Skills Needed for Success in Role:**

- **Financial Markets**: Knowledge of the products, types, competitors, customer base, major issues and regulatory considerations in the financial markets; ability to apply financial market knowledge to various business environments.
- **Financial Analysis**: Knowledge of tools and approaches of financial analysis; ability to read, interpret and draw accurate conclusions from financial and numerical material.
- **Planning and Organizing**: Knowledge of the process of planning and arranging tasks and resources; ability to plan and organize both time and resources to get things completed while structuring and maintaining work in a systematic and highly methodical way.
- **Communicating Complex Concepts**: Knowledge of effective presentation tools and techniques to ensure clear understanding; ability to use summarization and simplification techniques to explain complex technical concepts in simple, clear language appropriate to the audience.
- **Problem Solving**: Knowledge of approaches, tools, techniques for recognizing, anticipating, and resolving organizational, operational or process problems; ability to apply knowledge of problem solving appropriately to diverse situations.

**What You Will Get**

Our goal at Caterpillar is for you to have a rewarding career. Our teams are critical to the success of our customers who build a better world. Here you earn more than just a salary because we value your performance.

- We offer a total rewards package that provides benefits on day one (medical, dental, vision, RX, and 401K) along with the potential of an annual bonus
- Additional benefits include paid vacation days and paid holidays (prorated based upon hire date).

**Additional Details**

- Location: Peoria, IL or Irving, TX - Candidates must be willing to relocate if necessary.
- Domestic relocation is available for those who qualify.
- Professionals within the program must be willing to travel as needed.
- Candidates must be willing to travel as needed.
- Visa Sponsorship is not available for this position.

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Vezetői szerep, nemzetközi környezet, stabil karrier – nálunk ez vár rád!

**Csatlakozz hozzánk Főkönyvelőként - Vezesd a számviteli folyamatokat egy világmárkánál – Légy a pénzügyi csapat motorja!**

Ha otthon vagy a számokban, szereted a kihívásokat és készen állsz inspirálni másokat, itt a lehetőség! A Caterpillar csapatában nemcsak egy pozíciót kapsz, hanem karrierlehetőséget egy dinamikus, nemzetközi környezetben, ahol a szakértelmed valódi értéket teremt.

**Feladataid lesznek:**

- Csapatépítés, támogatás és inspiráció – nálunk nem csak vezető leszel, hanem mentor is, aki segít a kollégáknak kibontakozni és ahol mindenki együtt dolgozik a közös célokért.
- Kulcsfontosságú pénzügyi folyamatok irányítása, hogy minden jelentés pontos és szabályos legyen.
- A havi mérleg- és eredménykimutatás összeállítása, könyvelők feladatainak koordinálása
- Éves beszámolók elkészítése a magyar számviteli szabványok (HAS) és az US GAAP előírásai szerint – precízen és időben.
- Vállalati adóbevallások összeállítása és benyújtása.
- Auditok gördülékeny lebonyolítása – legyen szó belső, külső vagy adóhatósági ellenőrzésről.
- Pénzügyi Fizetési utalások pontos és időben történő végrehajtása.

**Amit várunk Tőled:**

- Felsőfokú végzettség pénzügy, számvitel, közgazdaságtan vagy gazdasági informatika területén
- Mérlegképes könyvelői képesítés.
- Tapasztalat pénzügyi kimutatások elkészítésében.
- Erős analitikai készség és komplex számviteli koncepciók megértése.
- Proaktív, pontos munkavégzés, nyitottság új feladatokra.
- Kisebb csapat vezetésében és fejlesztésében való tapasztalat
- Magabiztos kommunikáció magyarul és angolul, belső és külső partnerekkel.

**Miért érdemes csatlakozni hozzánk?**

- Versenyképes fizetést és átfogó juttatási csomagot kínálunk (cafeteria, éves bónusz).
- Utazási támogatás: 45 Ft/km autós bejárás esetén.
- Dinamikus, nemzetközi környezetben dolgozhatsz, amely támogatja szakmai és személyes fejlődésed.
- Nyitottak vagyunk az ötleteidre, és tiszteljük a tapasztalatod
- Hosszú távú karrierlehetőséget nyújtunk egy olyan vállalatnál, amely segít egy jobb világ építésében.

**Posting Dates:**

January 14, 2026 - January 29, 2026

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Role Definition**

We are looking for a Lead System Test Architect to join our newly formed System Test Architecture (STA) team. The STA team is a part of Quality Assurance group that is working with talented cross-functional engineering teams that are building new applications and platform utilizing cutting-edge technologies. STA team is leading relentless pursuit of quality and best test practices on multiple large-scale programs for the CAT Digital Platform.

**What you will do:**

- Provide system test strategy and traceability matrix for manual and/or automation testing of features and capabilities of platform and applications.
- Be responsible for overseeing and mentoring junior STA team members and handle architecture, product and technical requirements, and data quality reviews.
- Competent to perform analysis, evaluation, make decisions, and identify actions to develop strategy for system level test execution teams on new and existing software products.
- Use your understanding of systems, regression, integration, performance, and user acceptance testing to identify gaps and overlaps in system test.
- Works directly on complex application/technical problem identification and resolution.

**What you will have:**

- **Effective Communications:** Understanding of effective communication concepts, tools and techniques; ability to effectively transmit, receive, and accurately interpret ideas, information, and needs through the application of appropriate communication behaviors.
- **Requirements Analysis:** Knowledge of tools, methods, and techniques of requirement analysis; ability to elicit, analyze and record required business functionality and non-functionality requirements to ensure the success of a system or software development project.
- **Application Design, Architecture:** Knowledge of basic activities and deliverables of application design; ability to utilize application design methodologies, tools and techniques to convert business requirements and logical models into a test strategies.
- **Software Product Testing:** Knowledge of software product testing; ability to design, plan, and execute testing strategies and tactics to ensure software product quality and adherence to stated requirements.

**Top candidates will have:**

- Typically have 12 years or more of software engineering experience

- Demonstrated experience developing test strategies and leading software projects.
- Prior experience with cloud environment (AWS strongly preferred). Knowledge of Azure DevOps, Power BI, and Excel (advanced).
- Experience with test automation tools such as Selenium and Karate

**Additional Details:**

- This position has the option to be based out of either our Chicago, IL, Peoria, IL, or Irving, TX (Dallas) offices.
- Relocation assistance is NOT available for this position
- Visa sponsorship is NOT available with this position now or in the future.

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you’re joining a global team who care not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers and future work builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don’t just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

The Resource Industries Operations and Products (RIOP) division consists of over 12 product lines, 8 global manufacturing facilities and more than 8,000 employees. Our products, services and solutions support customers primarily in the construction, surface and underground mining and quarry and aggregates industries.

The Caterpillar OEM Solutions Business is a growing segment in Resource Industries with a mission is to provide derivative Caterpillar components, systems, and machines for OEM applications. Our customers value OEM Solutions products because we leverage validated solutions and enable access to the Caterpillar dealer network giving the OEM products an advantage in the market. This role provides team leadership, project planning, budgeting and resource planning in Designing advanced electrical control systems and integrating electrical and mechanical systems.

**Additional Information:**

- **Location:** Mossville, IL (United States)
- **Required Travel:** Less than 10% (Domestic + International)
- **Relocation Assistance Offered:** Domestic
- **US Work Sponsorship Offered:** None

**What You Will Do:**

We are seeking a **Controls System Team Leader** candidate to lead the OEM Solutions powertrain and systems controls segments. In this key role you will report to the OEM Engineering manager and have design control responsibility for OEM Solutions Powertrain and Systems Software and Electrical product lines. This leader will work directly with end customers to architect new solutions integrating Caterpillar components and systems in their applications. This leader will have indirect leadership of team members and will develop Powertrain & Systems solutions for our Power Module & Components business, integrating Caterpillar Industrial Engines, Transmissions, and Caterpillar controls systems for existing and new OEM applications. The candidate will also Co-Lead powertrain industry growth projects to expand our product line and will have responsibility for Statements of Work (SOW) to secure resources for the key projects. The candidate will architect, scope, budget, and manage controls projects with major OEMs and have maintenance and product support responsibility for those product lines. The candidate must have experience with Caterpillar transmissions and hydraulics designs as well as Caterpillar test and development experience. A minimum of 10 years of qualified work experience with skills directly related to Caterpillar software and development tools and knowledge of Caterpillar L4 and L5 controls experience is required for this role.

**What You Will Have (Basic Requirements):**

• **Education Requirement:** Bachelor’s degree in an accredited Mechanical, Electrical Engineering or Computer Science or related curriculum

• **Technology Advising:** Knowledge of effective advisory methods and ability to provide valued information and advice to clients regarding products, technologies, services and solutions for a specific technology domain.

• **Project Management:** Knowledge of effective project management strategies and tactics; ability to plan, organize, monitor, and control projects, ensuring efficient utilization of technical and administrative resources to achieve project objectives.

• **Embedded Systems:** Knowledge of embedded systems and their applications; ability to develop embedded systems with varying levels of complexity to control the operations of the machine it is embedded within.

• **Requirements Analysis:** Knowledge of tools, methods, and techniques of requirement analysis; ability to elicit, analyze and record required business functionality and non-functionality requirements to ensure the success of a system or software development project.

**What You Will Get:**

**Role Definition**

This Logistics Management Engineer (PFEP Engineer) role focuses on optimizing warehouse storage and material flow, managing automation projects, and developing intelligent supply chain solutions such as vertical warehouse systems and AGVs. The position involves data analysis, benchmarking industry best practices, and building partnerships to improve supply chain performance, reduce costs, and enhance inventory utilization.

**Responsibilities**

- Internal warehouse storage optimization and development.
- Material replenishment flow optimization.
- Warehouse automation project management.
- Identifying, defining, and implementing integrated supply chain solutions and performance improvements, including building internal and external alliances to explore opportunities.

- Able to effectively develop intelligent supply chains, such as vertical warehouse systems, AGVs, automatic tooling equipment, etc.
- Establishing effective linkages, partnerships, and best practices with business partners and internal functions.
- Investigating and incorporating industry benchmarks, metrics and best practices to develop optimal supply chains for suppliers and/or product sets.
- Developing and analyzing data and information to identify and prioritize opportunities for improving performance and creating more cost-effective supply chains, inventory utilization, and business partnerships.


**Requirements:**

- Bachelor's degree or above, Mechanical or Automation Major is a plus.
- 3-5 years of experience in warehouse optimization and material replenishment planning.
- Experience in intelligent warehouse development, such as AGV, Vertical warehouse, automated supply chain is a plus.
- Digital Skills: Familiar with Excel, Power Bi, Power Apps, etc.
- Good communication ability in English is preferred.

**Additional information**

The position requires the candidate to work a 5-day-a-week schedule in the office.

## Description

**Career Area:**

Engineering

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**感知算法工程师（矿山无人驾驶方向）**

**岗位职责：**

设计并优化矿山无人驾驶场景下的多传感器融合感知系统，包括激光雷达、摄像头、毫米波雷达等，实现高精度环境建模与目标识别。 开发并迭代感知检测与融合算法，如BEV感知、Occupancy Grid、语义分割、目标跟踪等，提升系统在复杂地形和极端天气下的鲁棒性。 针对矿区扬尘、雨雪、夜间低光等特殊工况，研发抗干扰与冗余机制，保障感知系统稳定运行。 在车规级嵌入式平台（如 NVIDIA Orin）上部署感知算法，优化计算效率与资源利用，实现算法的工程化落地。 与硬件、控制、系统集成等团队协同工作，参与从传感器选型、标定到整车部署的全流程，确保感知模块与整车系统的高效协同。

**任职要求：**

硕士及以上学历，计算机、电子工程、自动化、人工智能等相关专业。 3年以上自动驾驶或机器人领域感知算法研发经验，具备完整项目开发与部署经历。 有矿山无人驾驶感知应用经验，熟悉矿山场景无人驾驶感知准确性以及相关处理方法， 了解矿山应用感知算法的缺陷，协助规划以及控制提供合适的方案 熟练掌握C++、Python编程，具备良好的算法实现与调试能力。 熟悉激光雷达点云处理、图像识别、毫米波雷达数据解析，了解多传感器融合技术。 熟悉激光雷达深度学习算法，了解激光雷达的算法模型，能够独立完成基于激光雷达点云的物体识别，距离检测，物体大小以及姿态测量的深度学习部署。 理解主流感知算法原理，如目标检测、语义分割、BEV建模、Transformer网络等，并具备实际应用经验。 具备良好的问题分析和解决能力，以及团队协作与沟通能力。

**加分项：**

- 有矿山、港口、物流等工业场景的无人驾驶项目经验。
- 有乘用车或商用车自动驾驶感知系统研发经验，了解从硬件选型到算法部署的完整流程。
- 熟悉Apollo、Autoware等开源自动驾驶平台。
- 具备嵌入式平台部署与优化经验，熟悉车规级硬件环境。

**Posting Dates:**

January 30, 2026 - February 19, 2026

## Description

**Career Area:**

Sales

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Join our dynamic team as a **Senior Product Consultant,** where you’ll be at the forefront of innovation in Medium Wheel Loaders.

In this exciting role, you’ll become the Medium Wheel Loader lead for Caterpillar Customer Solutions Core Division, driving the New Product Introduction (NPI) functional requirements to the Global Product Group (Earthmoving Division) ensuring we meet our customers’ needs in North America, Europe, Japan, Australia, New Zealand and Pacific Islands.

You’ll work closely with customers globally, regional Sales teams, and the Earthmoving Division’s New Product Introduction Manager and Vice President Product Management, gathering insights, shaping functional specifications, and defining critical requirements for Caterpillar Medium Wheel Loader products.

If you’re passionate about Caterpillar Medium Wheel Loaders and eager to make a global impact, this role offers an unparalleled opportunity to lead, innovate, and excel! Join us and be part of a team that’s revolutionizing the industry!

**What You Will Do:**

- Deeply understand customers to clearly define customer problems & package tailored lifecycle solutions to solve them. Develop recommendations into Product Group MGPP & strategy development.

- Develop and own go-to-market strategy and scalable messaging strategy.

- Develop and present Caterpillar Customer Solutions input at all Product Group NPI Gateway reviews.

- Provide unmet customer needs and functional requirements to Product Group.

- Act as a key subject matter expert across North America, Europe, Japan, Australia, New Zealand and Pacific Islands.

- Travel up to 50% required.


**What You Have (Core Skills):**

- **Technical Excellence:** Deep expertise in Medium Wheel Loaders or similar heavy equipment, developing global customer requirements and specifications for New Product Introduction, with a track record in competitive performance studies, value analysis, and cost analysis, translating customer pain points into actionable recommendations. Must clearly demonstrate the ability to understand and articulate technical machine performance and application knowledge that allows you to be regarded as an "expert" in product application and specifically for Medium Wheel Loaders.

- **Products & Services:** Ability to bridge customer needs and product vision, identifying portfolio gaps and analyzing trends and demand, performing complex analyses for strategic planning, and communicating customer insights for strategic decision.

- **Effective Communications:** Exceptional communication skills to convey insights to global teams and executives, preparing and presenting detailed reports for New Product Introduction approvals, developing global training programs, and mentoring less experienced personnel.

- **Customer Focus:** Exceptional customer-back focus in order to develop true customer insights and demonstrate a sense of urgency in their customer back requirements work.


**Top Candidates Will Also Have:**

- Degree in business or engineering.

- Field Rep experience and global work experience.

- Strong ability to navigate and communicate across matrix organization to ensure seamless collaboration and alignment.

- Familiarity with heavy machinery or other similar industries, providing valuable context for immediate contribution.


**Summary Pay Range:**

$147,760.00 - $221,640.00

Compensation and benefits offered may vary depending on multiple individualized factors, job level, market location, job-related knowledge, skills, individual performance and experience. Please note that salary is only one component of total compensation at Caterpillar.

**Benefits:**

Subject to plan eligibility, terms, and guidelines. This is a summary list of benefits.

- Medical, dental, and vision benefits\*

- Paid time off plan (Vacation, Holidays, Volunteer, etc.)\*

- 401(k) savings plans\*

- Tuition Reimbursement


\\* These benefits also apply to part-time employees

This position requires working onsite five days a week.

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Unser Team ist unser stärkster Erfolgsfaktor**

Die Caterpillar Energy Solutions GmbH steht mit ihren beiden Marken MWM und CAT für hoch­effiziente und umweltfreundliche Anlagen zur dezentralen Energie­erzeugung. Das in Mannheim ansässige Unternehmen wurde im Jahre 1871 vom Autopionier Carl Benz gegründet. Die Produkte stehen für mehr als 150 Jahre Erfahrung in der Entwicklung und Optimierung von Gasmotoren und Stromaggregaten für Erdgas, Biogas und weitere Sondergase. Seit November 2011 ist unser Unternehmen Teil des Caterpillar-Konzerns.

Alle Caterpillar Energy Solutions Standorte sind nach ISO 9001:2015 sowie 5001:2018 zertifiziert. Die Umsetzung der entsprechenden Leitlinien verstehen wir nicht nur als „Nice-to-have“, sondern vielmehr als Anforderung unserer Kunden. Darüber hinaus setzen wir eigene Richtlinien als strategisches Werkzeug ein, um Mitarbeiter, Produkte und Dienstleistungen stetig weiterzuentwickeln.

Wir wachsen weiter und suchen zur Verstärkung unsers Team im Bereich **„Qualität / Integrierte Managementsysteme“** mehrere engagierte

### **Quality Engineers (IMS) / Qualitätsingenieure Maschinenbau (m/w/d)**

**Einsatzort:** 68167 Mannheim

**Ihre Aufgaben bei uns im Team:**

- ### Weiterentwicklung und Pflege der integrierten Managementsysteme (ISO 9001, ISO 50001, ggf. weitere Normen).

- ### Selbständige Durchführung interner und externer Audits sowie Vorbereitung von Zertifizierungen.

- ### Analyse bestehender Prozesse und Ableitung von Verbesserungsmaßnahmen zur Effizienzsteigerung.

- ### Erstellung und Aktualisierung von Richtlinien, Standards und Prozessdokumentationen.

- ### Beratung von Fachbereichen und Führungskräften zu Normen, gesetzlichen Anforderungen und internen Richtlinien.

- ### Entwicklung und Pflege einer Kennzahlenlandschaft für regelmäßige Berichterstattung an die Leitung.

- ### Leitung bereichsübergreifender Projekte und Steuerung von Maßnahmen zur Prozessoptimierung.

- ### Koordination von Teamaktivitäten und Sicherstellung einer effizienten Zusammenarbeit innerhalb des IMS-Bereichs.

- ### Übernahme einer Schlüsselrolle bei Projekten und Unterstützung des Managements bei strategischen Entscheidungen.

- ### Beobachtung gesetzlicher Änderungen und Ableitung notwendiger Anpassungen.


**Das zeichnet Sie aus:**

- ### Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Qualitäts-, Energie-, Umwelt- oder Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen oder vergleichbare Qualifikation.

- ### Fundierte Kenntnisse der Normen ISO 9001 und ISO 50001; Auditorenqualifikation von Vorteil.

- ### Erfahrung in der Analyse von Prozessen und Umsetzung von Verbesserungsmaßnahmen.

- ### Sicherer Umgang mit MS Office; Kenntnisse in Managementsystem-Software wünschenswert.

- ### Sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift, Niveau B1/B2 erforderlich.

- ### Ausgeprägte Führungs- und Kommunikationsfähigkeiten, Organisationstalent und die Bereitschaft, Verantwortung zu übernehmen.

- ### Analytische Fähigkeiten sowie strukturierte und selbstständige Arbeitsweise.


**Ihre Benefits – was wir Ihnen bieten:**

- **Abwechslungsreiche und herausfordernde Aufgaben**

- **Attraktives Vergütungssystem / AT-System / Bonussystem**

- **30 Tage Urlaub**

- **Flexibles Arbeitszeitmodell / Gleitzeitmodell für außertarifliche Mitarbeiter**

- **Betriebliche Altersvorsorge**

- **Fahrrad-Leasing / E-Bike**

- **Professionelles Onboarding**

## Description

**Career Area:**

Product Support

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

We are looking for a Minestar Application Specialist to join our team. If you have experience in technology or mining and thrive on solving complex problems and creating innovative solutions, we want to hear from you!

Your job is to help keep a fleet of CAT haul trucks running smoothly using the Autonomous Haulage System (AHS) with Cat MineStar™. You’ll be part of the team making sure these trucks stay on track and get the job done safely and efficiently.

This role will be based in **Ontario**, working with a key customer at the **Côté Gold Mine**. Your roster will align with the customer's schedule and may adjust based on the project you're focused on.

**Why This Job is Awesome:**

- **Innovative Environment:** Be at the forefront of mining technology, working with a team that is changing the way mining is done.

- **Global Impact:** Work alongside the world’s leading mining companies and make a significant impact on the industry.

- **Career Growth:** Enjoy a robust training program and numerous career advancement opportunities within a global company.

- **Work-Life Balance:** Experience a role that offers flexibility and aligns with your personal and professional life.


**What You Will Do:**

**Maximize Performance**: Implement and optimize Autonomous Haulage System performance.

**Lead Safety Initiatives:** Be a safety leader within Command for Hauling operations at the mine site.

**Drive Continuous Improvement:** Work on projects to ensure continuous improvement for mining operations within MineStar®.

**Collaborate and Advise:** Become a trusted advisor for the customer through close collaboration internally and externally.

**Support and Innovate:** Provide technical support and advise autonomous haulage system Supervisors, Builders, Controllers, and Pit Technicians in all aspects of MineStar® performance.

**Ensure Quality:** Support hardware and software installation and configuration through establishing Quality Assurance and Quality Control processes.

**Measure and Improve:** Measure, benchmark, and report on MineStar® Command System performance, and suggest opportunities for improvements.

**Develop Best Practices:** Develop best practices and improve processes related to autonomous mining dispatch systems.

**Tailored Training:** Benefit from a training program tailored to your needs, including classroom training, on-the-job training, and mentoring.

**What Skills You Will Have:**

**Technical Excellence:** **Experience with** **mining technology products** and various application methods; ability to develop and provide solutions to significant technical challenges.

**Field Support:** Experience in providing post-sales support and maintaining hardware products.

**Problem Solving:** Ability to recognize, anticipate, and resolve organizational, operational, or process problems effectively.

**Communication Skills:** Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to work autonomously as well as part of a complex team.

**Analytical Skills:** Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong attention to detail and change management skills.

**Adaptability:** Ability to multi-task and thrive in a fast-paced environment.

**Top Candidates Will Also Have:**

Knowledge of mining technology products and product applications, including Fleet Management systems.

**Additional Information:**

This position is based at the **Côté Gold Mine** site in Ontario **.**

Transportation is provided via bus from either Sudbury, ON or Timmins, ON.

While on shift, employees are provided with housing and meals.

**Most MineStar Application Specialists work 7x7 day and night shift roster.**

Domestic relocation assistance to Sudbury, ON is available.

Sponsorship is not available.

**What you will get:**

Our goal at Caterpillar is for you to have a rewarding career. Our teams are critical to the success of our customers who build a better world.

Here you earn more than just wage, because we value your performance, we offer a total rewards package that provides:

- Competitive Base Salary

- Annual incentive bonus plan\*

- Medical, dental, and vision coverage

- Paid time off plan (Vacation, Holiday, Volunteer, Etc.)

- Defined Contribution Pension Plan

- Employee Share Purchase Plan

- Short and long-term disability coverage

- Life Insurance

- Employee Assistance Programs

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Caterpillar is embarking on a **bold digital transformation to revolutionize our dealers’ systems** \- seamless, intelligent, and designed for the future. We are developing a scalable, modern ERP solution for Caterpillar dealers, built on Microsoft Dynamics 365 and Azure Integration Services. This next-generation platform will leverage pre-configured business processes, optimized workflows and AI-powered automation to streamline operations, accelerate digital capability deployment, and elevate customer experience across our global dealer network.

We are seeking a delivery-focused **Regional Deployment Manager** **(APD)** to lead a team of Deployment Consultants supporting Caterpillar Dealers and their selected System Integrator to deploy our Dealer ERP Template and the Annual Deployment Plan for Dealers with in the Asia Pacific region. This high-impact role is part of a newly launched strategic initiative and will serve as a delivery orchestrator, aligning teams to program objectives, managing risks and dependencies, and ensuring consistent, high-quality outcomes through scaled Agile practices. The Annual Deployment Plan supports the selected required and pilot deployments for all dealers, this role will lead Deployment Consultants that will work with dealer to gain commitment to deliver requested deployments and liaison with dealers oversee the successful deployment completions on time.

**The Regional Deployment Manager Will Do:**

- Managing the day-to-day activities of existing Caterpillar Deployment consultants and their ongoing Dealer Deployment projects both for **ERP Cat Dealer Template** and **Annual Deployment Plan (ADP),** including overseeing project responsibilities and schedules. Developing contingency plans for potential risks.


- Organizing and coordinating teams who work on diverse aspects of digital programs around the Caterpillar Dealership.


- Carry “Voice of the Dealer” into the Product and Engineering team for future deployment and dealer needs on MS Dynamics 365, and to Caterpillar Product owners for other Caterpillar Deployment Projects within the Annual Deployment Plan (ADP)


- Serving as an collaborative leader and liaison, developing deep working relationships between Caterpillar Dealers, System Integrators and Caterpillar product and engineering teams and Caterpillar DSD and Digital leaders.


- Facilitating meetings and discussions with Caterpillar Dealer, Cat Digital & DSD leaders to share continual update and full transparency of status on deployment projects for ERP & ADP.


- Lead and mentor existing team members to foster a high-performing Agile culture


- Identifying, escalating, and tracking risks, issues and cross-team dependencies so deployment projects run to committed time schedules and expected quality.


- Managing the Azure DevOps environment to support efficient deployment projects and accurate recording of Caterpillar Dealer Deployment needs for success project completion.


- Be a strong deployment SME voice in regular product and steering committee meetings and stakeholder events for the Cat Dealer ERP Template and other Cat Digital Products.


- Give strategic leadership to your deployment team and set working priorities.


- Achieve Enterprise set Deployment targets for each calendar year.


- Develop existing teams talent and skillsets in MS Dynamics 365 and supporting deployment tools such as Mavim, Leapworks, etc.


- Manage team T&E budget.


- Limited Travel required. (Up to 25% of working time)


​

- **ERP & Systems Knowledge** **(MS Dynamics 365 focus)**


- Hands-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 in a product, process or deployment leadership role.


- Understanding of ERP configuration, security, and integrations with related systems.


- Awareness of Microsoft release cycles and ability to assess new features for business value.


- Knowledge of System product lifecycle management, including adoption and change management.

**Job Summary:**

As a member of Cat Technologies Connectivity team this role will be the point-of-contact to support dealers and regional representatives in our efforts to upgrade equipment with the latest technology.

**What You Will Do:**

- Overseeing the resolution of connectivity program issues and managing dashboard requirements.
- Manage every stage of Program issue resolution, often coupled with connectivity dashboard enhancements, from early ideas through ongoing updates. This includes collecting data from various stakeholders, the analytics team, and platform owners to establish robust connectivity solutions.
- Gathers and implements feedback from Dealers, Regional Representatives, and Industry Product Groups, applying corrective actions and updates to dashboard insights as needed.
- Lead and participate in workshops to collect user requirements and turn them into detailed functional specifications, ensuring technical features support vital business needs.
- Serve as the main contact between technical teams and stakeholders. Present program and dashboard solutions to business leaders and obtain formal approvals to ensure solutions meet strategic goals.
- Root cause analysis, change requests, and effective communication are utilized to ensure high-quality outcomes.
- Deliver timely and robust solutions while maintaining organizational trust and quality standards.

**What You Will Have:**

- **Planning: Tactical, Strategic:** Knowledge of effective planning techniques and ability to contribute to operational (short term), tactical (1-2 years) and strategic (3-5 years) planning in support of the overall business plan.
- **Decision Making and Critical Thinking:** Knowledge of the decision-making process and associated tools and techniques; ability to accurately analyze situations and reach productive decisions based on informed judgment.
- **Data Gathering and Reporting:** Knowledge of tools, techniques and processes for gathering and reporting data; ability to practice them in a particular department or division of a company.
- **Implementation:** Knowledge of how to run applications for organizations; ability to implement application software within an organization and help end-users perform specific tasks (ex: accounting or graphic design).

**Consideration For Top Candidates:**

- Experience with BI platforms like Power BI, Tableau, or Grafana to build dashboards.
- Understanding of systems integration with the ability to identify cause issues
- Experience working with Caterpillar dealers
- Ability to collaborate with internal and external business partners
- Strong communication skills
- Understanding of APIs and programming languages such as SQL or Python (helpful)

**Additional Details:**

- This position requires the candidate to work full-time at the Mossville, IL office.
- Domestic relocation assistance is available for this position.

We don’t just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Our **Product Support and Logistics Division (PSLD)** is one of the most innovative divisions within Caterpillar. In PSLD, as within all of Caterpillar, our priority is our customers and helping them build a better world. We touch nearly every element of the services value stream. PSLD is responsible for Aftermarket Parts Distribution of Cat Parts, Integrated Logistics and Global Services supporting Caterpillar dealers and customers. Our teams work together in critical areas that leverage digital capabilities, technology and operational excellence.

As an **Operations Automation Technician**, you will support multiple robotic and automated systems and leads projects for the operations management team.

**Additional Information:**

- **This role is located in Morton, IL and does not offer relocation**
- **This role is 100% on-site with no opportunities for hybrid or remote schedules**
- **This role does not currently require travel**

**What You Will Do:**

- Program, debug and troubleshoot robotic and automated systems; support or lead efforts to deploy automated systems.

- Partner with support teams on application and/or software troubleshooting. Operational owner of ticket resolution and communication to stakeholders.

- Train end users and operations support teams on the automated system applications. Own local operation application support. Support the execution of standards.


**What You Have (Basic Requirements):**

- **Industrial Automation/Automatic Control:** Knowledge of various industrial automatic control systems, processes and methodologies in a manufacturing environment; ability to design, implement and maintain automatic production systems/processes to improve productivity and efficiency, as well as minimize human intervention.

- **Problem Solving:** Knowledge of approaches, tools, techniques for recognizing, anticipating, and resolving organizational, operational or process problems; ability to apply knowledge of problem solving appropriately to diverse situations.

- **Technical Troubleshooting:** Knowledge of technical troubleshooting approaches, tools, and techniques; ability to anticipate, recognize, and resolve technical issues on hardware, software, application, or operation.

- Engineering/Technical Degree or Equivalent Experience
- Demonstrated success in implementing warehouse operations improvement via new technology and processes
- Prior experience as an industrial electrician
- Programmable Logic Controls (PLC) experience
- Experience with robotics, automated retrieval systems, and/or self-driving vehicles

Caterpillar Inc. is the world’s leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines and diesel-electric locomotives. For nearly 100 years, we’ve been helping customers build a better, more sustainable world and are committed and contributing to a reduced-carbon future. At Caterpillar, we work to help our customers build a better world. Without our team of talented, bright and driven individuals, we wouldn’t be the Caterpillar we are today. Now, this is your chance to join our team and do work that matters. We want you to help us enable customer success, make progress possible around the world and help our communities grow and thrive. We are looking for a dynamic, organized self-starter to join our Cat Technology Division as a **Senior Telematics Component Engineering Specialist in Mossville, IL.**

**Cat Technology** collaborates with partners across the Caterpillar enterprise to advance technologies including autonomy and automation, which helps enhance site safety, productivity and efficiency across customer operations worldwide. Cat Technology also owns enterprise-wide initiatives in innovation, research and development, manufacturing automation and advanced materials, ensuring Caterpillar continues to lead the industry in technological excellence and engineering innovation.

We’re **committed** to our customers, who build a better world with our products, services and solutions. We understand and show the value of why they should always choose us. We’re exploring new ideas and opportunities – **innovating** to discover the breakthroughs necessary for tomorrow’s growth.

**Job Summary** **:**

- This **Senior Telematics Component Engineering Specialist** will design, develop, and test electrical and electronic components for use within machines and engine systems, with a specialized focus on wireless communications technologies and systems.

**Additional Info**:

- This role is located in Mossville, IL
- **Domestic Relocation Assistance Offered**: Yes
- This role requires up to 10% domestic/international travel

**What You Will Do:**

- Lead the design and development of wireless communication components, including radio wave propagation analysis, bandwidth and frequency constraint management, and signal collection strategies.
- Oversee compliance with development processes and regulations for electrical, electronic, and wireless systems and components.
- Investigate and troubleshoot technical issues related to communications systems, circuits, devices, and microcontroller-based platforms.
- Manage and negotiate with vendors for the development of wireless and electronic systems and components.
- Mentor technicians and engineers in the development of wireless communication systems and electronic components.
- Utilize advanced simulation and design tools such as MATLAB (RF/Comms Toolbox), ANSYS HFSS, and CAD software for product design and analysis.
- Develop and test electronic circuits and devices, including analog and digital designs, with consideration for wireless signal integrity and reliability.
- Apply programming skills in Python and C++ for embedded systems, signal processing, and automation tasks.
- Guide reliability management efforts, utilizing statistical measures and evaluation metrics to ensure hardware product performance in wireless environments.
- Direct troubleshooting efforts for complex communications and integration problems in multi-vendor and multi-technology environments.

**What You Have (Required Skills):**

- Degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Materials Science, or a related accredited curriculum. Or equivalent experience.
- Project Management experience
- **Technical Excellence:** Hands‑on proficiency using MATLAB (RF/Comms Toolbox), ANSYS HFSS, and/or Linux OS, and scripting languages such as Python or C++ to support hardware characterization, signal analysis, and system‑level troubleshooting. Skilled in using these tools to validate RF performance, analyze test data, and diagnose hardware issues rather than focusing on software development.
- **Troubleshooting Technical Problems**: Experience with hardware. Strong background working directly with microcontrollers, embedded platforms, and/or electronic subsystems, including board‑level bring‑up and interface debugging. Experienced in evaluating and troubleshooting wireless technologies—Bluetooth, satellite, GNSS, and cellular—through hands‑on measurement, protocol analysis, and RF test equipment operation.
- **Effective Communication**: Understanding of effective communication concepts

Caterpillar es el principal fabricante del mundo de equipo de minería y construcción, motores diésel y a gas natural, turbinas industriales a gas y locomotoras diésel y eléctricas. Somos líderes y estamos orgullosos de tener la mayor presencia a nivel mundial en las industrias donde ofrecemos nuestros productos y servicios.

Desde hace casi 100 años, ayudamos a nuestros clientes a construir un mundo mejor.

**Descripción**

El titular del puesto es responsable del armado de piezas metálicas y su unión mediante la aplicación de soldaduras utilizando el proceso de soldadura por arco, así como el seguimiento de procedimientos de soldadura y trabajo estándar además del aseguramiento de la calidad mediante la inspección de soldaduras terminadas. Siguiendo al 100% el trabajo estándar según la estación asignada, además asegurar los comportamientos descritos en los mandamientos de seguridad del área.

- Capacidad para trabajar con gran exactitud.
- Capacidad de concentrarse en el trabajo de soldadura durante mucho tiempo seguido.
- Capaz de entender diagramas y dibujos técnicos.
- Ser capaz de seguir instrucciones técnicas con exactitud y de seguir los procedimientos de seguridad en todo momento.
- Buena coordinación.

No se requiere experiencia.

Disponibilidad de rolar turnos.

Tiempo completo.

**Detalles de beneficios para empleados**

Nuestro objetivo en Caterpillar es que usted tenga una carrera gratificante. Nuestros equipos son fundamentales para el éxito de nuestros clientes.

Aquí gana más que solo un salario por hora, porque valoramos su desempeño, ofrecemos un paquete de recompensas total que brinda beneficios desde el primer día (transporte, comedor, servicio médico interno, utilidades, uniformes, vacaciones, aguinaldo, IMSS, etc.) junto con el potencial de un bono mensual.

**Datos finales**

Por favor, compruebe con frecuencia el correo electrónico asociado a su solicitud, incluyendo la carpeta de correo no deseado, ya que este es el principal método de correspondencia. Si desea conocer el estado de su solicitud, utilice el inicio de sesión del candidato en nuestro sitio web de carreras, ya que reflejará cualquier actualización de su estado.

## Description

**Career Area:**

Enterprise Cross Functional

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**【契約社員】キャタピラー(有期契約)：油圧ショベル等の建設機械の溶接工／溶接経験者大歓迎！資格不問、充実の福利厚生(社食200円等)**

### 仕事内容

### ◆溶接スタッフの仕事内容◆ キャタピラー明石工場での、ショベルカー等の建設機械の製造・溶接スタッフ。 配属先のラインや作業内容により部品点数や工具の大きさ等の異なる点はありますが、ダイナミックな仕事までさまざまな作業にチャレンジ可能です！ ＜具体的なお仕事内容例＞ ○ 加工後の材料同士の接合(目的に合わせ、半自動溶接、アーク溶接、ガス溶接などの手法を使い分けています) ○ 厚板溶接（20ミリ程度の厚さの溶接を行うことは珍しく、高い技術が身につけられます) ○ グラインダーでの表面成形、 ○ 部材の運搬 など 年齢層も幅広く、女性社員も活躍している職場です。 それぞれの作業場にて先輩作業者（指導員）の指導を受けながら、仕事を覚えて頂きます。 子ども達にも人気の働く車を一緒に作りませんか？ ※有期契約社員：半年ごとに勤務態度等に基づいて更新 / 最長５年

### 求める人材

### 溶接経験者、大歓迎！！ ＜以下の資格・経験がある方は活かせます！＞ ・製造業や工場勤務などの経験 ・中板の半自動溶接作業の経験 ・床上操作式クレーン運転技能・玉掛技能・ガス溶接技能 ＜性別・学歴は問いません！＞ ＊10代から20代・30代・40代中心に幅広い年代が活躍しています！ ＊女性多数活躍中！ ＊建設現場や復興現場等で製品が活躍している風景(映像)を見て友人･家族に自慢できます モノ作りに興味のある方・好きな方はもちろん、様々な方にご活躍いただける環境です。 ・技能・知識を身につけて活躍したい方 ・元気で前向きな方 ・働く車が好きな方 ・安定した環境で働きたい方 ・技能・知識を身につけて活躍したい方 ・コツコツモクモク作業がすきな方 など

### **給与**

### 時給 1,755円 〜 2,255円

### ＋特殊作業手当(溶接)あり…月2,600円

### ＋皆勤手当あり…無遅刻・無欠勤で月３万円

### ＋満期慰労金あり…6カ月ごとに20万円（欠勤６日以内）

### ＋残業手当あり

### ＋夜勤手当あり

### ＊契約期間内の頑張り次第で契約更新毎の時給UPのチャンスあり！

### ＜月収・年収例＞

### 月収：41万９千～51万5千円 （時給1,755～2,255円×8h×20日＋特殊作業手当２万６千＋皆勤手当３万円＋残業手当+夜勤手当）

### 年収：542万９千～657万７千円 （月収×12ヵ月＋満期慰労金20万円×２回）

### ※月残業15時間・夜勤10日とした場合 ※四捨五入

### **勤務時間**

### 勤務形態：シフト制

### ＜勤務時間＞ 日勤 8:30-17:30 (休憩１時間)

### \*夜勤ありシフトの場合：夜勤 20:00 - 翌５:00 (休憩１時間)

### ＜月平均残業時間＞ 15時間前後（昨年実績）

### 契約更新期間：有期契約社員：半年ごとに勤務態度等に基づいて更新 / 最長５年

### **休日休暇制度**

### 休日休暇制度  2025年年間休日126日／※別途、有休等あり

### ＜各種休暇(有給)＞  ・有給休暇（6ヶ月経過後10日付与、以降6ヶ月毎に付与日数増）  ・傷病休暇（6ヶ月毎に５日付与）  ・安心サポート休暇（6ヶ月毎に５日付与、育児・介護休業等取得時に充当可)

### ＜休日＞  ・土日祝(※)  ・夏季休日  ・年末年始休日 (12/30-1/4)  ・ゴールデンウイーク休日  ・メーデー休日(5/1)  ※但し、会社カレンダーによる  ※ゴールデンウイークやお盆に長期連休あり（昨年実績より）

### **社会保険／福利厚生**

### 【社会保険】  健康保険、厚生年金保険、雇用保険、労災保険

### 【福利厚生】  ■交通費全額支給  ■社会保険完備  ■有給休暇  ■残業代全額支給  ■制服貸与・ヘルメット貸与  ■髪型・髪色自由  ■工場内社員食堂（昼食補助で1食200円に）  ■お弁当持参ももちろんOKな風土  ■休憩スペースやお昼を食べる場所あり   ■無料送迎バス（電車通勤の方用）  ■車・バイク・自転車通勤可  ■駐車場・駐輪場あり  ■化粧室は男女別（シャワートイレあり）  ■更衣所は男女別（鍵付き個人ロッカー完備）  L一部更衣室にシャワー施設あり  ■充実した福利厚生施設  L会社敷地内にコンビニエンスストア（ローソン）あり  Lフィットネスルーム・体育館・テニスコート  L従業員専用レストラン

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

Are you looking for an opportunity to learn a specialized skill within a manufacturing environment? Caterpillar Building KK in East Peoria, IL is looking to train new employees that desire to become our next employees in Heat Treat.

The **Metal Treating Specialist Trainee** program is a paid program designed to provide a unique learning experience for highly motivated individuals who are interested in pursuing a career in manufacturing and learning new skills. Positions may be available on all shifts after training on 1st shift for 4-6 weeks at the facility. Upon completion of the program, individuals will be responsible for:

**What You Will Do (including but not limited to)**

- Set up, operation and adjustment heat-treat equipment to perform carburizing, cycle annealing and die quenching of gears Physical Qualifications (With or Without Accommodation)

- Will work in environment that has extreme temperatures

- Work in environment with frequently changing temperatures

- Stand for a minimum of 8 hour shifts

- Frequent walking, bending, reaching

- Occasional stooping, crouching, and climbing

- Repetitive grasping, pushing, pulling rotating


**Degree Requirement**

- High school diploma or previous work/volunteer experience


**Basic Qualifications**

- High school diploma or previous work/volunteer experience

- Knowledge of manufacturing processes and procedures

- Basic computer skills

- Experience in a manufacturing environment

- Ability to work overtime when required, including weekends


**Top Candidates will also have**

- Previous heat treat experience

- Previous machine shop experience

- Strong commitment to safety at all times

- Excellent attitude and willingness to learn


**Additional Information**

- Pay is $23.22 hr

- Positions are available for 2nd & 3rd shift in East Peoria Bldg. KK

- **This is a supplemental position – minimum 40 hours per week with limited benefits available**

- Relocation is not offered for this position

- This position requires no travel.


Caterpillar is not currently hiring foreign national applicants that require or will require sponsorship tied to a specific employer, such as H, L, TN, F, J, E, O. ## Description

**Career Area:**

Finance

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Job Description Summary**

The Financial Systems Governance Lead – Legal Entity Reporting plays a critical role in shaping Caterpillar’s financial reporting processes through effective governance and system design, primarily focused on SAP and related ERP platforms. This role emphasizes system architecture, integration, process governance, and compliance, ensuring alignment with Caterpillar’s global standards and transformation initiatives.

As part of the Caterpillar IT Corporate Services team, this position is responsible for defining and maintaining scalable, standardized, and sustainable system designs supporting Legal Entity Reporting and other key financial processes. The role requires close collaboration with Corporate Consolidations and business unit stakeholders to align business needs with system capabilities.

**What You Will Do:**

- Act as the primary liaison between technology teams and business process owners, ensuring business needs are represented during system design and redesign.

- Serve as the subject matter expert for Legal Entity Reporting, providing guidance on system design and resolving process-related issues.

- Own the roadmap for CCR Light projects, including Service Center migrations, De-consolidation projects, M&A Activity - Acquisitions, Liquidations.

- Lead the execution of CCR Light deployments in close collaboration with the Financial Systems Delivery Team.

- Support M&A initiatives by onboarding acquired entities and integrating them into Caterpillar’s financial systems and processes.

- Contribute to the design phase of the Harmonizer Project, ensuring accuracy and integrity in financial reporting.

- Drive financial aspects of future SAP S/4 upgrade projects, including modernization of the CBS SAP system.

- Provide expertise on multiple ERP deployments and other projects, serving as a Foreign Currency SME.

- Establish and enforce technical standards, best practices, and internal control requirements, including SOX compliance.

- Govern change requests to ensure alignment with global standards and business objectives.

- Participate in onboarding projects by developing cost estimates (ROMs), timelines, and related deliverables.

- Partner with stakeholders—including Corporate Consolidations and business units—to gather requirements and translate them into scalable system designs.

- Offer strategic input on financial transformation initiatives and system enhancements.

- Communicate effectively with global stakeholders on financial systems governance and accounting-related matters.


**What You Have:**

- Experience in accounting or financial systems roles.

- Hands-on experience with SAP and other ERP platforms, including system design, integrations, and implementations.

- Strong understanding of finance transformation principles and practices.

- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or Business.

- Solid accounting knowledge and familiarity with core financial processes.

- Demonstrated leadership, communication, and business acumen.

- Ability to thrive in a global, matrixed environment and collaborate across diverse teams


**Additional Info**:

- The ideal location for this role is East Peoria – IL or Dallas – TX

- This position may require 10% travel.


**About Caterpillar -**

Caterpillar Inc. is the world’s leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, off-highway diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines and diesel-electric locomotives. For nearly 100 years, we’ve been helping customers build a better, more sustainable world and are committed and contributing to a reduced-carbon future. Our innovative products and services, backed by our global dealer network, provide exceptional value that helps customers succeed.

## Description

**Career Area:**

Sales

**Job Description:**

**Your Work Shapes the World at Caterpillar Inc.**

When you join Caterpillar, you're joining a global team who cares not just about the work we do – but also about each other. We are the makers, problem solvers, and future world builders who are creating stronger, more sustainable communities. We don't just talk about progress and innovation here – we make it happen, with our customers, where we work and live. Together, we are building a better world, so we can all enjoy living in it.

**Rejoignez une entreprise internationale à taille humaine, à Overijse !**

**Qui sommes-nous ?**

Cat Financial est la captive financière du groupe Caterpillar, leader mondial dans la fabrication d’équipements et de moteurs pour le BTP, l’industrie, les mines, et les secteurs de l’énergie. Depuis plus de 40 ans, nous accompagnons nos clients, concessionnaires et partenaires avec des solutions de financement sur mesure.

Présents dans plus de 40 pays, avec 2 600 collaborateurs et 36 milliards de dollars d’actifs gérés, nous avons pour mission de faciliter l’accès aux équipements Caterpillar grâce à des solutions de financement adaptés aux besoins de chaque client.

Nous opérons pour le groupe Caterpillar, en partenariat avec des concessionnaires exclusifs comme Bergerat Monnoyeur Belgique et Bergerat Monnoyeur France, tous deux membres du Groupe Monnoyeur.

Notre département commercial, basé principalement à Paris, regroupe :

\- 1 Sales Manager

\- 1 Responsable des Opérations commerciales

\- 7 Responsables commerciaux en région (6 en France et 1 en Belgique)

\- 4 Gestionnaires Commerciaux (3 pour la France, 1 pour la Belgique)

Nous gérons un portefeuille de 6 300 contrats, représentant 630 millions USD, et dans le cadre d’un remplacement, nous recrutons aujourd’hui un(e) Responsable Commercial(e) Régional / Territory Manager pour la Belgique.

**Votre mission principale :**

Par son action, vous participez à développer l’activité de Cat Financial Services Belgium sur son marché. Vous détectez les actions promotionnelles à mettre en place en collaboration avec les Concessionnaires et les Industries de Caterpillar.

**Vos taches principales :**

\- Promotion des ventes de matériels Caterpillar et des travaux de réparation par des solutions de financement adaptées, en liaison permanente avec l’équipe de vente et d’après-vente du concessionnaire et des partenaires

\- Proposition, négociation, instruction et montage les dossiers de leasing, renting et contrat d’ouverture de crédit

\- Fidélisation et développement la clientèle existante

\- Reporting régulier auprès de la Direction Commerciale

\- Force de proposition sur son secteur géographique et au sein de la structure

**Ce que nous attendons de vous :**

Orientation Client : Connaissance des valeurs et pratiques qui placent les besoins et la satisfaction du client comme considérations primordiales dans toutes les décisions commerciales, et capacité à exploiter ces informations pour créer des solutions personnalisées.

Connaissance du secteur : Connaissance du secteur d’activité de l’organisation, des tendances, orientations, enjeux majeurs, considérations réglementaires et acteurs influents ; capacité à appliquer ces connaissances de manière appropriée dans diverses situations.

Prise de décision et pensée critique : Connaissance du processus décisionnel et des outils et techniques associés ; capacité à analyser avec précision les situations et à prendre des décisions productives basées sur un jugement éclairé.

Communication efficace : Compréhension des concepts, outils et techniques de communication efficace ; capacité à transmettre, recevoir et interpréter correctement idées, informations et besoins grâce à des comportements de communication appropriés.

Négociation : Connaissance des concepts et techniques de négociation réussie ; capacité à négocier efficacement au sein de l’organisation et avec des fournisseurs et clients externes de manière constructive et collaborative.

Gestion des relations : Connaissance des techniques de gestion des relations ; capacité à établir et maintenir des relations de travail saines avec les clients, fournisseurs et pairs.

Gestion de compte : Connaissance de la gestion de compte ; capacité à gérer les activités quotidiennes, fournir des services et un support aux clients existants.

Vente basée sur la valeur : Connaissance des principes et pratiques de vente de produits, technologies et services ; capacité à fournir une « valeur » globale et à différencier les offres de support répondant aux besoins clairement identifiés des clients.

**Ce que nous recherchons chez vous**

**Obligatoire :**

\- **Bilingue Flamand / Néerlandais - français**

\- Une expérience, même courte, en banque ou dans une captive est également requise

\- Diplômés de l’enseignement supérieur, les candidats auront 5 années d’expériences professionnelles comprenant notamment une expérience commerciale réussie e

title: AI Engineer
raw_text:
# AI Engineer

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! Key Responsibilities · Platform Development and Evangelism: Build scalable AI platforms that are customer-facing. Evangelize the platform with customers and internal stakeholders. Ensure platform scalability, reliability, and performance to meet business needs. · Machine Learning Pipeline Design: Design ML pipelines for experiment management, model management, feature management, and model retraining. Implement A/B testing of models. Design APIs for model inferencing at scale. Proven expertise with MLflow, SageMaker, Vertex AI, and Azure AI. LLM Serving and GPU Architecture: Serve as an SME in LLM serving paradigms. Possess deep knowledge of GPU architectures. Expertise in distributed training and serving of large language models. Proficient in model and data parallel training using frameworks like DeepSpeed and service frameworks like vLLM. Model Fine-Tuning and Optimization: Demonstrate proven expertise in model fine-tuning and optimization techniques. Achieve better latencies and accuracies in model results. Reduce training and resource requirements for fine-tuning LLM and LVM models. LLM Models and Use Cases: Have extensive knowledge of different LLM models. Provide insights on the applicability of each model based on use cases. Proven experience in delivering end-to-end solutions from engineering to production for specific customer use cases. DevOps and LLMOps Proficiency: Proven expertise in DevOps and LLMOps practices. Knowledgeable in Kubernetes, Docker, and container orchestration. Deep understanding of LLM orchestration frameworks like Flowise, Langflow, and Langgraph. Communication & Articulation Ability to explain complex AI/ML topics and design choices to technical and business audiences. Experience in presenting AI strategies and results to senior executives, highlighting impact. Ability to lead cross-functional discussions to clarify issues and achieve engineering consensus. Ability to persuade stakeholders and secures support on solution approaches. Continuous Innovation & Adaptive Learning Proactively tracks emerging AI research, frameworks, and industry design patterns. Validates new concepts through quick experimentation and iterative "fail-fast" testing. Translates cutting-edge developments into practical improvements for production systems. Demonstrates a self-driven commitment to learning and adopting evolving AI technologies. Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law. Learn more. Adobe aims to make Adobe.com accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call (408) 536-3015.

title: Product Experience Manager
raw_text:
# Product Experience Manager

JOB LEVEL P50 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor At Adobe, we believe exceptional customer experiences are built through strong partnerships — and we build those bridges intentionally. As a Product Experience Manager, you’ll help us create consistent, reliable frameworks that connect Adobe Customer Solutions (ACS) teams with our Adobe Experience Cloud Product teams. ACS includes Customer Engineering (Support), Professional Services (Consulting), Ultimate Success (Technical Account Management, Field Engineering, Support Success Management), and Customer Success. Our Product teams include both Product Engineering and Product Management — and you’ll work across all of them to close the gaps that matter most. Your mission is to ensure customer pains, workflow friction, and improvement signals flow predictably across the organization. You translate these signals into clear product outcomes and high-quality customer-facing results. This is a strategic, cross-functional role where we rely on you to help the organization respond quickly to systemic issues, align on priorities, and keep teams moving in the same direction. Together, we’ll design and manage cross-departmental operating programs , build repeatable models, and ensure the right insights reach the right teams at the right time — all supported by a strong, closed-loop feedback process. If you love orchestrating clarity in complex spaces and asking, “How do we make this whole system work better?” — you’ll feel right at home here! KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Customer Understanding & Signal Flow We build deep empathy for customer workflows, friction points, and success criteria across enterprise journeys — and you’ll help us turn that understanding into action. Develop and reinforce a unified approach for collecting, triaging, and routing customer signals in partnership with Support, PS, Success, and field teams. Identify emerging themes, gaps, and patterns across break‑fix and feature improvement pathways to guide prioritization. Incorporate structured input from Beta participants, internal employees, customer champions, and external collaborators. Execution & Delivery Clearly articulate customer and internal workflow issues to Product and Engineering so teams can move quickly and effectively toward resolution. Partner with Engineering to ensure customer participation in Beta programs is meaningful and feedback loops remain complete. Drive execution rigor across multiple interconnected workstreams and cross-functional groups. Lead change management for new releases to ensure smooth internal and external adoption. Define success metrics, monitor performance post-launch, and highlight customer impact. Provide crisp, data-driven updates on progress, risks, issues, and mitigation strategies. Collaborator Management We depend on you to build bridges — strengthening alignment across Product, Engineering, Support, Professional Services, Customer Success, GTM, and Operations. Communicate roadmap implications, prioritization tradeoffs, and program status with clarity and consistency. Serve as the single point of accountability for the health, visibility, and end-to-end execution of assigned programs. Support leadership in pre-alignment conversations, ensuring regional and global teams stay informed and engaged. Measurement & Continuous Improvement Use data to diagnose issues, assess workflow health, and identify opportunities for improvement across teams. Implement tools, automation, and AI to boost efficiency, predictability, and transparency. Identify ways to standardize, scale, and operationalize repeatable operating models across ACS and Product teams. Lead retrospectives and translate findings into stronger processes and more impactful customer experiences! WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED Required Qualifications 10+ years in Product, Program, or Project Management supporting enterprise-scale, customer-facing solutions. Demonstrated success leading large, cross-functional, matrixed programs. Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including executive-level storytelling. Experience with program and workflow tools (e.g., Jira, ADO, Asana, Smartsheet, Confluence, Workfront). Proven ability to influence senior leaders and drive customer experience improvements. Dedicated, adaptable, and comfortable navigating ambiguity and change. Preferred Qualifications Experience in SaaS, Cloud, platform architecture, or enterprise technology environments. Familiarity with Agile, Scrum, or hybrid delivery models. Background in operational excellence, GTM readiness, or scaled program operations. Ability to influence without authority and build alignment across diverse teams. Knowledge of Adobe Digital Marketing solutions, especially their application and integration within enterprise environment Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $148,100 -- $282,100 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. ‌ In California, the pay range for this position is $194,800 - $282,100 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP). In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award.
