title: Senior Product Manager
raw_text:
# Senior Product Manager

JOB LEVEL P40 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor About the Developer Platform, Adobe Express & the Embed SDK The ​Developer Platform team's mission is to ensure Adobe’s creative products are built with extensibility in mind. Adobe Express is the quick and easy creative-anything app and platform for everyone from small businesses to enterprise teams to students and teachers. Built on this platform, the Adobe Express Embed SDK empowers partners and customers to seamlessly integrate Adobe’s generative and editing superpowers into their own applications, enabling users to create and publish content without leaving their workflow. This includes capabilities like AI-powered image generation, quick actions for media editing, and access to thousands of templates and assets. The Opportunity We are seeking a strategic and user-centric Senior Product Manager to lead the vision, roadmap, and execution of embeddable experiences within Adobe Express. This role is pivotal in shaping how Adobe delivers subscriber value by bringing creative tools into Adobe Acrobat, Creative Cloud Desktop, and Adobe’s AI Assistants. What You'll Do Define and Guide Product Strategy: Create and manage the product roadmap for the Embed SDK, concentrating on modules, quick actions, and full editor experiences. Cross-Functional Leadership: Collaborate with engineering, build, marketing, and partner teams to deliver seamless integration experiences. Partner Engagement: Work closely with internal partners to understand their ideal user experience, gather feedback, and ensure successful Embed module adoption. User-Centric Development: Advocate for end-users by conducting user research, analyzing feedback, and ensuring the product meets diverse user needs. Performance Monitoring: Establish benchmarks, monitor product performance, and iterate based on data-driven insights. Stay Ahead of Trends: Keep abreast of industry trends in web development, SDKs, and creative tools to inform product decisions. What You'll Bring Experience: 5+ years in product management, preferably with a focus on SDKs, APIs, or developer platforms. Technical Competence: Solid grasp of web technologies (JavaScript, HTML, CSS) and experience using developer tools. Critical Thinking: Proven ability to develop product strategies that align with business goals and user needs. Communication Skills: Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex concepts to diverse audiences. User Empathy: A deep understanding of user needs and a passion for delivering outstanding user experiences. Collaboration: Demonstrated success in leading cross-functional teams and managing collaborator relationships. Preferred Qualifications Education: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Engineering, Building, or a related field; MBA is a plus. Build Sensibility: Experience with design tools and an appreciation for user experience and visual design. Global Perspective: Experience collaborating with international teams and awareness of localization factors. # Machine Learning Engineer 3

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Opportunity Adobe is looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to understand and optimize the experience of Adobe’s Digital Experience customers. By partnering with Adobe Research and other business units, you will build products that transform how companies approach audience creation, journey optimization, and personalization at scale. Join a diverse, collaborative, and lively group of engineers and scientists well-established in the ML space. The work here is dynamic, fast-paced, creative, and data-driven. What you'll Do Develop innovative models in collaboration with Adobe Research. Design, develop, and deploy applications powered by predictive and generative models, with a focus on building autonomous agents and using agentic frameworks for adaptive decision-making. Implement ML-Ops best practices to ensure scalable, reliable, and efficient machine learning workflows. Engage in the product lifecycle, including architecture, design, deployment, and production operations. Understand data to make recommendations for the right predictive models, quality metrics, and governance approaches. What you need to succeed The ideal candidate will have the following background: MS in Computer Science, Data Science or Statistics with 3+ years of applied AI/ML experience, including developing, evaluating ML models, and deploying models into production or PhD degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field. Deep understanding of statistical modeling, machine learning, or analytics concepts, with a proven track record of solving problems using these methods. Experience in building large-scale data pipelines. Ability to quickly learn new skills and work in a fast-paced team. Proficiency in one or more programming languages such as Python, Scala, Java, or SQL. Proficiency in ML frameworks such as scikit-learn, SparkML, TensorFlow, or PyTorch. # Enterprise Architect - Retail Martech

JOB LEVEL P40 ADDITIONAL JOB LEVELS - EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor Job Description Role Description The Technical Pre-Sales Enterprise Architect (EA) serves as the primary technical driver for closing strategic sales opportunities and enabling customer success through marketing technology value realization. For Adobe’s Top 50 customers, the EA partners with technology, promotional, and executive-level contacts. They assess current-state technology and business objectives. They deliver future-state marketing architectures, manage digital engagement roadmaps, and showcase “Art of the Possible” experiences. Minimum Qualifications Past experience in a lead consultative role supporting large-scale enterprise digital marketing ecosystems as an Architect, Principal Consultant or Marketing/Technical leader. Working knowledge of end-to-end digital marketing MarTech, such as CDPs, Marketing Automation, omni-channel personalization, Journey Orchestration, Measurement and Optimization, Content Management, Content Generation, Digital Asset Management (DAM), Tag Management and Data Collection, Identity and Consent Management, CRM, Media Execution, Social Media Management, Reporting/Insights, and multi-channel activation. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! Job Description We're looking for a Creative Director, In-App Content, to coordinate the creation and delivery of high-quality, trend-aligned templates, effects, filters, transitions, and motion assets for Adobe Premiere on mobile. In this role, you'll define the visual style and lead the creative strategy for ongoing, regular content releases across global markets. You will set aesthetic standards to ensure quality, and offer mentorship to a distributed network of designers, animators, creators, and regional partners. What You’ll Do We will join forces to build a team of creatives and producers. Your direct responsibility will include the visual identity and motion language for in-app content that will be deployed in Adobe Premiere on mobile. We look for you to establish a high standard of craft and originality across templates, transitions, effects, titles, filters, and beyond. Content will be global and needs to reflect regional trends and cultural sensitivity. As Creative Director you will handle reviewing, refining, and approving all assets ahead of their release. As this is a new function, you'll be responsible for developing some of the content yourself while we build out the team. You'll join forces with our Lead Producer and trend analysis team to craft monthly themed content packs guided by cultural calendars, platform trends, and emerging aesthetics. You'll convert trend research into detailed creative briefs for both internal and external contributors. We expect you to develop style guides, creative guidelines, and documentation that help maintain content consistency with product and technical requirements on all applicable surfaces. Team Building & Collaboration As the creative lead for internal designers and a worldwide network of creatives, you will guide employees, freelancers, agencies, and regional teams daily. You will coach designers and animators to improve their skills and maintain consistently outstanding results. We will be working cross-functionally with engineering, product management, marketing, our community and social teams, finance, and more. Understanding how the team's work fits into the broader strategy and effectively communicating and working with others will be key to your success. What You’ll Bring 10+ years of experience in creative direction, motion design and animation, content creation, or related fields. A strong portfolio demonstrating elevated design aesthetic, motion graphics expertise, and brand-forward thinking. Experience editing video, creating motion graphic animations, and designing templates. Ability to guide both conceptual and production-level work across global teams. Solid understanding of social trends (TikTok, Instagram, YouTube Shorts) along with knowledge of cultural nuance for regional content packs. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. This role partners closely with Security, Engineering, Legal, Privacy, and business stakeholders to ensure Adobe’s technology platforms meet global security, regulatory, and customer trust expectations. You will lead enterprise-wide security assurance and compliance programs while building scalable, automated, and continuous assurance What you will do Join the Tech GRC leadership team under the leadership of the Sr. Director, Tech GRC, to drive technology compliance activities across Adobe. Drive APAC security compliance certifications including regional specific compliance programs such as ISMAP, IRAP, etc. and other industry standard audit efforts (SOC2, ISO, PCI, FedRAMP, etc.), while designing AI-enabled approaches to improve audit readiness, evidence collection, and control validation. Act as an AI-first visionary security compliance leader by finding ways to use AI to transform security assurance, compliance monitoring, and audit reporting. Do this through intelligent automation and predictive insights. Supervise the development, introduction, and carrying out of internal controls and processes guaranteeing ongoing conformity and risk management. Plan, outline, and review the design and performance of security controls during GRC reviews. Coordinate security assurance reviews across Adobe business units, third-party service providers, and cloud environments. Partner with the Security Automation Engineering team to build AI-enabled, automated GRC workflows that enable continuous control monitoring, real-time risk visibility, and proactive compliance management. Collaborate with various functions across security domains such as Vulnerability Management, Penetration Testing etc, building a unified working model for continuous assurance. Establish and apply standards and criteria based on recognized security compliance frameworks for cloud security and governance. Coordinate security assurance reviews involving fields like User Access Management, Network Operations, Encryption, among others. Assess business requirements to prioritize the implementation of internal controls according to relevant security frameworks. Help the team develop and communicate policies, procedures, guidelines, and plans to internal customers. Build a team of global security experts dedicated to developing and supporting company-wide security compliance capabilities. Collaborate with partners across multiple geographies for driving cross-functional projects related to security assurance. Develop goals aligned with business strategy and present status reports with value-based metrics to senior leadership for prioritization and decision-making. What You Need to Succeed Bachelor's or advanced degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. Minimum 10-12 years of experience in driving enterprise-level cybersecurity audits and security compliance efforts, with at least 3 years in a leadership role. Deep expertise in security compliance frameworks and privacy rules relevant to the APAC region (e.g., IRAP, ISMAP, KFSI, SOC 2, ISO 42001 etc.). Insight into how these affect cloud-native and AI-based systems. Experience In Managing And Leading A Team In A Fast-Paced And Dynamic Environment. Proficiency in essential organizational processes including SDLC, Identity/User Access Management, Backup and Disaster Recovery procedures, Vulnerability Management, System Monitoring, End Point Security, and Network Operations. Outstanding interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with both business and IT collaborators, including executive leadership. # Global Finance & Business Management Analyst Program

Deepen your understanding of the firm, while learning how we operate, generate revenue, and manage expenses when you join our global finance and business management team. You’ll support specific lines of business or our corporate function, working on projects that directly affect the firm’s bottom line alongside our top-tier professionals.

Because you’ll be analyzing results and providing insights to business leaders to help make decisions on how we do business, we welcome your perspectives from day one. You’ll combine sophisticated analysis methods, accurate reporting, and financial discipline to ensure the firm is capable of navigating all types of market environments, while maintaining a fortress balance sheet.

Applications are currently closed

## Apply now



Please note, only locations where this program is currently open will be displayed. If you're not able to find your desired location, please follow us on Facebook or Linkedin for program application updates.


## Inside the program

### What you'll do

While your responsibilities will vary based on the role, you’ll help generate meaningful analysis and reporting, coordinate and collaborate with partners across the firm on strategic initiatives, generate budgets and forecasts, and contribute to important projects across businesses. Start gaining the skills you need for a wide-ranging and rewarding career that can take you in any direction.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We are looking for highly motivated individuals with a passion for producing financial analyses to help drive strategic business decisions and the adaptability to work on many different types of projects. If you have excellent leadership, interpersonal, and communications skills and thrive in working in a collaborative, dynamic environment, then this is the role for you.

#### **Key skills**

You should have a basic understanding of financial statements, accounting procedures, internal and external reporting, and variance analysis. We’re looking for those with well-rounded academic backgrounds with a strong interest in financial management, data, and analytics. Those who are analytical, curious leaders with a passion for critical thinking and working as part of a community to shape the future will enjoy the many opportunities available here.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll be an active contributor to a best-in-class team working on business management, external reporting, financial analysis, financial controls, or project management. Your contributions will help drive business decisions.

#### **Training**

The program begins with hands-on training in finance and business management practices, accounting methods, and Excel, led by internal and external professionals. Your professional and technical skills development will continue throughout the program.

#### **Career progression**

title: Commercial Real Estate Summer Analyst | JPMorgan Chase & Co.
raw_text:
We and our partners use cookies and other tools for advertising, to help stop fraud, and for other purposes. By using this site, you agree to how your information is used as outlined in our [Privacy Policy](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/legal/privacy).

[![Close Icon](https://www.chase.com/etc/designs/chase-ux/css/img/icon_close.svg)](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/cre-summer#)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/cre-summer#main)

# Commercial Real Estate Program

Our Commercial Real Estate group works with developers, investors, owners, companies, investment funds, government agencies, community development financial institutions, and both for-profit and not-for-profit organizations to build the future of our communities.  Learn to build financial models, create credit approval memos, prepare marketing materials, gain an understanding of our products, and support a team of professionals working to shape the future of our communities.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/cre-summer#first-apply-now-link)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

During the summer, you will gain a unique perspective on the commercial real estate industry, while building client relationships. You’ll work with people across the team to hone your commercial real estate skills, laying a strong foundation for a successful career.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for well-rounded, self-motivated critical thinkers who have demonstrated an interest in real estate and have experience in finance,  accounting, real estate, economics, and/or public policy.

#### **Key skills**

Strong interpersonal and analytical skills are important in this role. Coursework in real estate, finance, accounting, and/or economics is a plus.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

In one of three distinct commercial real estate lines of business, you will learn how large real estate projects are financed and what considerations go into financing  commercial real estate loans, while working alongside some of the best bankers in the business.

#### **Training**

Following orientation, you will embark on an immersive learning experience, working closely with seasoned real estate industry professionals. This hands-on approach will provide comprehensive exposure to all facets of commercial real estate and provide opportunities to engage in collaborative projects, gain insights into market trends, and develop the skills necessary to excel in the dynamic world of commercial real estate.

#### **Career progression**

Our multi-week summer internship is designed to develop financial and analytical skills, as well as provide opportunities to network. At the conclusion of the program, high performing interns may receive offers of continued employment with the firm.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/cre-summer.jpg)

title: GTM Operations Manager
raw_text:
# GTM Operations Manager

JOB LEVEL P40 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor The Opportunity We are seeking a GTM Operations Manager to join a team dedicated to driving global business transformation initiatives and governing key global management, planning, and sales processes. You will play a pivotal role in driving a consistent operating rhythm at global level and across the Americas, EMEA, and J/APAC. This role presents an exciting opportunity to lead change on a global scale, with a focus on driving the performance of strategic priorities as well as oversight of some of our core management processes. What you'll Do Identification of operational improvements based on current processes and develop and implement strategies that improve the efficiency, effectiveness, and consistency of key global management processes and business reviews. Optimize sales processes and tooling, driving adoption globally while maintaining consistency across regional teams Run and act as the point of contact for several of our global management processes Work closely with our offshore Operations teams to ensure alignment with strategic GTM objectives and delivery support for our global management processes Demonstrate strong stakeholder management, building effective teams with project partners located in different regions to drive projects to completion Leading multiple projects and priorities simultaneously and effectively prioritize the time between each What you need to succeed Exceptional program management and influencing abilities, capable of inspiring change and collaboration with cross-functional teams. Strong analytical and quantitative problem-solving skills, including conducting research, analyzing data, developing hypotheses, and synthesizing recommendations Proficiency in CRM platforms (e.g., Salesforce, Dynamics) and using sales analytics tools (e.g., Power BI) to draw out insights Outstanding communication and interpersonal skills, facilitating seamless collaboration across departments and regions. Meticulous attention to detail, coupled with superb organizational skills, enabling effective project management and process optimization on a Global level. Ability to clearly communicate complex ideas both verbally & in writing Comfortable working on multiple projects and able to shift priority as needed while maintaining high quality results Attention to detail and pride in delivering your best work Curiosity, initiative, and the desire to experiment Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $106,100 -- $220,650 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. ‌ In California, the pay range for this position is $152,400 - $220,650 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP). In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award. State-Specific Notices: California : Fair Chance Ordinances Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances. Colorado: Application Window Notice If this role is open to hiring in Colorado (as listed on the job posting), the application window will remain open until at least the date and time stated above in Pacific Time, in compliance with Colorado pay transparency regulations. If this role does not have Colorado listed as a hiring location, no specific application window applies, and the posting may close at any time based on hiring needs. Massachusetts: Massachusetts Legal Notice It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. Internal Opportunities Creativity, curiosity, and constant learning are celebrated aspects of your career growth journey. We’re glad that you’re pursuing a new opportunity at Adobe! Put your best foot forward: 1. Update your Resume/CV and Workday profile – don’t forget to include your uniquely ‘Adobe’ experiences and volunteer work. 2. Visit the Internal Mobility page on Inside Adobe to learn more about the process and set up a job alert for roles you’re interested in. 3. Check out these tips to help you prep for interviews. 4. If you are applying for a role outside of your current country, ensure you review the International Resources for Relocating Employees on Inside Adobe, including the impacts to your Benefits, AIP, Equity & Payroll . Once you apply for a role via Workday, the Talent Team will reach out to you within 2 weeks. If you move into the official interview process with the hiring team, make sure you inform your manager so they can champion your career growth. At Adobe, you will be immersed in an exceptional work environment that is recognized around the world. You will also be surrounded by colleagues who are committed to helping each other grow through our unique Check-In approach where ongoing feedback flows freely. If you’re looking to make an impact, Adobe's the place for you. Discover what our employees are saying about their career experiences on the Adobe Life blog and explore the meaningful benefits we offer. Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, se

title: Senior Digital Experience Data Architect
raw_text:
# Senior Digital Experience Data Architect

JOB LEVEL P40 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor Opportunity As a Senior Digital Experience Data Architect at Adobe, you will lead the development of advanced, secure data solutions for our innovative digital marketing platforms. We craft data strategy, architecture, SQL and governance for customer solutions, including end-to-end profiles, segmentation, journeys, and campaigns! What You’ll Do Provide deep experience to build data solutions across multiple Adobe products and cloud platforms aligned with enterprise data strategy. Engage closely with customers and Adobe team to facilitate data discovery by evaluating systems and business processes. Analyze customer sources, data movement, data granularity, relationships, data format, etc. Unify disparate data (e.g., sales, CRM, analytics data, etc.) to meet customers’ digital marketing use cases. Lead and develop conceptual, logical, and physical data models for multi-solution delivery. Establish frameworks for data quality, define standards, master data management, data services, data governance, data modeling, tools, data security, privacy, and compliance with industry regulations (PII, PCI, HIPAA, GDPR, etc.). We develop and champion data architecture guidelines via detailed documentation. These encompass architectural blueprints, ERDs, data flow diagrams, source-to-target mappings, data dictionaries, metadata catalogs, governance frameworks, guardrails, and security and compliance protocols. We engage with cross-disciplinary groups and Adobe professionals to identify customer demands. We provide data layout and strategic blueprints, advocating for scalable, secure resolutions. Assume the role of a liaison to promptly tackle technical obstacles. We develop strategies for growing data volumes, tools, and integrations, while aligning with customers’ objectives. We deliver mentorship on data architecture to customers and consultants to align work with targets. We deliver Adobe platform data lake and real-time customer profile to get ready for data ingestion and activation. Employ REST APIs, Postman, and basic scripting to automate and streamline the implementation and setup. Develop and construct data ingestion solutions for streaming and batch processing, online and offline data within AEP. Collaborate with data engineers to develop and implement ETL/ELT pipelines for data collection and extraction. We improve data query performance across platforms by tuning models and adhering to guidelines and ensure product guardrails are followed and deployed in solutions Requirements Degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Science, or a related field or equivalent experience. 10+ years of data architecture, modeling, SQL, and working with customers! Expert proficiency in crafting customer-centric database models, datasets (e.g., CRM, Call Center, Marketing, Offline, Point of Sale, etc.) with relational, hierarchical (e.g., RDBMS, Columnar, Data Warehouse, BI, Document, Semantic layer modeling, Data Lakes, OLTP & OLAP, etc.). Advanced skills in SQL development (e.g., functions, data transformations, arrays, joins details, stored procedures, data analysis). Deep experience in translating business requirements into scalable data architecture by conducting structured analysis of data workflows and processes Demonstrate outstanding organizational skills and ability to balance customer projects concurrently with strong verbal, written communication skills and previous experience with customer-facing engagements Special Consideration given for Hands-on use or administration of Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) Deep domain expertise in one or more industries (Retail, Telecom, Healthcare, Automotive, Cloud and Streaming platforms, etc.) Experience with programming languages (e.g., Python or Bash scripting) Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $146,800 -- $263,900 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. ‌ In California, the pay range for this position is $182,300 - $263,900 │ In New York, the pay range for this position is $182,300 - $263,900 │ In Washington, the pay range for this position is $171,600 - $248,600 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP). In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award. State-Specific Notices: California : Fair Chance Ordinances Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances. Colorado: Application Window Notice If this role is open to hiring in Colorado (as listed on the job posting), the application window will remain open until at least the date and time stated above in Pacific Time, in compliance with Colorado pay transparency regulations. If this role does not have Colorado listed as a hiring location, no specific application window applies, and the posting may close at any time based on hiring needs. raw_text:
# Business Development Specialist

JOB LEVEL S30 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor The opportunity As Business Development Representatives (BDR) for ANZ, you are responsible for helping individuals, teams, and enterprises unleash creativity and productivity with industry-leading tools like Creative Cloud, Acrobat, and Adobe Express. What you’ll do You are the main driving force for identifying and providing high-value opportunities into the Sales pipeline . You are the first line of relationship development with prospective high-value accounts. Build awareness and interest in Adobe’s Digital Media solutions (Creative Cloud, Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Sign, Firefly Services, etc.). Deliver a “consultative” approach to selling; taking time to understand the prospect’s business, their goals, objectives, and challenges before recommending a solution. Consistently achieve quality pipeline , progression of them & other metrics such as Inbound lead follow-up & outbound account engagement metrics. Manage territory creation plan for your key account executives & their accounts. Researching key accounts, identifying decision-makers, generating interest, and developing accounts to stimulate opportunity. Prospect, educate, qualify, and develop Target Accounts and inbound leads to create sales ready leads and opportunities. Quickly route prospects and current customers to appropriate colleagues to assist in solving problems and driving value. Nurtures & provides education on areas of interest for underinformed customers. Setup, attend & run qualification meetings with customers in partnership with relevant Account Executive & Solution Consultants. Updating CRM, Outreach, and other tools as necessary to ensure capture of key history & ensuring a smooth hand-off to the relevant Account Executive as necessary. E xceed monthly and quarterly targets for qualified pipeline opportunities. Providing closed-loop feedback to ensure continuous process optimisation. The business development rep must be technical and understand Adobe's Digital Marketing solutions. Skills 2 years of experience as a BDR or in a similar role, specifically supporting the creative industry space. Track record of successful business development in software, or a tech sales environment. Knowledge of creatives within an agency environment would be an advantage. Self-starter with high motivation, independence, resilience, and ability to collaborate with sales & marketing teams to deliver pipe creation goals. Proven sales skills delivering inbound & outbound experiences and building strong relationships at multiple levels within an organisation. Strong communication including listening, written, verbal skills Experience managing all areas of the BDR process including account planning, pre-call preparation, opportunity qualification, and objection handling. Ability to quickly learn & understand sophisticated business problems & translate customers' needs into solution plays. Consistently achieving inside sales goals in an automated sales environment where accurate entry and management of lead data in a CRM system was required. Positive & upbeat personality who loves to spend time engaging customers through phone, social media & email. Curiosity to learn; always be asking questions! Proficient with standard corporate productivity tools (email, voicemail, MS Office) and experience with sales tools such as Microsoft Dynamics, SFDC, Outreach, ZoomInfo, Lusha, etc. Strong time management and ability to ruthlessly prioritise key tasks Adobe for All Adobe strives to create an environment where our employees can do their best work and drive their career growth and development based on their personal goals. From fair-pay practices to Employee Networks and programs designed to make everyone feel included, we're committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace for all. Take the plunge and jump in Liking what you have read and keen to jump in? Thinking you might not have all the skills we are looking for? Just take the plunge and apply – we know that by bringing together a diverse group of people, we become so much better together. We'd love to see where we can help drive Creativity for All together. Whether you’re beginning an internship or have years of experience, your learning never stops. You’ll have access to training, mentorships and more—all designed to help you build the skills you need for every stage of your career.

title: Manager, Firefly Customer Engagement
raw_text:
# Manager, Firefly Customer Engagement

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Opportunity Join our “DevOps for Content” revolution, where we partner with global brands and agencies to transform their end-to-end creative workflows—from ideation through activation—to unlock the GenAI-powered Content Supply Chain. As a Manager, Firefly Customer Engagement, you will lead, mentor, and inspire a team of AI Engagement Managers—our customer-embedded strategists and product owners—who shape and realize customers’ GenAI transformation journeys. Your team operates at the intersection of product, engineering, enterprise consulting, and client advocacy, ensuring fast, iterative value realization that drives adoption, growth, and trust. This role calls for an inspiring people leader who balances mentorship with hands-on engagement. You’ll serve as the ultimate advocate for your team and your customers—empowering your AI Engagement Managers to deliver value at speed, building long-term executive relationships, and ensuring the voice of the customer fuels Adobe Firefly’s product innovation. If you are passionate about cultivating top consulting talent, scaling GenAI adoption, and translating bold ideas into measurable outcomes, this is your stage. What You’ll Do Team Leadership Recruit, coach, and empower a high-performing team of AI Engagement Managers. Create a culture of customer obsession, product ownership, and continuous learning, centered on GenAI and Adobe Firefly. Act as mentor and sponsor—guiding AI Engagement Managers on executive presence, value realization, and scalable playbooks. Champion career development and inspire your team to raise the bar across every engagement. Customer Engagement & Program Delivery Oversee and steer large-scale enterprise programs focused on Firefly and GenAI adoption. Ensure your team applies Forward Deployed Engineering principles—rapid prototyping, iterative feedback, and agile collaboration with customers. Act as the executive escalation point and trusted advisor for strategic customers. Reinforce a culture of measurable impact—every engagement tied to business value and ARR growth. Strategic Leadership & Partnerships Partner with Product, Engineering, and Customer Success to translate field insights into Firefly’s roadmap. Lead your team to pilot new features and best practices—accelerating adoption and shaping Adobe’s GenAI delivery standards. Represent your team in executive reviews, customer workshops, and cross-functional forums, elevating Firefly’s role in the enterprise GenAI market. Thought Leadership Stay current on GenAI, creative automation, and content supply chain advancements. Enable knowledge sharing across teams, embedding lessons learned into reusable playbooks and scaling innovation across engagements. Advocate for best practices in customer engagement, consulting excellence, and GenAI adoption inside and outside Adobe. What You Bring 8+ years in digital strategy, enterprise consulting, or AI/ML roles; 2+ years in people leadership or team management. Proven ability to lead customer-facing consulting talent—mentoring high-potential Engagement Managers to elevate impact and executive presence. Deep understanding of GenAI, Adobe Firefly, and first- and third-party AI models, with the ability to translate concepts into business value. Experience delivering enterprise-scale digital transformation or AI adoption programs with measurable impact. Familiarity with Adobe Creative Cloud, Firefly, and GenStudio Modules; Adobe Experience Cloud knowledge is a plus. Expertise in scaled agile delivery methodologies and tools (Jira, Workfront, Confluence, Miro). Exceptional communication and executive influence—capable of simplifying complexity and driving alignment at the C-suite level. Flexible, adaptable, and energized by fast-paced, high-growth environments. Willingness to travel up to 30% for customer and team engagement. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $163,900 -- $267,600 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process.

In California, the pay range for this position is $184,800 - $267,600
In New York, the pay range for this position is $184,800 - $267,600

title: Senior Expert Solution Consultant, Agentic
raw_text:
# Senior Expert Solution Consultant, Agentic

JOB LEVEL P40 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor The Opportunity Adobe is seeking a Senior Practice Lead, Expert Solution Consultant to help advance readiness, clarity, and confidence across Agents & Skills within Adobe Experience Platform (AEP). As AEP continues to evolve into an increasingly agent-powered platform, this role plays a critical part in ensuring field teams and customers feel prepared, enabled, and supported in understanding and demonstrating agentic capabilities across Real-Time CDP, Adobe Journey Optimizer (AJO), Platform Activation, and broader Experience Cloud workflows. This is a hybrid role that blends product readiness, roadmap translation, prompt design, and high-impact communication. The individual in this role will help connect where Adobe’s agentic platform is today with where it is heading—making complex innovation accessible, practical, and relevant for both internal teams and customers. Key Responsibilities Roadmap Understanding & Field Enablement · Build and maintain a strong understanding of generally available (GA) agents and skills across AEP, including their capabilities, triggers, dependencies, and common use cases. · Partner with Product, PMM, and field teams to communicate the multi-solution agent and skill roadmap to customer executives, architects, and internal stakeholders. · Translate roadmap concepts into clear, inclusive narratives that explain how agentic capabilities support faster adoption, improved time-to-value, and modern customer architectures. Readiness, Enablement & Demo Orchestration · Ensure newly released agents and skills are deployed, configured, and validated across AEP demo environments (RTCDP, AJO, Platform Web UI, edge workflows, and more). · Collaborate with Expert, Platform, and Enterprise Architecture teams to develop reusable prompt patterns, testing approaches, and demo scenarios for internal and customer-facing use. · Maintain feature readiness assets that may include: o Positioning and messaging guidance o Demo walkthroughs and best practices o Technical requirements, considerations, and known limitations Product Partnership & Feedback Loops · Actively contribute to recurring PM and PMM syncs, representing field perspectives, customer patterns, and readiness needs. · Participate in structured testing activities such as bug bashes, hands-on QA sessions, and cross-functional validation cycles ahead of broader field release. · Share thoughtful, actionable feedback to help improve both product quality and the overall field experience. Field Communication & Adoption Support · Deliver regular readiness communications that summarize agent and skill updates, roadmap highlights, demo org changes, and evolving guidance for AEP field teams. · Serve as a go-to partner for agent- and skill-related questions, ensuring clarity, consistency, and approachability in responses. · Track adoption trends, recurring questions, and enablement gaps to continuously improve readiness materials and processes. Internal & Customer Engagement · Support customer conversations, workshops, and executive briefings focused on AEP’s agentic capabilities and the value they unlock across acquisition, activation, and orchestration. · Partner closely with TPS, Product Specialists, and Enterprise Architects to ensure aligned positioning across customer engagements. · Translate technical concepts into customer-friendly language that builds confidence, trust, and momentum throughout the sales cycle. Required Qualifications · Typically 5–8+ years of experience in Solution Consulting, Pre-Sales, Enterprise Architecture, Product Enablement, or related roles. · Working knowledge of Adobe Experience Platform, including RTCDP, AJO, CJA and data activation workflows. · Demonstrated ability to learn emerging technologies quickly and translate them into practical, actionable guidance. · Experience collaborating on prompt design, Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. raw_text:
# Enterprise Sales Account Director, State and Local Government

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Opportunity Adobe aims to grow as a leading provider of enterprise software solutions. The Enterprise Sales Account Director will sell Adobe's digital experience solutions to State/Local Government agencies. This role focuses on driving business in specific states and collaborating with the sales ecosystem to identify and close opportunities. Building strong relationships with C-level customers and creating account plans are key to success. This role involves virtual interactions with customers and internal sales personnel, navigating enterprise organizations to uncover cross-selling and upselling chances. Reporting to State & Local business management. What You'll Do Run an enterprise sales territory focusing on state & local government customers Develop target named account strategies and tactical penetration plans Develop and maintain relationships at the “C” and “VP” levels of the defined target named accounts Develop compelling value propositions based on return on investment cost/benefit analysis Successfully navigate the sales process by identifying potential business prospects, assessing their suitability, and finalizing new deals. Sell against annual revenue targets for software licenses and services Coordinate with pre-sales and professional services teams Supply creative ideas and participate in marketing events Provide accurate and timely sales forecasts Develop, maintain and strengthen third party relationships What You Will Need Demonstrated sales track record with Tier 1 and Tier 2 government customers (5-10 years’ experience) Understanding of public sector industry with an emphasis on digital experience for customers Understanding of broad competitor solution footprints for the information systems marketplace Be able to work with prospects to understand their business requirements and value models Ability to quickly adapt and then clearly articulate value propositions Ability to work cross functionally and collaboratively to bring in the right resources to bear at the right stage in the selling process Experience and success in selling high value, long lead time enterprise solutions software ($500K and above) Excellent new business development skills and sales quota attainment track record Demonstrates a resourceful and proactive approach; takes the initiative to connect with prospects in creative ways; takes full responsibility for their own success with minimal support until prospects are qualified; showcases perseverance and a strong desire to achieve. Strong skills in the following: communication, presentation skills, negotiation, organizational and attention to detail Proficient networker. Ability to develop and use relationships with senior industry leaders and key influencers High comfort level and presence with senior government executives An accomplished history of selling multi-level to business, technical, IT people, and C-level executives Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in a related area Regional travel for this role is approximately 50% by land and/or air Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. title: Senior Data Scientist, Firefly
raw_text:
# Senior Data Scientist, Firefly

JOB LEVEL P40 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor The Opportunity: Adobe Firefly is transforming how creators ideate, create, and express themselves through generative AI. As Firefly continues to scale, data-driven growth and high-quality experimentation are core to how we learn, prioritize, and deliver value to millions of users. We are looking for a Senior Data Scientist to drive growth insights, experimentation strategy, and decision-making for Firefly. In this role, you will partner closely with Product, Engineering, Design, and Marketing to design experiments, measure impact, and uncover opportunities to improve user acquisition, activation, engagement, and retention across Firefly surfaces. This is a highly visible role with significant ownership, technical depth, and influence over product direction. What you’ll do: · Define and evolve core growth, engagement, and experimentation metrics for Firefly. · Translate ambiguous product and growth questions into structured analytical approaches. · Identify user behavior patterns, friction points, and growth opportunities across the Firefly funnel (acquisition, activation, engagement, retention). · Provide thought leadership on experiment design, tradeoffs, and prioritization. · Develop, complete, and analyze experiments (A/B, multivariate, quasi-experimental) to answer critical product and growth questions. · Partner with product teams to define experiment success metrics and guardrails aligned with user value and business goals. · Communicate / present concise and actionable insights through engaging data storytelling to product and business partners. · Define and scale self-serve analytics and dashboards that drives an improved understanding of product usage & end to end customer lifecycle · Partner with data engineering to improve data quality, logging, and experimentation infrastructure. · Work closely with Product Managers, Engineers, UX Researchers, and Designers to guide product strategy with data. What you’ll need to succeed: · 4+ years of experience in data science, applied statistics, or analytics, preferably in product, growth, or experimentation-focused roles. · Deep understanding of experimental design, statistical inference, causal reasoning, and common pitfalls in experimentation. · Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python or R for analysis and modeling. · Experience using modern data science workflows on the cloud (e.g., Git, Databricks, Python, AWS, Azure or equivalent). · Experience working with large-scale product datasets and behavioral event data. · Proven experience influencing product or growth decisions through data. · Strong intuition for user behavior and ability to connect metrics to real-world user experiences. · Ability to clearly communicate complex analyses and tradeoffs to both technical and non-technical audiences. · Experience partnering with senior product and engineering leaders. · Experience with Adobe Analytics or similar web/product analytics tool. · BS, MS, or PhD in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Engineering) or equivalent practical experience. #FireflyGenAI Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. title: Machine Learning Engineer 3
raw_text:
# Machine Learning Engineer 3

JOB LEVEL P30 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor The Opportunity Adobe is looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to understand and optimize the experience of Adobe’s Digital Experience customers. By partnering with Adobe Research and other business units, you will build products that transform how companies approach audience creation, journey optimization, and personalization at scale. Join a diverse, collaborative, and lively group of engineers and scientists well-established in the ML space. The work here is dynamic, fast-paced, creative, and data-driven. What you'll Do Develop innovative models in collaboration with Adobe Research. Design, develop, and deploy applications powered by predictive and generative models, with a focus on building autonomous agents and using agentic frameworks for adaptive decision-making. Implement ML-Ops best practices to ensure scalable, reliable, and efficient machine learning workflows. Engage in the product lifecycle, including architecture, design, deployment, and production operations. Understand data to make recommendations for the right predictive models, quality metrics, and governance approaches. What you need to succeed The ideal candidate will have the following background: MS in Computer Science, Data Science or Statistics with 3+ years of applied AI/ML experience, including developing, evaluating ML models, and deploying models into production or PhD degree in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field. Deep understanding of statistical modeling, machine learning, or analytics concepts, with a proven track record of solving problems using these methods. Experience in building large-scale data pipelines. Ability to quickly learn new skills and work in a fast-paced team. Proficiency in one or more programming languages such as Python, Scala, Java, or SQL. Proficiency in ML frameworks such as scikit-learn, SparkML, TensorFlow, or PyTorch. Experience working with both research and product teams. Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. Excellent communication and relationship-building skills. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $125,600 -- $234,150 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. ‌ In California, the pay range for this position is $161,700 - $234,150 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP). In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award. State-Specific Notices: California : Fair Chance Ordinances Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances. Colorado: Application Window Notice If this role is open to hiring in Colorado (as listed on the job posting), the application window will remain open until at least the date and time stated above in Pacific Time, in compliance with Colorado pay transparency regulations. If this role does not have Colorado listed as a hiring location, no specific application window applies, and the posting may close at any time based on hiring needs. Massachusetts: Massachusetts Legal Notice It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. Internal Opportunities Creativity, curiosity, and constant learning are celebrated aspects of your career growth journey. We’re glad that you’re pursuing a new opportunity at Adobe! Put your best foot forward: 1. Update your Resume/CV and Workday profile – don’t forget to include your uniquely ‘Adobe’ experiences and volunteer work. 2. Visit the Internal Mobility page on Inside Adobe to learn more about the process and set up a job alert for roles you’re interested in. 3. Check out these tips to help you prep for interviews. 4. If you are applying for a role outside of your current country, ensure you review the International Resources for Relocating Employees on Inside Adobe, including the impacts to your Benefits, AIP, Equity & Payroll . Once you apply for a role via Workday, the Talent Team will reach out to you within 2 weeks. If you move into the official interview process with the hiring team, make sure you inform your manager so they can champion your career growth. At Adobe, you will be immersed in an exceptional work environment that is recognized around the world. You will also be surrounded by colleagues who are committed to helping each other grow through our unique Check-In approach where ongoing feedback flows freely. If you’re looking to make an impact, Adobe's the place for you. Discover what our employees are saying about their career experiences on the Adobe Life blog and explore the meaningful benefits we offer. Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law. Learn more. Adobe aims to make Adobe.com accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com o

title: Principal Creative Solutions Marketing Manager
raw_text:
# Principal Creative Solutions Marketing Manager

JOB LEVEL P50 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor As a Principal Creative Solutions Marketing Manager on Adobe’s Enterprise Solutions Marketing team, you will shape the future of how Adobe brings multiproduct creative solutions to market. In this highly strategic and deeply hands-on role, you’ll sit at the intersection of storytelling, product innovation, and customer reality—crafting compelling, end-to-end narratives that showcase the transformative business value of Adobe’s Digital Media ecosystem. You’ll play a pivotal role in defining how customers understand—and experience—the power of integrated solutions spanning Creative Cloud, Firefly, Frame.io, 3D/Immersive, and more. If you're energized by the opportunity to influence Adobe’s vision, help guide product direction, and equip our sales teams with the narratives and tools they need to win, this role puts you at the forefront of Adobe’s next wave of innovation. Key Responsibilities Craft Multi-Product Solution Narratives: Partner with PMMs and cross-functional collaborators to build clear, compelling, and differentiated solution stories rooted in real customer pain points and business outcomes. Develop Sales-Ready GTM Content: Create crisp, actionable sales collateral—including pitch decks, solution guides, value frameworks, and talk tracks—that show sellers how to position, articulate, and land Adobe’s multiproduct value. Design Visionary Solution Demos: Architect and produce elevated, future-facing solution demos that showcase our products working together to solve high-impact creative workflow challenges. Influence Product Direction: Advocate for new capabilities, integrations, and roadmap investments that unlock deeper solution potential across Digital Media products. Champion Real-World Customer Outcomes: Work closely with presales, customer success, and account teams to identify lighthouse customers, develop reference stories, and demonstrate practical impact through real workflows and results. Align Across the GTM Engine: Collaborate with sales leaders, field enablement, marketing, and PMMs to ensure GTM motions are consistent, relevant, and fueled by strong, repeatable narratives. Be the Creative Practitioner Voice: Bring hands-on expertise with Adobe’s creative tools and an understanding of end-to-end creative operations to ground solution stories in authenticity and credibility. Required Skills & Experience BA/BS Degree preferred OR 10+ years in Product Marketing, solutions marketing OR equivalent practical experience. 10+ years in Product Marketing, Solutions marketing, creative workflow strategy, or a related field within SaaS, digital media, or creative technology. Deep hands-on experience with Adobe Digital Media products (e.g., Creative Cloud, Firefly, Frame.io, 3D/Immersive). Practitioner background in design, creative operations, content production, or related creative disciplines—able to speak the language of creative teams. Proven ability to create multiproduct narratives, GTM messaging, and customer-facing collateral. Demonstrated success building high-impact demos, product stories, or solution use cases that connect business value to technical capability. Strong cross-functional leadership skills and experience partnering with sales, PM, PMM, and customer-facing teams. Exceptional storytelling, communication, and presentation skills. Ability to simplify complex product ecosystems into crisp customer value propositions. Preferred Qualifications Prior experience supporting enterprise sales or complex B2B solution sales motions. Familiarity with creative workflow and content supply chain challenges faced by large enterprises. Experience shaping product strategy or advocating for customer-driven roadmap decisions. Strong understanding of AI’s role in modern creative workflows and its emerging business value. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $151,800 -- $257,100 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. ‌ In Colorado, the pay range for this position is $161,800 - $234,250 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP). In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award. State-Specific Notices: California : Fair Chance Ordinances Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances. ## **Design the career of your dreams.**

title: Senior Product Manager — Workflow Builder Runtime & Extensibility (Agentic, AI-First Enterprise Workflow Platform)
raw_text:
# Senior Product Manager — Workflow Builder Runtime & Extensibility (Agentic, AI-First Enterprise Workflow Platform)

JOB LEVEL P40 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor Our Company Adobe is changing the world through digital experiences. We build the platforms and tools that creators and the world’s biggest brands rely on to deliver exceptional content at global scale. We’re committed to a workplace where everyone is respected, supported, and empowered to do the best work of their career. The Opportunity The next wave of automation isn’t about stitching together integrations , it’s about building a programmable, enterprise-grade runtime teams can trust in production: observable, governable, resilient, and fast . Workflow Builder is Adobe’s path to that future. We’re building an agentic, AI-first workflow runtime that orchestrates production workflows across services, tools, and systems , then elevating it with a first-class extensibility platform so customers can build custom actions and reuse them safely across workflows and teams. In this runtime, “AI-first” isn’t a bolt-on. It’s part of the execution model: workflows can include reasoning steps that plan, select tools/actions, validate outcomes, and escalate to humans when needed while staying inside enterprise guardrails. We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own the runtime + extensibility foundation end-to-end. This is a high-agency platform role: you’ll define the execution model, developer surface area, governance guardrails, and the roadmap that unlocks real production adoption at enterprise scale. Who This Role Is Built For You’ll thrive here if you’re energized by: • Building platforms that enable other builders (creative technologists, solution teams, partners, customers , developers ) to move faster. • Designing primitives and contracts (not one-off features) that scale across many workflows and use cases. • Crafting a developer experience that teams choose because it’s clear, composable, and reliable . • Owning hard problems in multi-tenant runtime execution: isolation, performance, observability, governance, lifecycle management . • Turning power-user needs into a system that’s approachable and production-ready , especially when workflows include AI/agentic steps. What You’ll Do Own the product strategy and roadmap for the Workflow Builder runtime: orchestration, execution semantics, state, scaling, reliability, and operability. Own extensibility end-to-end: define how customers build, test, deploy, version, govern, and operate custom actions that execute safely within workflows, including: Packaging & distribution Versioning, compatibility, and migration strategies Authentication/authorization models Data access boundaries, secrets handling, and tenant isolation Policy enforcement, approvals, and governance controls Runtime limits, performance, and cost controls Observability (logs/metrics/traces), debugging, incident readiness Define the platform contracts: workflow definitions, triggers, connectors, action schemas, retries, idempotency, error handling, and SLO-backed reliability. Define and ship agentic runtime primitives : structured state/context, tool/action invocation, planning + execution loops, bounded autonomy, deterministic fallbacks, and human-in-the-loop checkpoints. Build the trust layer for AI-first execution : permissions, policy enforcement, audit trails, evaluation hooks, and enterprise controls over what agentic steps can do. Partner deeply with engineering, security, and platform teams to ensure the runtime is enterprise-grade: compliance, auditing, tenancy, and secure-by-default execution. Drive tight discovery with enterprise adopters and internal solution teams to validate requirements and ensure the platform supports real production workloads. Lead cross-functional execution , aligning stakeholders, clarifying trade-offs, and shipping iteratively with measurable adoption. The Hard Problems You’ll Own This role directly tackles challenges like: Safe, multi-tenant execution of customer-built actions (isolation, quotas, data boundaries) Deterministic, reproducible workflow behavior across versions Governance that scales (policy, approvals, auditability) without killing builder velocity First-class observability across workflow + action boundaries Reliable execution under production load (timeouts, retries, backpressure, failure modes) Making agentic behavior enterprise-trustworthy : bounded autonomy, predictable outcomes, and auditable decisions Operating AI-first workflows at scale: latency/cost controls, fallback strategies, and incident-ready visibility across model + action boundaries What Success Looks Like (First 6–12 Months) Examples of outcomes you may drive: A production-ready extensibility framework where customers can ship custom actions with clear lifecycle management and governance Defined execution semantics and reliability targets that unlock customer confidence (SLOs, incident playbooks, operational maturity) A developer experience that materially reduces time-to-integrate and time-to-debug (SDK/ API , testing/sandboxing, great docs) Early enterprise reference workloads running end-to-end with measurable adoption and reusability A clear, shippable foundation for AI-first workflows: policy + evaluation hooks + human-in-the-loop patterns that teams can use safely in production What You’ll Bring 8+ years of product management experience, ideally spanning platform products, workflow/orchestration systems, developer tools, or enterprise infrastructure. Strong technical fluency and comfort with systems thinking APIs, distributed execution, multi-tenancy, isolation boundaries, reliability/performance trade-offs. Experience building extensible platforms: plugins/extensions, SDKs, “bring your own code,” custom integrations, or ecosystems with third-party builders. Experience shipping AI-powered systems in production (agents, orchestration, evaluation, guardrai

title: Risk Management Full-time Analyst | JPMorgan Chase & Co.
raw_text:
We and our partners use cookies and other tools for advertising, to help stop fraud, and for other purposes. By using this site, you agree to how your information is used as outlined in our [Privacy Policy](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/legal/privacy).

[![Close Icon](https://www.chase.com/etc/designs/chase-ux/css/img/icon_close.svg)](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/risk-fulltime-analyst#)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/risk-fulltime-analyst#main)

# Risk Management Analyst Program

Risk management is critical to every aspect of our businesses, from developing trading models to structuring financing for mergers and acquisitions. You will mitigate and manage risk, while maintaining transparency with management, clients, regulators, and shareholders.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

In this program, you’ll be part of the Credit or Risk teams, where you'll learn about risk management and gain knowledge through a variety of hands-on experiences.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

You should have a positive attitude, be cooperative and intelligent, exercise sound judgment, and be detail-oriented and driven.

#### **Key skills**

You should have excellent written and verbal communication skills, sharp analytical skills, and be familiar with corporate finance and capital markets.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

Working with internal and external stakeholders, you will conduct industry, credit, and financial analysis. You’ll be instrumental on deal teams, engaged in documentation negotiations. You’ll help define and set risk frameworks while managing exposures.

#### **Training**

Your new career in risk management begins with four weeks of training. You’ll learn real-world applications working alongside top professionals, and enjoy access to ongoing instruction through our development programs.

#### **Career progression**

Post-program career opportunities vary, ranging from advancement or lateral moves to a risk management team in a different business.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/risk-fulltime-analyst.jpg)

# Marketing Leadership Development Program

We’re looking for applicants with a passion for marketing, a creative mindset, and the strategic ability to turn insights into actionable results. You’ll be part of a cross line of business program that is integral to the future of marketing at the firm and will give you the skills to build your career at JPMorganChase.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

The program starts with hands-on training, followed by three eight-month-long rotations across our lines of business and corporate marketing team. You’ll build your foundational knowledge, professional skills, and experiences in the following areas: Marketing Analytics, Strategy and Execution, and Brand/Creative.

You’ll work on highly strategic projects, including supporting the delivery of effective plans to drive brand awareness and engagement, engaging with creative partners to support the development and delivery of impactful marketing programs, and using data to support decision-making.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued Qualities & Skills:**

Excellent project management and problem-solving skills, exceptional interpersonal and communication skills, and aptitude to digest large amounts of information and use it to develop innovative solutions with strong attention to detail.

### What we offer

In this two-year program, with three 8-month rotations, you will have the opportunity to learn from and collaborate with experts to help define and manage our brands, conduct market research, and drive customer acquisition and engagement.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You will collaborate with our partners to produce marketing and promotional materials, oversee external vendors and agencies, and measure the impact and effectiveness of marketing campaigns.

#### **Training**

Over the course of the program, you’ll have access to development training, networking, business deep dives, and one-on-one mentorship.

#### **Career progression**

Throughout your rotations, we’ll provide ongoing support to prepare and empower you to take the next step in your marketing career. Upon successfully completing the program, you’ll have the opportunity to transition to a new role within our Marketing organization.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/marketing-fulltime-inside-program.jpg)
