title: Director, Value Consulting — Foundry
raw_text:
# Director, Value Consulting — Foundry

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Opportunity Adobe’s Digital Strategy Group (DSG) is a specialized advisory team that helps enterprise customers accelerate transformation and unlock measurable business impact through Adobe technology. The emergence of Firefly Foundry —Adobe’s enterprise grade platform for developing private, deeply-tuned generative AI models—has created a new category of customer value. The Director, Value Consulting — Foundry will play a critical role in shaping and quantifying that value. This position partners with strategic customers to define the business impact of Foundry’s AI model development, content supply chain transformation, and next generation creative workflows. You will serve as a senior advisor to executive stakeholders, translating complex AI capabilities into compelling, data-backed business cases that accelerate decision-making and deal progression. What You’ll Do Strategic Customer Engagements Lead value-driven engagements with C-suite, SVP, and VP-level stakeholders to understand business objectives, current content operations, AI maturity, and transformation goals. Translate Foundry’s technical capabilities (e.g., private multimodal model training, content production acceleration, creative workflow redesign) into clear, quantifiable business outcomes. Prioritize the needs of customers, understanding business specific challenges and goals ensuring alignment to Adobe solutions. Value Proposition & Business Case Development Build global standardized financial models to support the technical/functional/business benefits of the Adobe Foundry solution. Collaborate with Sales, Solution Consulting, Product, and Foundry GTM to ensure alignment on assumptions, pricing constructs, and customer-specific use case scoping. Develop Foundry success stories by working closely with post-sales engagement teams. Advanced Analytics & Insight Generation Use financial modeling, benchmarking, productivity analysis, AI-driven insights, and customer data (when available) to articulate the business impact of Foundry’s model development tracks, Media Intelligence, and creative automation capabilities. Identify opportunities to expand the business case across adjacent Adobe solutions (e.g., Content Supply Chain, Creative Cloud, GenStudio). Thought Leadership & Executive Storytelling Develop and present compelling narratives that frame the strategic value of foundational AI models in enterprise content creation and delivery. Influence customer vision by bringing forward market insights, industry trends, and competitive intelligence relevant to AI adoption in M&E, CPG, Retail, and other content-intensive industries. Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with Foundry Product Management, Engineering, and Applied Science teams to incorporate the latest capabilities into customer value frameworks. Partner with Field teams to prioritize opportunities, shape deal strategy, and support executive alignment. Value Methodology & Asset Development Create a global standard for Value Benchmarks and structured financial models. Help define standards for measuring value realization post-launch in partnership with Customer Success. Enable the broader field organization on value narrative and adoption. What You’ll Need to Succeed Experience & Expertise 10+ years in value engineering, enterprise B2B selling, management consulting, creative agency or a similar customer-facing strategic role. Deep understanding of AI-driven transformation, generative AI (especially multimodal models), digital content creation workflows, or cloud-based enterprise platforms. Strong financial modeling, quantitative analysis, and business case development experience. Demonstrated ability to guide and influence senior-level stakeholders through complex decision cycles. Experience with database capturing of value/benefit categories and impact. MBA preferred Skills & Abilities Exceptional executive communication and storytelling skills—able to distill complex concepts into clear, compelling business narratives. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to structure ambiguous challenges and build advanced Excel models. Collaborative mindset with the ability to partner across product, engineering, and sales teams. # PS Capacity Planning Manager

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! PS Capacity Planning Manager Join our world-class team at Adobe as a PS Capacity Planning Manager. This outstanding opportunity allows you to bring to bear your strategic insight and expertise in Professional Services to deliver flawless outcomes. Based in Lehi, you will collaborate closely with various teams to ensure efficient capacity planning and implementation. Your work will help build the future of digital experiences in an exceptionally dynamic environment. Key Responsibilities Lead all aspects of Professional Services capacity planning to ensure resources are improved efficiently. Provide strategic, data-driven insights that support ambitious business decision-making and growth. Present data and insights to collaborators in a clear and compelling manner. Collaborate with teams to identify and implement process improvements. Monitor and analyze benchmarks to determine business trends. Ensure data accuracy and integrity through strict adherence to data governance principles. Successfully implement data analytics projects from conception to completion. Requirements 10+ years of experience in the Professional Services domain. Proven background as a Business Intelligence Analyst or in a related position. Outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI). Ability to communicate complex data insights to non-technical collaborators. Ambitious, collaborative, and diligent approach. At Adobe, we foster an inclusive and collaborative environment where you can thrive and make a significant impact. Join us as we continue to set the standard for digital innovation and compete at the highest levels of the industry! Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $130,800 -- $262,025 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. 
In Illinois, the pay range for this position is $154,000 - $223,000
In Massachusetts, the pay range for this position is $154,000 - $223,000
 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award. title: Firefly Product Sales Specialist – Middle East
raw_text:
# Firefly Product Sales Specialist – Middle East

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! Location: KAFD, Riyadh Hours of work : 40 hours per week Education requirement: Degree or equivalent qualification/experience Experience requirement: Minimum 3 years in SaaS solution environment Benefits: Private medical insurance; Employee stock purchase plan; Holiday & global well-being days; Allowances plus many more. Application start and end date: Starting 9th January for approx. 4-6 weeks. Adobe Middle East Adobe in the Middle East empowers businesses and individuals through creativity and digital transformation. In Saudi Arabia, we support Vision 2030 by driving innovation, developing local talent, and enabling exceptional customer experiences. As the regional headquarters for foreign companies, Adobe serves as a strategic hub for growth and collaboration across markets. Join us to help shape the future of digital experiences in one of the world’s most dynamic economies. The Opportunity As a Product Sales Specialist at Adobe you will help our enterprise clients Scale Creative Asset Production with Adobe Firefly Enterprise Solutions. This role will partner with Adobe’s Account Executives to help grow the existing business through identifying cross-sell and upsell opportunities. This role requires solution selling capabilities, experience selling value to customers, strong business and selling instincts, and direct, in-person consultation with customers. The Firefly Enterprise Solutions Product Specialist is responsible for guiding and navigating through an enterprise organization to ensure a successful sales cycle, start to finish. As a Firefly Enterprise Solutions Product Specialist, you will play a crucial role in pushing the boundaries of digital creativity and empowering artists and designers to build awe-inspiring content. Responsibilities Sales Field Alignment – Work with Adobe Account Executives to support Multi-Product Enterprise Solutions, Strategic Firefly-only Solutions or as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) for general Firefly sales engagements. Expert in Firefly and GenAI for your area to improve efficiency in creative GenAI projects, collaborating with various departments. Account Planning – Develop targeted account strategies and tactical penetration plans. Relationship Management – Develop and maintain relationships at the “C-Suite” and “VP” levels within targeted accounts. Relationships will be with both IT and Business segments of the enterprise. Build & Develop Pipeline – Identify cross-sell opportunities through targeted whitespace analysis, partner with pre-sales to drive maturity and sales stage progression in preparation for in-quarter execution. Deliver Product Presentations – Understand customer needs and solution fit by delivering effective product demonstrations, use cases, positioning differentiated value/vision, and sales pitches. Develop Arguments – Develop strong Creative GenAI arguments that emphasize a compelling differentiated value proposition, thought leadership, and return on investment cost/benefit analysis. Coordinate Resource Expertise – Own coordination of pre-sales, product and commercial expertise in the sales cycle while project managing key deliverables to an established timeline. Close Sales – Build proposals, negotiate contract pricing & contractual agreement to close sale. Qualifications Minimum 5+ years consistent track record of enterprise-level, technical solution direct sales expertise within a more sophisticated sales model is required. Shown experience consulting and selling digital transformation and enterprise solutions to “C-Suite” executives and overachieving on quotas is required. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams and explain technical concepts to non-technical collaborators. Experience working with Emerging or Creative Technologies would be a plus. Adobe Creative Cloud products (i.e., Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, and After Effects) or Adobe Experience Cloud products (i.e., AEM, AEP) is nice to have. Knowledge of generative AI technologies, including deep learning, neural networks, and generative models is a plus. Strong knowledge of creative workflows and the design process, with the ability to find opportunities for integrating generative AI techniques. Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to develop innovative solutions to complex challenges. As our many awards will tell you, at Adobe you’ll be immersed in an exceptional work environment that is recognized around the world. You’ll be surrounded by colleagues who are committed to helping each other grow through our unique Check-In approach where ongoing feedback flows freely. If you’re looking to make an impact, Adobe's the place for you. Discover what our employees are saying about their career experiences on the Adobe Life blog, https://blog.adobe.com/en/topics/adobe-life and explore the fantastic benefits we offer at Rewards.adobe.com. Adobe is an equal opportunity employer. We hire dedicated individuals, regardless of gender, race or colour, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We know that when our employees feel appreciated and included, they can be more creative, inn

title: Enterprise Sales Account Manager
raw_text:
# Enterprise Sales Account Manager

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Opportunity The Education- Enterprise Sales Account Manager, sells Adobe Creative Cloud and Document Cloud to customers in assigned Territory. May generate prospective customers through cold calling and may qualify and follow up with sales leads. Sales can be made through multichannel, inbound and/or outbound sales activities. Requires application of in-depth technical knowledge of products. Responsible for selling prospective education customers on Adobe’s subscription offering through the VIP teams and enterprise license programs. This will include deal development and deal qualification, articulating value proposition to mid-level and top executives, establishing and gaining buy in around pricing, and closing the deal. Deal sizes will generally range from $11K and up. This is a fast growing, dynamic market with outstanding sales potential. Adobe is owning the way forward in the market and seeks a motivated sales professional to join the team. What you’ll do: Constantly fill the pipeline with new deals Deal development & qualification, lead generation as well as closure on all deals. Includes cold calling as well as cultivation of existing leads and working with partner ecosystem. Articulating the Adobe value proposition to mid as well as c-level decision-makers. Help customers migrate to Enterprise version of software from older perpetual licensing and Teams subscription. Grow the customer’s use of Adobe’s products and spend with Adobe. Accurately forecast and handle pipeline on a weekly, monthly and quarterly basis. Keep accurate and up to date records of all deals in CRM system. What you need to succeed: 8-10 years sales experience, preferably in education vertical. Shown success in penetrating new markets and closing new business. Consistent over-achievement of quota. Technically adept, ambitious, and motivated by results. Ability to accurately forecast based upon realistic opportunity assessments. Ability to build and develop relationships with the partner ecosystem. Ensure 100% customer satisfaction with all respective customers. Maintain professional internal and external relationships that meet our core values and align with our culture. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Prior experience with MS Office Suite as well as CRM solutions such as Salesforce.com. Adobe is an equal opportunity employer. We hire hardworking individuals, regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, ancestry, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, cultural background or religious beliefs. We know that when our employees feel appreciated and included, they can be more creative, innovative and successful. This is what it means to be Adobe For All. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law. Learn more. Adobe aims to make Adobe.com accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call (408) 536-3015.

title: Manager - India Greenfield
raw_text:
# Manager - India Greenfield

JOB LEVEL M20 EMPLOYEE ROLE People Manager Adobe Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences. We’re passionate about empowering people to create appealing and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours. The Art of Sales is changing The concept of selling has been around for decades, but businesses are digital now. The way sales are conducted must modernize too. In most companies, sales teams are somewhere on the journey below: (1) Sale scratches out their own leads by prospecting their networks, sending one-off or batch emails. (2) Marketing turns over long lists of leads, which are typically contacts in your target audience (3) Marketing has established a repeated process of generating MQL, SAL & SQL that yield some measurable returns. They sometime add lead scoring to this mix. On the other hand, we at Adobe - ICX Sales are a Modern Sales Team of 400+ Digital sellers, supported by analytics and data science teams. We are integrated with Adobe.com digital marketing & engagement funnel and are developing capabilities on picking cues from the customers demographic (who) & behavioral (what) data that they leave on their journey with our business to predict next ‘sales action’ that will lead to revenue conversions (Future state). The Challenge: We are looking for a Sales Manager and Leader who is leading sales for a team and enable the Digital Sales team to continue exceeding the defined sales goals and critical metrics across the North Americas territory. You would be responsible for the Digital Media portfolio of Adobe. An ideal candidate would be a leader with vision and a proven record of leading successful sales operations on field or in Digital Sales of reasonable size and possess the ability to turn findings into executable plans. The candidate must also be an encouraging leader capable of building, developing & encouraging a high-performing team. What you will do: As a Leader: • Leading the team by coaching mentoring & motivating the team with a high level of EQ & becoming a trusted leader for the team. • Should be a great teammate, as the Digital Sellers use other teams for their own success. • Driving the highest level of performance from the team at the same time role modelling & instilling Adobe values in the team. • Building knowledge of Adobe products and services – their features, benefits, prices, and additional services. • Developing ways to coach the team to identify customer needs and pitch the relevant product benefits to complete a sale. • Working with Marketing, Sales Operation, Business units, Corporate Retention teams to source sales or retention campaigns and marketing leads. Analyze business goals, customer & agent data & daily reports to find areas of continuous improvement. As Sales Professional: • The Adobe TSM will lead a set of sellers and will be accountable to help identify, drive & close revenue from existing Adobe Direct customers. • Will own the “more connect per seller” and “more per connect” ICX philosophy. • Will develop account penetration strategies, identifying business opportunities by creating and implementing campaigns (phone/email) • You will help consolidate feedback from team, to identify and gather customer journeys and present / implement proposals to nudge the stalled ones. • Streamline sales processes, improve efficiency, and lead pipeline oversight, forecasting, and sales behaviors for achieving the future state. • You will monitor, coach, and develop ways to coach the team to identify customer needs and pitch the relevant product benefits to complete a sale. • You will accomplish ICX human resource objectives by recruiting, selecting, orienting, training, assigning, mentor, counselling, and improvement plans for employees; communicating job expectations; monitoring, appraising, and reviewing job contributions; planning and reviewing compensation actions. • You will maintain a daily cadence of leads/ CTA touched; opportunities created and transactions. • Define and Achieve Weekly, Monthly & Quarterly targets both on the input & Output parameters. What you need to succeed • Demonstrated success in previous experiences with ability to define, refine and implement successful sales processes, procedures and policies that get results and the bottom line. • Possess capabilities to successfully lead the team virtually, given that we are a remote sales team. • Demonstrated ability to be a quick learner. • Should know how to motivate and lead the team to build skills on new Adobe Solutions • Ability to compile & analyze complex data, translate the results into actionable outcomes and present in digestible form to the entire organization. • Willingness to work across shifts. • Advanced knowledge of Sales Operations, Technology used in Digital sales. • Thought leadership on Sales, Customer Success, and Subscription management. • Postgraduate with demonstrated ability in Field Sales and/or Digital Sales • Validated ability to inspire and motivate people, hire and train, coach new sales team members. • Confirmed ability to communicate and present to management new insights, proposals and feedback to move the business forward. Get to know the team ICX Sales ICX focuses on advancing innovative content and driving digital transformation of industries. It provides tools and services that enable individuals, small businesses, and enterprises to create, publish, promote, handle and monetize their content anywhere through the Adobe Creative Cloud and Document Cloud. title: Sr. Program Manager / Strategy Lead, EWX Transformation
raw_text:
# Sr. Program Manager / Strategy Lead, EWX Transformation

JOB LEVEL P50 EMPLOYEE ROLE Individual Contributor The Opportunity We’re seeking an experienced Senior Program Manager, EWX Transformation to shape the future of employee experience at Adobe. In this high-visibility role, you’ll join the Office of Transformation within the Employee & Workplace Experience (EWX) organization—a team of bold strategists, process/service designers, and change leaders passionate about solving complex challenges. You’ll own a portfolio of global transformation initiatives that modernize how we deliver HR services, connect employees to meaningful support, and accelerate Adobe’s business outcomes. This is one of the most exciting times to be part of an employee experience organization. Companies everywhere are reimagining how they support and inspire their people—and Adobe is leading the way. This is an opportunity to lead enterprise-wide change and deliver measurable impact. If you’re motivated by big change and ready to make your mark on how a global workforce is supported, this is your moment! What you'll do Lead HR Transformation Programs: Own the end-to-end delivery of transformation programs that reimagine how HR services are delivered at scale—with an emphasis on effectiveness, efficiency, and employee experience. Strategy & Roadmap Development: Translate executive vision into actionable strategies, detailed plans, and measurable outcomes. Secure investment for transformation efforts through cost-benefit analyses, financial modeling, and clear articulation of return on investment. Portfolio Management: Oversee the transformation project portfolio, including intake, prioritization, resourcing, and ongoing monitoring and communication to maintain visibility and ensure successful delivery. Support project teams in d efining objectives and key results, tracking project/program performance, and providing regular updates to leadership. Change Management & Enablement: Develop coordinated change, training, and communication plans that drive adoption of new tools, services, and processes. Create learning strategies to help teams build new skills, capabilities, and ways of working—including AI fluency. Champion Product Thinking: Drive scalable solutions with a focus on usability, global and local relevance, and continuous improvement. Leverage agile practices to test, learn, and adapt quickly. Guide teams in developing intuitive, inclusive services and efficient processes for a variety of personas. Cross-Functional Leadership: Partner with global and regional Employee Experience, IT, Legal, and Finance teams to align on goals, co-create solutions, and ensure smooth execution across geographies. What you need to succeed 10+ years in strategy consulting or program leadership in global, matrixed organizations; MBA preferred . Proven experience leading enterprise-level HR or business transformation initiatives from strategy through execution. Expertise in HR strategy and operations, portfolio and change management, process optimization, and service delivery models. Strong ability to influence through clear, evidence-based recommendations and investment plans. Exceptional communication, facilitation, and storytelling skills—able to simplify complexity and inspire action . Product approach with experience applying agile methodologies . Familiarity with ServiceNow, Workday, Power BI a plus. Our compensation reflects the cost of labor across several U.S. geographic markets, and we pay differently based on those defined markets. The U.S. pay range for this position is $133,400 -- $248,400 annually. Pay within this range varies by work location and may also depend on job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for the job location during the hiring process. ‌ In California, the pay range for this position is $171,600 - $248,400 │ In New York, the pay range for this position is $171,600 - $248,400 │ In Washington, the pay range for this position is $153,200 - $221,900 At Adobe, for sales roles starting salaries are expressed as total target compensation (TTC = base + commission), and short-term incentives are in the form of sales commission plans. Non-sales roles starting salaries are expressed as base salary and short-term incentives are in the form of the Annual Incentive Plan (AIP). In addition, certain roles may be eligible for long-term incentives in the form of a new hire equity award. State-Specific Notices: California : Fair Chance Ordinances Adobe will consider qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records for employment in accordance with state and local laws and “fair chance” ordinances. Colorado: Application Window Notice If this role is open to hiring in Colorado (as listed on the job posting), the application window will remain open until at least the date and time stated above in Pacific Time, in compliance with Colorado pay transparency regulations. If this role does not have Colorado listed as a hiring location, no specific application window applies, and the posting may close at any time based on hiring needs. Massachusetts: Massachusetts Legal Notice It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. Internal Opportunities Creativity, curiosity, and constant learning are celebrated aspects of your career growth journey. We’re glad that you’re pursuing a new opportunity at Adobe! Put your best foot forward: 1. Update your Resume/CV and Workday profile – don’t forget to include your uniquely ‘Adobe’ experiences and volunteer work. 2. Visit the Internal Mobility page on Inside Adobe to learn more about the process and set up a job alert for roles you’re interested in. 3. Check out these tips to help you prep for interviews. 4. title: Customer Success Manager
raw_text:
# Customer Success Manager

Our Company Changing the world through digital experiences is what Adobe’s all about. We give everyone—from emerging artists to global brands—everything they need to design and deliver exceptional digital experiences! We’re passionate about empowering people to create beautiful and powerful images, videos, and apps, and transform how companies interact with customers across every screen. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and are committed to creating exceptional employee experiences where everyone is respected and has access to equal opportunity. We realize that new ideas can come from everywhere in the organization, and we know the next big idea could be yours! The Challenge: We are hiring a Named Customer Success Manager, Industry, to join our Adobe Experience Cloud team. To achieve the significant opportunity ahead, Adobe Customer Success needs to lead the post-sales customer experience, driving adoption and value for our customers. In this role, you will serve as an Named Customer Success Manager passionate about helping your accounts achieve their goals for personalization at scale, unifying voices across Adobe for a cohesive plan of action, and quantifying impact along the way. Named CSMs deliver an outstanding experience for Adobe’s top customers, including responsibilities for strategic planning, use case adoption, advocacy and value realization. We hire dynamic, hardworking, and creative individuals, adept at storytelling who thrive in fast-paced environments. What You'll Do: The Named Customer Success Manager will be responsible for customer-facing leadership to evangelize mutual success plans, engagement strategies, and frameworks for measurable impact. You will use strong executive presence and have a deep understanding of digital marketing, applying diverse sources of information, especially business trends and metrics, towards innovative customer experiences. Responsibilities include: Accountable for Customer’s overall success with Adobe, including vision planning, customer health, multi-solution adoption, and measurable impact through the lens of industry specialization Act as the central point of contact throughout the Customer’s lifecycle, define measurable success plans with clear outcomes, and ensure cohesive communication across internal and customer audiences at all levels. Network within accounts to achieve successful execution of client's strategy and roadmap Deliver an exceptional customer experience with proactive communication, orchestrating the right internal resources, and using the customer engagement model to align, track and evolve customer business goals Drive adoption of Adobe products – using data to provide insights and progress from baseline through the maturity curve Champion innovation by sharing industry trends and new ways your customers can use Adobe solutions to advance their digital maturity Identify Customer risk, and collaborate with the extended Adobe team to build and activate “get well” plans Be the voice of the customer internally at Adobe – sharing strategic use-cases, process improvements, and asks back into the internal ecosystem Contribute to Customer Success thought leadership and share out best practices and feedback across the organization to continuously improve our approach What you need to succeed: Bachelor’s Degree and/or relevant work experience Fluent in English and Polish Several years of Customer Success experience in Software as a Service, Digital Marketing Passion for driving customer success and measurable outcomes with proven success guiding customers to deliver business value Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to effectively navigate and mediate conflict and cultivate joint partnerships Strong consulting skills Ability to prioritize, multi-task, and perform in a fast-paced environment Exceptional organizational, presentation, and communication skills, both verbal and written Existing knowledge of software in digital marketing and/or digital media space, including data platforms, content management and customer journeys Skilled at Program Management within large organizations, helping to inspire change across groups by engaging key partners Effective at leading executive C-level discussions and presentations Flexibility to travel (approx. 30%) Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law. Learn more. Adobe aims to make Adobe.com accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call (408) 536-3015.

title: Commercial & Specialized Industries Program
raw_text:
We and our partners use cookies and other tools for advertising, to help stop fraud, and for other purposes. By using this site, you agree to how your information is used as outlined in our [Privacy Policy](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/legal/privacy).

[![Close Icon](https://www.chase.com/etc/designs/chase-ux/css/img/icon_close.svg)](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/csi-summer#)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/csi-summer#main)

# Commercial & Specialized Industries Program

The Commercial & Specialized Industries team works with mid-sized companies, municipalities and not-for-profit entities across a variety of industries, including technology, healthcare, life science, higher education and more. The team works with bankers, treasury management officers, client leaders, and more to provide a range of financing solutions and strategic advice for clients across diverse industries.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/csi-summer#first-apply-now-link)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

In this nine-week internship program, you’ll dive into one of our Commercial & Specialized Industries businesses, which will prepare you for a career in banking, treasury management or credit risk.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

You should be able to ask pointed questions, demonstrate high client-care standards, and pay close attention to detail. We’re looking for individuals with a positive attitude and who are open to challenges and new learning experiences.

#### **Key skills**

You should have superior communication and multi-tasking skills, a strong understanding of industry fundamentals, and the ability to work well on a team.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

This program provides you the opportunity to contribute to live deals and complete industry projects based on our clients. Your work may include researching a prospective client, developing cash flow models, conducting valuable market research, or helping to create a pitch book.

#### **Training**

You will receive regular training throughout the summer on the fundamentals of banking and lending while developing the soft skills to work with clients. They will also have access to senior leaders for guidance and the chance to attend networking events.

#### **Career progression**

This program prepares participants for a long-term career in Commercial & Specialized Industries. High performing interns who successfully complete the program may receive an offer of continued employment with the firm.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/mmbsi-summer.jpg)

raw_text:

[![Close Icon](https://www.chase.com/etc/designs/chase-ux/css/img/icon_close.svg)](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/asset-mgmt-analyst#)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/asset-mgmt-analyst#main)

# Asset Management Analyst Program

J.P. Morgan Asset Management is a leading investment manager of choice for institutions, financial intermediaries, and individual investors worldwide. The business offers a broad range of core and alternative investment strategies and has investment professionals operating in every major market around the world.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You’ll have the opportunity to choose between two dynamic tracks: **Client** and **Product**.

On the **Client** track, you'll immerse yourself in the complete client journey—from onboarding to expert advice delivery—sharpening your ability to enhance client relationships. You’ll learn from and collaborate with teams managing institutional and financial intermediary relationships, gaining insights and experiences that hone vital problem-solving, communication, and presentation skills.

On the **Product** track, you'll engage in the research, analysis, and development of innovative investment strategies and models, collaborating with top experts to serve global institutions, financial intermediaries, and individual investors. You'll have the opportunity to analyze portfolios, support senior investors, and liaise with internal partners, gaining technical skills and deepening your market and product knowledge.

If in the Asia Pacific region, specifically, you will have the unique opportunity to experience rotations in both tracks, broadening your expertise and enhancing your career development.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Key skills**

You should possess superior multi-tasking, organizational, and communication skills, plus the ability to leverage quantitative and qualitative resources to understand complex financial situations and present solutions.

Those interested in the **Client** track should demonstrate aptitude for sales and client relationship management, as well as strong communication and presentation skills. Individuals interesting in the **Product** track should show interest and fluency in financial markets and macro-level economic trends, exceptional analytical skills, high aptitudes for numbers and strong modelling skills.

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for highly-motivated students who are interested in learning about the asset management industry. You’ll need a genuine interest in financial markets, investing, and macro-level economics, as well as the ability to take initiative. You’ll also need good judgment, discretion when working with confidential information, and integrity.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

We offer four distinct tracks, each designed to cater to specific regional and functional focuses: Asia Pacific, Europe, North America – Client, and North America – Product.

- **Asia Pacific**: Participants are empowered to rotate across client and product teams, gaining broad exposure to our Asset Management line of business.
- **Europe**: Participants are aligned to an asset class, empowering them to become product experts in key roles, like Investment Specialist, Market Strategy, Portfolio Manager, Product Strategy & Development, Real Estate Asset Management & Acquisitions, Research and Trading.
- **North America – Client**: Participants engage in rotations that provide a deep understanding of our clients and the strategic support we offer them. Participants begin with a foundational rotation in Client Services, advance to a business management or strategy rotation to learn client channel skills, and conclude with a sales-focused rotation supporting our Advisory or Institutional business.
- **North America – Product**: Participants are aligned to an asset class, empowering them to become product experts in key roles, like Investment Specialist, Market Strategy, Portfolio Manager, Product Strategy & Development, Real Estate Asset Management & Acquisitions, Research and Trading.

#### **Training**

Our comprehensive training program begins with a regional orientation, followed by an immersive global training experience in New York. You’ll join others from around the world, gaining insights into our firm, learning how we conduct business, and developing both technical and professional skills. You'll have the opportunity to hear from senior executives and expand your global peer network. Throughout the program, you'll benefit from a blend of formal and informal training opportunities designed to set you up for success.

#### **Career progression**

Top performing participants who successfully complete the program may receive an offer for continued employment.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/asset-management-products-analyst/asset-management-products-analyst-inside-the-prog.jpg)

On the **Product** track, you'll engage in the research, analysis, and development of innovative investment strategies and

# Bournemouth Think Ahead Program

If you think a career in financial services is right for you, then join us for this week-long summer program to help hone your skills, build your network, and increase your industry exposure. You’ll walk away with a better understanding of how a global financial services firm is run, increased industry knowledge, and a network of peers and mentors to help you on your career path.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You’ll join us at a Bournemouth university for a week of intensive research, coursework, and first-hand experiences to help you learn how a large financial institution operates. You’ll gain experience and exposure to tasks and ideas that will help you enhance your future prospects, and work on an intensive team project designed to develop your research, presentation, time management, leadership, and collaboration skills.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for highly motivated and curious students who have one year of secondary school remaining and are interested in a career in financial services. Individuals with excellent teamwork, problem-solving, analytical, and creative thinking skills will do well in the program. We’re also looking for students who can think and work independently, as well as communicate effectively.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll participate in an extended group research project designed to test and develop your professional skill set. We partner with the university to offer courses to help you understand various aspects of financial services such as markets, cybersecurity, and products to enhance your knowledge and help with the project. At the end of the week, your team will present your solution to a panel of J.P. Morgan and industry experts.

#### **Career Progression**

With insight into the various career paths available, you’ll have an invaluable understanding of the business world and a head start on future opportunities at the firm. You may be fast-tracked for our apprenticeship program or asked to serve as a JPMorganChase ambassador with your university.

raw_text:

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/oap-summer-analyst#main)

# Operations Analyst Program

Operations is at the heart of running the firm efficiently and effectively, continuously delivering value for our clients. You’ll play an integral role as a part of the Operations function to deliver solutions while growing business-specific expertise.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the Program

### What you’ll do

In this nine-week internship, you will be hired directly into one of our high-performing operations teams. During the program, you will work in a transformative environment that develops your skills and knowledge while delivering core services to the firm and its clients.

Your responsibilities will vary based on your location and team assignment. You’ll help deliver key solutions to our clients in coordination with other teams. In addition, you will gain exposure to senior leaders and receive best-in-class training as part of a broader analyst community.

You will make an impact in a specific area within operations, which could include payments, trading services, lending services, client management, and many others. Some examples of the impact you could make are helping to manage the end-to-end client onboarding experience by coordinating with clients, being responsible for processing and investigating client transactions for a variety of products, providing data analytics and reporting support for operational functions, or helping support a trading desk by booking trades, tracking profits & losses, and monitoring overall asset health.

You’ll develop skills that will help you grow within Operations, while increasing your contributions to the team.

### Who we’re looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for motivated people who want to begin and develop their careers in Operations. We’re looking for people with diverse perspectives who are excited to contribute to their team's success, expand their knowledge of businesses and products, and drive our business forward.

#### **Key skills**

Individuals who are adaptable, analytical, and have good communication skills will succeed in the program. You will be working closely with your team and interacting with a diverse set of internal/external stakeholders. You should be excited to solve problems and work to resolve them quickly.

### What we offer

#### This program will give you the opportunity to learn about Operations and develop your career. You will be fully integrated within a high-performing team and make a clear impact on the organization and our clients.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll begin contributing to an operations team from day one, by owning key parts of the operations process, ranging from client management to transaction processing to asset servicing. While driving your core responsibilities, you will be growing your product expertise and looking for ways to innovate within the space.

In addition to on-the-job experience, you will be part of a community of other analysts and will attend social events, interact with senior leaders, and learn new skills.

#### **Training**

You’ll begin with an orientation training that will provide an overview of the firm, our lines of business, and technical skills. You’ll also focus on building leadership and communications skills and strengthening core competencies.

#### **Career progression**

You’ll finish the internship prepared for a career in operations. Many of our high-performing interns receive full-time offers.

raw_text:

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/military-uk#main)

EMEA Military & Athlete Programmes

Pursue your post-military or sporting career with a company that aligns with your values – determination, dedication, and service. You’ll have opportunities to put your skills to work and immediately make a difference in one of our many areas of operations. We’ll help you build your product and industry knowledge, make connections across the firm, and gain real-world experience that will help you take your career in any direction.

Programme locations: London, Bournemouth, Glasgow, Edinburgh, Dublin, Cork & Frankfurt

Stay informed about the 2026 programme:

[Register](https://jpmc.recsolu.com/app/collect/event/WKhuFz-Ar1s1Im0Z2DT7aA "JPMorgan Military Veterans Affairs - 2026 Programme- Registration of Interest, learn more") your interest to receive the latest updates about the 2026 Transition and Pathways Programme.

Join our Virtual Financial Services Taster Event:

[Sign up](https://jpmc.recsolu.com/app/collect/event/XWfdk0ePPrw9Q04Bw3TgGA "JPMorgan Military & Athlete Transition Programme - Financial Services Taster Virtual Event, learn more") for our upcoming Virtual Financial Services Taster Event on the 9-10th February.

Applications for the Military & Athlete Transition programme and Pathways will open on the 1st April 2026.

Applications for the Technical Programme are now closed.

## Inside the program

### Timelines

#### **Time frame and programmes**

Transition 6-month programme (Military and Athlete): The Transition Programme will run from October 2026 to 1 April 2027

Pathways 2-year rotation programme (Military only): The Pathways Programme will run from October 2026 to October 2028

Technical 9 month Programme (Military and Athlete): The Technical programme will run from July 2026 to March 2027

#### **Further details**

There are opportunities to apply for the **Markets & Investment Banking** Progressional programmes, once you have started on the Transition programme.

#### **Eligibility**

Military candidates: Have been in full time regular service for 3+ years.

Athlete candidates: Elite athletes that have competed at a senior level internationally or nationally, representing a professional sports team or their country for 3+ years. (We may ask your NGB for confirmation, if applicable.)

Both military and athlete candidates: Have been out of service or retired from sport no more than 2 years by start date of the programme and have the right to work in the location applying to.

#### **Upcoming events**

We will be offering an online Taster Event on the 9th and 10th of February 2026.

- Financial Services Taster Event: During our 2-day virtual Financial Services Taster Event, we will give you the opportunity to hear from Military and Athlete Programme Alumni who have transitioned from service and sport. You will hear first hand their personal experiences and receive overviews of what a day in the life in their roles is like.

We are also pleased to announce our Insight Days, which will be held in person at our J.P. Morgan offices, with one session available virtually. Registration links for these events will be released in the new year.

#### **Insight day dates and locations:**

- Bournemouth: 30th March
- Virtual: 31st March
- London: 1st April
- Glasgow: 7th April
- Dublin: 8th April

### What you’ll do

Following a bespoke week long induction, you will join your team and undertake a six-month placement in one of our lines of business. Your manager will set clear objectives and will support you in your learning journey as you grow your knowledge of the business. You will have opportunity to network across the organization and determine where your skills will be best applied.

You’ll also work to help us uphold our core values of integrity, determination, and leadership across the organization. We’re looking for those who can help us maintain our commitment to inclusion, innovation, and service.

### Who we’re looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We are seeking our next group of leaders who are results-oriented, thrive under pressure, and adapt easily to new environments.

We value individuals who are capable, demonstrate integrity, and maintain a client-focused approach. Ideal candidates are motivated self-starters who learn rapidly and exhibit strong judgment and discipline.

#### **Key skills**

We’re looking for those with excellent problem-solving and communication skills, who collaborate and work well with a team. You should be comfortable talking to anyone in the organization and flexible enough to take on new assignments and learn new terminology.

### What we offer

The breadth of opportunities and amount of support make our post-military and athletes program unique. We take the time to get to know you, understand your skills, and where you’d like to go in your career before placing you in a role. We offer insight evenings to help you learn more about the firm, understand the opportunities available, and discover how you can make a meaningful contribution across our organization.

We hire veterans and athletes of all backgrounds, ranks, and experiences for roles across the firm, including project management, risk, compliance, finance, sales and trading, asset management, operations, technology and business management.

You’ll make an impact on our business from the beginning by helping us solve real business challenges and taking responsibility for projects from beginning to end. Applying the skills

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll work with the different sub-lines of business. You’ll help analyze trends in the financial markets, build valuation models, evaluate different companies’ financing and risk management practices, dive into different wholesale banking products, provide cutting-edge data analytics to optimize business decisions, and develop end-to-end client-ready materials.

#### **Training**

Expert professionals will train new joiners on relevant technical skills required for the job. Take advantage of many training initiatives organized by the firm to enhance technical skills, soft skills, and personal well-being.

#### **Career progression**

You may rotate within the regional/global onshore teams within the first few years. Subsequent career opportunities may include moving to different businesses and different locations around the globe.

You'll have access to career mobility and career development platforms which will enable you to take advantage of the global network as well as develop relevant industry skills.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/cib-research-analytics-summer-analyst.jpg)

Increase your understanding of specific companies, industries, markets, and products while supporting our businesses in banking, markets, research, data analytics, and product teams. Over the years, you'll build a range of skills and work across the firm's businesses with a global team.

### Who we're looking for

We want curious and analytical minds.

#### **Valued qualities**

You should be a self-starter with a positive attitude and excellent attention to detail.

#### **Key skills**

You should have business acumen, communication and analytical skills, and a keen interest in developing a career in financial services.

You’ll help analyze trends in the financial markets, build valuation models, evaluate different companies’ financing and risk management practices, dive into different wholesale banking products, provide cutting-edge data analytics to optimize business decisions, and develop end-to-end client-ready materials.

#### **Training**

# Global Payments Analyst Program

Be at the forefront of innovation in payments, trade, and finance solutions. Our program puts you at the lead of executing comprehensive client solutions for multinational corporations, financial institutions, and public sector clients.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

Through this rotational program, you’ll join a globally renowned business that processes nearly $10 trillion in payments daily and learn how we do it. You’ll have the opportunity to work on the products that power this massive movement across 120+ currencies, and you’ll have exposure to how we work with clients to ensure we’re providing efficient, effective, and world-class service.

### Who we're looking for

Creative and adaptable problem-solvers with strong analytical and communication skills, the ability to learn quickly, and a strong grasp of business fundamentals will thrive in this role.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll embark on a dynamic journey through three diverse rotations, each offering a unique perspective on the world of payments. You'll have the opportunity to delve into areas such as product development, design, trading, and relationship building, while collaborating with global clients across a variety of industries. By the end of the program, you'll emerge with a comprehensive and well-rounded understanding of the top merchant acquirer in the United States equipped with the skills and insights to excel in your career.

#### **Training**

You’ll complete three weeks of in-depth training focused on our various products, as well as technical skillset training, before launching into three eight-month rotations. This supportive environment complements on-the-job instruction, guided by managers and mentors. You’ll also continue your development in the program through ongoing assigned trainings over the course of the two years.

#### **Career Progression**

High-performing participants are well-positioned for promotional advancement following completion of the program. Additionally, this opportunity prepares participants for success in the long term, helping to advance careers to the next level in Payments and beyond.

raw_text:

[![Close Icon](https://www.chase.com/etc/designs/chase-ux/css/img/icon_close.svg)](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/wealth-management-fulltime-financial-advisor-development-program#)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/wealth-management-fulltime-financial-advisor-development-program#main)

# Financial Advisor   Development Program

We’re looking for highly motivated people to become Financial Advisors. You’ll be part of a dynamic team that aims to help individuals achieve their financial goals and a program that will give you the skills and the opportunity to obtain licenses for a career providing financial advice. Following program completion, you will have the skills, experience, training, and support to successfully launch your career as a Financial Advisor.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You’ll learn the fundamentals of working with clients in a service-oriented role. Our team will provide in-depth industry training and analysts will have active client engagement in our Client Service Center. You’ll enhance your service skills and gain the experience needed for future success as a Financial Advisor.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for highly motivated individuals with a passion for client service and an interest in building a foundational career in wealth management and providing financial advice.

#### **Key skills**

You should have exceptional analytical, interpersonal and communication skills, integrity, and the motivation to develop.

### What we offer

In this two-year accelerated program, you’ll gain investment knowledge and enhance your client service skills through a progression of roles in the Client Service Center. You’ll do meaningful work building trust with our clients and obtain a suite of FINRA licenses to provide both service and eventually financial planning and advice when graduating from the program as a Financial Advisor. Your experience in the client service center is a benefit to your future career as a Financial Advisor.

#### **On-the-job experience**

Your experience will include providing customer support, identifying client needs, initiating trades in capital markets, assisting with brokerage accounts, and completing service-oriented transactions. Obtaining FINRA licenses SIE, Series 7, Series 63, and Series 66/65 is a program requirement.

#### **Training**

Over the course of the program, you’ll have access to professional development, leadership development, financial planning, and advice development training. Additionally, you’ll have access to networking, business deep dives, one-on-one mentorship, and a dedicated financial advisor mentor.

#### **Career progression**

After successfully completing the full-time program, highly motivated analysts will have the opportunity to compete for Financial Advisor positions in the J.P. Morgan Wealth Management Remote Advice Channel. The experience and mentorship gained through participation in the Financial Advisor Development Program provides you with the industry knowledge, experience, and client service background to be in the best position to successfully compete and gain these roles. If selected, you’ll work on a team offering comprehensive financial planning and advice to clients by helping identify appropriate products/services that meet their financial needs. As a Financial Advisor, you will have immediate access to additional development and the opportunity to take The Wealth Management Certified Professional (WMCP) designation within your first year as a Financial Advisor.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/wealth-management-fulltime-financial-advisor.jpg)

You’ll enhance your service skills and gain the experience needed for future success as a Financial Advisor.

#### **Key skills**

Job Description

**Location: Largo, FL**

**Job Type: Full-Time \| 1st Shift (Mon-Fri: 6AM - 2:30PM + Saturday OT as required)**

**Compensation: $18.18/hour + Benefits + Overtime opportunities**

**About the Role**

Are you a hands-on problem solver who enjoys working with tools and being part of a fast-paced team? Join us as a Machine Operator and help build high-quality air conditioning units that keep homes and businesses comfortable year-round.  Our 1st shift roles available include Brake Press, CNC Turret, and Insulation. These are great opportunities to develop your skills in a supportive, safety-first environment with plenty of room for advancement.

**What We Offer**

- Competitive salary

- Paid vacation, holidays, and sick time

- Comprehensive benefits: medical, dental, vision, and 401(k)

- On-the-job training and cross-training opportunities

- A collaborative, safety-first culture with a Zero Harm policy

- Opportunities for career growth and development


**What You will Do**

You will operate and perform basic machine set-ups with minimal supervision, ensuring efficient and accurate production in the hard tool and lights-out areas. You will also fabricate, cut, shape, drill, and form metal workpieces to meet precise specifications and maintain continuous production aligned with established quality standards, and you will receive comprehensive training and certification to operate a variety of machines, including but not limited to Brake Press, Turret, Hairpin, Fin Press, and Expander.

**How you will do it**

- Knowledgeable in basic set-up and operation of metal fabricating machines to cut, bend, and form metal plates, sheets, and structural shapes according to blueprints and specifications.

- Read and interpret blueprints, engineering specifications, engineering drawings, and shop orders to determine machine setup, production methods, sequence of operations, and material requirements.

- Safety Awareness - Take responsibility for his or her own safety as well as the safety of others.  Understand the proper processes/equipment in the department.  Knowledge of JSAs, PPE usage, 5S, and all safety regulations in the facility.  Responsible for overall cleanliness of working area.

- Quality - Detect and report defective materials or questionable conditions to the department supervisors.  Perform first article inspection for conformance to specifications both blue print and work order.

- Teamwork – Build teamwork by working with other departments, shifts, and management to meet and exceed customer expectations.  Communicate regularly and effectively with co-workers, utilities, coordinators, and management and reports problems with safety, quality, processes, equipment, materials, and/or questionable conditions to the Department Supervisor.  Display professionalism in the workplace, able to perform duties during times of stress and pressure.

- Continuous Improvement – Ability to manage time and deliver results.  Ability to troubleshoot and provide solutions with the understanding of value-added versus non-value added activities and improve the ratio.  Ability to collect and understand production data corresponding to the machinery.

- Flexibility.  Availability to work overtime and/or move between shifts for training and/or coverage purposes to support production as needed.

- Demonstrated ability to use computer, utilizing software such as Microsoft Office, Microsoft Excel, Microsoft Word, and ERP systems.  Relevant knowledge to look up blueprints, production orders, key parts into inv

**What you will do**

The Solution Development Engineer is a member of the sales support team for thedivisionFire and Security and reports to the Solution Development Manager. In this role you work closely with the sales- and operations team to developthe solidproposals and present the best possible security solution to the customer.  You will work within a dynamic environment where high quality and innovative solutions are the standard.

Job Description

**Johnson Controls**is a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings. With operations in over 150 countries, we are committed to driving innovation and excellence in building technologies and integrated solutions. Our people are at the heart of our success, and we are passionate about attracting and developing top talent across the globe. We are looking for a **Technical Engineer Contact Center.**

**What will you do?**

The **Technical Engineer – Contact Center** plays a key role in designing, implementing, supporting, and optimizing the technologies that enable our customer experience operations. You will work closely with cross‑functional teams—including IT, Customer Service, Vendor Partners, and Operations—to ensure our contact center platforms are reliable, scalable, and continuously improving.

**How will you do it?**

- Provide advanced technical support for contact center infrastructure (voice, chat, IVR, ACD, integrations, call recording, etc.).

- Troubleshoot Tier 2/Tier 3 issues, perform root‑cause analysis, and implement long‑term fixes.

- Configure, maintain, and optimize contact center platforms (routing logic, workflows, user provisioning, telephony settings).

- Monitor system performance and proactively identify issues before they impact operations.

- Participate in or lead deployments of new features, upgrades, migrations, and technology enhancements.

- Support integrations with CRMs, ticketing systems, reporting tools, and automation solutions.

- Create detailed technical documentation and collaborate with stakeholders on solution design.

- Analyze call flows and performance metrics to recommend operational and technical improvements.


**What are we looking for?**

- Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Engineering, Telecommunications, or related field.

- +3 years of experience in a technical support, systems engineering, or contact center engineering role.

- Experience with at least one major contact center platform (e.g., Genesys Cloud, Cisco UCCE, Avaya Aura, Five9, NICE inContact, Amazon Connect).

- Knowledge of network fundamentals, VoIP, SIP, routing, and telephony concepts.

- Familiarity with APIs, scripting, or automation (Python, PowerShell, JSON) is a plus.

- Understanding of workforce management, call routing strategies, and reporting analytics.

- Experience integrating CCaaS solutions with CRM or ITSM platforms.

- Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently.

- Customer‑centric mindset with a focus on reliability and quality.


**Whatdowe offer?**

- Comprehensive benefits package.

- Opportunities for professional growth and development.

- Supportive and inclusive work environment.

- Commitment to work-life balance and employee well-being.

- Engagement in meaningful projects that make a difference.


**Johnson Controls does not request pregnancy or HIV testing as a condition for hiring, continued employment, or promotion**,in accordance withits commitment to labor equality and non-discrimination.

Job Description

Job Description

**Location: Largo, FL**

**Job Type: Full-Time \| 1st Shift (Mon-Fri: 7AM – 3:30PM)**

**Compensation: $17.16/hour + Benefits + Overtime opportunities**

**About the Role**

Are you a hands-on problem solver who enjoys working with tools and being part of a fast-paced team? Join us as an Assembler and help build high-quality air conditioning units that keep homes and businesses comfortable year-round. We are hiring for both our Sub Assembly and Final Assembly teams. This is a great opportunity to grow your skills in a supportive, safety-first environment with plenty of room for advancement.

**What You’ll Do**

- Assemble air conditioning units using hand and power tools

- Follow blueprints and engineering drawings to build subassemblies and final products

- Maintain a clean, safe, and organized work area

- Inspect parts for defects and report issues

- Collaborate with your team to meet production goals

- Support continuous improvement by identifying ways to streamline processes

- Use basic computer tools (Excel, Word) to track production and inventory

- Be flexible with overtime and shift changes as needed


**What You Bring**

- High school diploma or GED

- Experience in a manufacturing or industrial setting

- Strong attention to detail and ability to follow instructions

- Physically able to lift up to 50 lbs, stand for long periods, and work in a non-climate-controlled environment

- Comfortable with repetitive tasks in a noisy and dusty setting


**Preferred:**

- Strong communication and teamwork skills


**What We Offer**

- Competitive salary

- Paid vacation, holidays, and sick time

- Comprehensive benefits: medical, dental, vision, and 401(k)

- On-the-job training and cross-training opportunities

- A collaborative, safety-first culture with a Zero Harm policy

- Opportunities for career growth and development


Work Environment

The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this Job, the employee is regularly exposed to work near moving mechanical parts; fumes or airborne particles and vibration. The noise level in the work environment is usually loud.

Physical Requirements

The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this Job, the employee is regularly required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms. The employee is frequently required to stand and walk. The employee is occasionally required to climb or balance; stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl and talk or hear. The employee must regularly lift and /or move up to 10 pounds and occasionally lift and/or move up to 50 pounds.

HPT

Must actively participate in all high-performance team activities.

**THE ABOVE DESCRIPTION OUTLINES THE MOST SIGNICANT JOB FUNCTIONS PERFORMED BUT MAY INCLUDE OTHER SIMILAR FUNCTIONS OR ASSIGNMENTS NOT SPECIFICALLY MENTIONED.**

Job Description

**Apprentice Installation Engineer - Fire**

📍 Basildon- this is a field-based role covering the Kent and Southeast area. You may be required to travel further afield.

_To get on this prestigious apprenticeship programme we require 5’s or C’s in your GCSE English and Maths. Please include your certificates in your application alongside your UK Driving licence._

**Ready to spark your future? 🔥🔐**

Join Johnson Controls and dive into the world of fire safety technology! We’re talking fire detection systems- fire alarms, fire panels and other fire safety equipment. You will also learn about security alarms, CCTV and access control. All the cool stuff that keeps people safe.

**What You’ll Be Up To:**

- Embark on a thrilling journey as you install, commission, and maintain electronic systems designed to protect people, properties, and assets.
- Gain in-depth knowledge about our cutting-edge systems, focusing on fire detection whilst also learning about intrusion detection, CCTV, and access control solutions.
- Hit the road visiting a variety of sites, from residential to commercial and retail locations.
- Collaborate with a dedicated team, absorbing invaluable skills that will pave the way to becoming a qualified fire and security engineer.

**Who We Are**

Johnson Controls is a global leader in technology and industry, serving customers in over 150 countries. We create intelligent buildings, energy-efficient solutions, and integrated infrastructure that drive progress. As an apprentice, you'll be part of our mission to shape a better tomorrow making buildings safer, smarter and more sustainable. Learn more about us [here](https://www.johnsoncontrols.com/).

**Where You’ll Train:**

🎓 Banham Academy

📚 Level 3 Fire Emergency & Security Systems Technician

🧠 Mix of college learning and hands-on mentoring. College is in a block format

**What You’ll Need:**

✅ GCSEs in English & Maths-Grade 5 (C) or above

✅ Full UK driving licence

✅ Comfortable working at heights and in all weather conditions

✅ Good Physical health, the job can be labour intensive at times

✅ Happy to travel, work away and stay over in hotels when needed

✅ Good vibes: team spirit, hardworking, communication skills, and a love for technology and Engineering

**Perks & Pay:**

💰 £17,160 starting salary

🛠️ All equipment + uniform + gear

📅 25 days holiday + bank holidays

💼 Access to Company pension scheme, overtime and travel payments where applicable

**Next Steps:**

📧 Send your CV, driving licence, English and Maths certificates- Grade 5 or C’s

📞 If your application stands out, you will have a chat with our Talent Acquisition team

🛠️ Smash the screening call and you will be invited to attend an assessment centre

🎉 Start your journey in late summer 2026!

**Deadline to apply:** Friday 10th April 2026
