Job Description

**What You Will Do**

Johnson Controls is seeking a UI Developer to join our Solution Navigator team. In this role, you will design, develop, and enhance user interface components for a large-scale, enterprise B2B ecommerce platform built on Salesforce.

You will work as a hands-on contributor, partnering with UI/UX designers, product owners, and senior engineers to deliver reliable, accessible, and scalable UI features. This role is ideal for a developer who is comfortable owning feature-on contributor, partnering with UI/UX designers, product owners, and senior engineers to deliver reliable, accessible, and scalable UI features. This role is ideal for a developer who is comfortable owning featurelevel UI work, contributing within established patterns, and continuing to grow their expertise-level UI work, contributing within established patterns, and continuing to grow their expertise in Salesforce Lightning Web Components and modern frontend development.

**Responsibilities**

· Develop, enhance, and maintain UI components using Salesforce Lightning Web Components (LWC).

· Implement UI features using JavaScript, HTML, CSS, jQuery, Angular and React related frontend technologies.

· Support feature development across Salesforce Experience Cloud applications.

· Translate UI/UX designs, user stories, and requirements into functional, responsive and high -quality UI components.

· Work within established UI standards, component libraries, and design systems.

· Collaborate closely with designers, product owners, QA, and senior developers during feature delivery.

· Participate actively in an Agile/Scrum development process, including sprint planning, standups, reviews, and retrospectives.

· Debug and resolve UI defects, performance issues, and cross-browser-browser compatibility concerns.

· Assist with integrating UI components with Salesforce data, Apex logic, and APIs.

· Contribute to code reviews, following best practices for quality, maintainability, and accessibility.

· Write and maintain basic unit tests for UI components as part of regular development work.

· Continuously improve skills by staying current with Salesforce releases and modern UI development trends.

· Build and manage Design Systems Manager (DSM).

**Required Qualifications**

· Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience).

· 3–5 years of professional experience in UI or frontend development.

· Hands-on experience developing with Salesforce Lightning Web Components (LWC).

· Strong proficiency in JavaScript, HTML, CSS, jQuery, Angular and React

· Experience developing and supporting UI features within an Agile delivery framework.

· Familiarity with Salesforce fundamentals, including objects, data models, and security concepts.

· Experience using Git-based version control systems-based version control systems.

· Experience working in Agile/Scrum environments, using tools such as Azure Dev Ops.

· Experience working with Angular.

· Ability to independently deliver UI features while collaborating effectively with cross-functional-functional teams.

· Strong attention to detail and commitment to high q-quality user experiences.

**Preferred Qualifications**

· Experience with Salesforce Experience Cloud (Communities).

· Experience supporting enterprise or B2B applications, including ecommerce platforms.

· Familiarity with frontend testing tools (e.g., Jest or similar).

· Familiarity with Figma Dev.

· Understanding accessibility standards (WCAG) and responsive design principles.

· Experience maintaining or enhancing UI features in existing, large-scale codebases.

Certifications (Nice to Have)

· Certified Salesforce Lightning Web Components Developer

· Salesforce Platform Deve

Job Description

**Field Project Commissioning Engineer for Coolant Distribution Units (CDU’s)**

As a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings, our mission is to reimagine the performance of buildings to serve people, places, and the planet.  Join a winning team that enables you to build your best future! Our teams are uniquely positioned to support a multitude of industries across the globe. You will have the opportunity to develop yourself through meaningful work projects and learning opportunities. We strive to provide our employees with an experience, focused on supporting their physical, financial, and emotional wellbeing. Become a member of the Johnson Controls family and thrive in an empowering company culture where your voice and ideas will be heard – your next great opportunity is just a few clicks away!

**What we offer**

- Competitive salary

- Paid vacation/holidays/sick time - 15 days of vacation, 10 paid holidays, and five sick days each year

- Comprehensive benefits package including 401(K) with match as well as insurance coverage that starts on day one (medical, dental, and vision care)

- Encouraging and collaborative team environment

- Dedication to safety through our Zero Harm policy


**What you will do**

Partnering with the CDU Operation Managers, Project Managers, Superintendents and Sub-contractors, you will support CDU installations, customer training, and company operations for customers and staff.

**How you will do it**

- Based initially in Manassas Virginia to support the installation and commissioning of  several phases of CDU installations. This role may subsequently extend to other North American territories

- Provide support of highly technical and sophisticated custom products.

- Achieve and exceed Customer Satisfaction objectives.

- Perform standard routine procedures to isolate and fix problems in malfunctioning equipment or software.

- Strong communication and interpersonal skills with ability to be flexible to adjust to changing field conditions.

- Understand project plans and specifications.

- Attend all startup and commissioning meetings.

- Verify that necessary Critical Documents are obtained, delivered and/or posted as required.

- Enforce company safety regulations, standard operating procedures, and quality standards.

- Work with customers, crews, and sub- contractors to resolve operational and technical issues and challenges.

- Provide static/less dynamic programming, commissioning, and troubleshooting of building control and facility management systems.

- Troubleshoot system problems. Calibrate systems requiring basic electronic test equipment.

- Perform job surveys for estimating and sales as required.

- Actively own the responsibility of writing and submitting Engineering Change Requests (ECR) to ensure the intended functionality, integrity, and safety of the product on site.

- Initiate internal investigations with on-site personnel as well as internal engineering departments to respond to submitted Requests for Information (RFI) of the product during construction/startup.

- Provide detailed and empirical data-driven reports that are structured in response to incidents that occur as well as scheduled field-witness testing.

- Attend and report all Craning/Rigging activities on site with timelines and any noted damages. Compensation is considered for these activities when extending beyond normal business/site hours.

- Established as a field point of contact for programming and software/firmware deployment packages and updates.

- Field startup and construction that requires programming activities can be validated (Point-to-Point) and signed off for completion by the Field Engineer.

- Serve

Job Description

Wir suchen aktuell in der Region Süddeutschland einen **Sales Operations Mitarbeiter (m/w/d).**

**Ihre Aufgaben:**

• Regelmäßige Überprüfung der Pipeline mit den Teilregionen, um Datenqualität und Fortschritt der Chancen sicherzustellen.

• Unterstützung der regionalen Manager durch Pflege sauberer Pipelines und Identifikation von Lücken oder Engpässen.

• Zusammenarbeit mit Vertriebsteams zur Entwicklung neuer Kampagnenregeln und Verfeinerung der Zielgruppenlogik.

• Analyse von Kampagnenergebnissen (z. B. Konversionsraten, Kampagnenleistung, Ablehnungsgründe) und Identifikation von Verbesserungsmöglichkeiten.

• Vertrauensvoller Partner für regionale Vertriebsteams, um Leads voranzubringen und Chancen zu konvertieren.

**Ihre Qualifikationen:**

• Erfahrung in Vertriebsoperationen, Vertriebsadministration oder Sales Enablement.

• Vertrautheit mit ERP-Systemen und geschäftsrelevanten Feldern für die Installationsbasis.

• Starke analytische Fähigkeiten zur Interpretation von Kampagnendaten und Ableitung umsetzbarer Verbesserungen.

• Erfahrung oder Bereitschaft zur Schulung in Lead-Generierungsplattformen (z. B. Installationsbasis).

• Ausgezeichnete Kommunikations- und Kooperationsfähigkeiten über regionale und funktionsübergreifende Teams hinweg.

• Ehrgeiz, sich in eine breitere Vertriebsmanagement- oder strategische Enablement-Rolle weiterzuentwickeln.

**Was wir Ihnen bieten:**

**Unsere Kultur:** Eine kollegiale Atmosphäre in einem internationalen Konzernumfeld

**Ihre Vergütung:** Leistungsgerecht und marktkonform

**Ihre Einarbeitung:** Umfangreich und intensiv – ein detaillierter Einarbeitungsplan an Ihre Erfahrung angepasst

**Ihre Weiterbildung:** Nutzen Sie unsere interne Trainingsbibliothek und fachspezifische Kurse je nach Bedarf

**Ihre Absicherung:** Eine Unfallversicherung 24/7, die im beruflichen und privaten Umfeld greift

**Ihre Benefits:** Nutzen Sie interessante Angebote von Corporate Benefits, gerne unterstützt Johnson Controls auch Ihre ehrenamtlichen Tätigkeiten oder unterstützt Sie mit der Zahlung von vermögenswirksamen Leistungen

Job Description

**Build your best future with the Johnson Controls team**

As a global leader in smart, healthy and sustainable buildings, our mission is to reimagine the performance of buildings to serve people, places and the planet.  Join a winning team that enables you to build your best future! Our teams are uniquely positioned to support a multitude of industries across the globe. You will have the opportunity to develop yourself through meaningful work projects and learning opportunities. We strive to provide our employees with an experience, focused on supporting their physical, financial, and emotional wellbeing. Become a member of the Johnson Controls family and thrive in an empowering company culture where your voice and ideas will be heard – your next great opportunity is just a few clicks away!

**What we offer:**

- Competitive salary

- Paid vacation/holidays/sick time

- Comprehensive benefits package including 401K, medical, dental, and vision care

- On the job/cross training opportunities

- Encouraging and collaborative team environment

- Dedication to safety through our Zero Harm policy


**What you will do:**

We are seeking a highly analytical and action-oriented Sales Intelligence Analyst to design and deliver commercial analytics that drive strategy, performance, and behavior across our global organization. In this role, you will create scalable tools, generate meaningful insights, and partner closely with leadership to accelerate commercial impact. You will thrive in a collaborative environment that values curiosity, continuous improvement, and data-driven decision-making.

- Design, develop, and deploy commercial analytics tools that support strategic initiatives across the Global Commercial team.

- Lead and support change management efforts tied to new tools, processes, and systems

- Query and model data from Snowflake and other sources to support both ongoing and ad hoc business needs.

- Analyze business performance and market trends, delivering insights that shape action plans and support annual targets.

- Provide clear, concise insights to regional teams and stakeholders across the business.

- Expand and enhance self-service analytics capabilities across the organization.

- Support additional projects and responsibilities as assigned.


**How You Will Do It:**

- Apply strong analytical and strategic thinking to refine questions, frame business needs, and guide decisions with data.

- Use advanced Power BI development skills to translate complex data into intuitive, action-oriented dashboards.

- Leverage SQL and Snowflake expertise to quickly pull data for ad hoc requests

- Communicate complex technical insights in clear, business-friendly language to influence decisions at all levels.

- Utilize strong business acumen to ensure analytics align with commercial objectives and long-range goals.

- Build strong partnerships across functional groups, regions, and leadership teams to ensure analytics drive real outcomes.

- Bring creativity, resourcefulness, and a self-motivated approach to solving problems and advancing analytics maturity.


**What We Look For:**

**Required**

- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Mathematics, or a related field

- 3+ years of experience in data analytics

- 3+ years of experience with a BI platform (Power BI strongly preferred)

- Proficiency in SQL and Snowflake


**Preferred**

- Strong Power BI development skills and experience building scalable dashboards

- Strong ETL and data modeling capabilities, including comfort with Excel, SQL, and data transformation

- Solid understanding of business performance KPIs and commercial levers

- Ability to influence and support change across diverse teams

- Strong org

Job Description

**What you will do**

Johnson Controls Inc is looking for forward-thinking talent to join our growing organization. During these unprecedented times, JCI is committed to the health and safety of all employees, customers,partnersand the communities we serve.

At JohnsonControlsyou’llhave the opportunity to work on some of the most exciting and relevant building automation technologies in today’s market. Our talented people empower us, and we believe in being part of a team that is open, collaborative, results-oriented,hardworkingand above all fun.

Are you looking to gainHeating, Ventilation, & Air Condition (HVAC) knowledgeand learn firsthand from industry leading professionals? Our team will give you this opportunity, in addition, we provide factory certification through our Service Technical Academy (STA). We will equip you with tools and a vehicle to perform your jobatthe highest standard. Provide our customers with the highest level of service to solve building inefficiencies and resolveservice relateditems. As a trusted advisor to ourcustomersyou will provide solutions and outcomes at the time of service

**Howyou willdo it**

- Apprentices will be trained and will perform on HVAC mechanical equipment and controls on client sites.


- Apprentices willparticipatein certifiedtrainingthrough Service Technical Academy that will build skill sets toprovidecontinued opportunities for recognition, monetaryrewardsand advancement.


- Through coaching and mentorship, apprentices will develop skills to troubleshoot and resolve both technical issues as well as customer needs.


- You will keep JCI, contractors, and the customer advised of job progress and issues as needed.


- You will meet attendance requirements for required Union training for first year.


- Provide recommendations and solutions to ensurefacilityis running efficiently to include upselling opportunities.


- Provide quotes to support recommendations and solutions.


**What we look for**

- Must be a member or willing to join the United Association of Journeymen and Apprentices of the Plumbing and Pipe Fitting Industry of the United States and Canada, AFL-CIO.


- 1+ years of industry experience or experience in servicing mechanical systems in the HVAC industry.


- Generally required knowledge includes mechanical HVAC systems, facilities management systems, site preparation, peripheral equipmentinstallationand servicing techniques


- Effective interpersonal skills toproperly representJohnson Controls to customers and other outside contacts in an ethical and professional manner.


- Leadership skills to support the activities of Mechanics working on non-complex, non-critical mechanical HVAC equipment on a job site


- Self-starter withabilityto eventually work independently.


- Prioritize safety in all forms.


- This position requires a valid driver’s license.


**Preferred**

1-2 years of trade school or college in a technical emphasis.

Pay Range: hourly ratedeterminedby the CBA/rate sheet. Benefits are provided through a local labor union aligned with a collective bargaining agreement. The posted salary range reflects the target compensation for this role. However, we recognize that exceptional candidates may bring unique skills and experiences that exceed the typical profile. If you believe your background warrants consideration beyond the stated range, we encourage you to apply. To support an efficient and fair hiring process, we may use technologyassistedtools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to helpidentifyand evaluate candidates. All hiring decisions areultimately madeby human reviewers.

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

_The Nordic HR team is expanding, and we are seeking a proactive HR & Data Business Partner to join our team in Aarhus._

As an HR & Data Business Partner, you will play a critical role in driving a strategic, data-driven HR agenda across the Nordic region and lines of business, in close collaboration with the UK&I team. You will ensure that HR maintains a strong and comprehensive overview of key business KPIs, translating data into clear, actionable insights for HR Advisors and relevant stakeholders throughout the region. In addition, you will partner with the broader HR organization and business leadership to drive alignment across countries, share best practices, and ensure consistent, high-impact HR execution in support of shared regional and global objectives.

This position is based at our Holme, Aarhus office; however, you will regularly collaborate online with managers and colleagues across the Nordic and EMEA region.

**Key Responsibilities**

- Act as a trusted advisor to leaders, coaching them to strengthen management capabilities and maximize the use of HR digital tools and self-service solutions (including AI solutions).
- Develop dashboards and reports using HR analytics; gather and interpret data from systems such as Workday and Power BI to support strategic planning, identify risks, and translate metrics into actionable insights (e.g., employee turnover, absence, time-to-hire, performance, and engagement).
- Exercise proactive leadership in driving key talent management initiatives, including workforce planning, onboarding, succession planning, retention strategies, development programs, and inclusion efforts.
- Support organizational design and change management through job profiling, structural planning, and workforce analysis.
- Align compensation and benefits practices with the Total Rewards calendar to ensure consistent implementation.
- Partner with Talent Acquisition to support business growth and resource planning.
- Stay up to date on HR trends, employment legislation, and business developments to provide proactive, customer-focused HR support.
- Build strong relationships with stakeholders across the business to ensure alignment with HR programs and initiatives.

**What We Look For**

- A strong interest in HR data, with a passion for working with numbers and conducting data analysis.
- Advanced user of Excel and Power BI, or comparable business intelligence tools.
- A degree in Human Resources, Economics, or a related field.
- HR experience combined with a passion for data-driven work.
- Comfort working across multiple platforms and analyzing data from various sources.
- Previous experience in a commercial or industrial business environment is an advantage.
- Experience in a global, matrixed organization with a customer-centric approach is beneficial.
- Strong technical HR expertise and the ability to establish credibility with senior stakeholders.
- Solid experience in compensation & benefits, as well as learning & development.
- Strong communication skills in Danish and English, both written and spoken

**What We Offer**

- Attractive salary package and health insurance
- 11% employer-paid pension
- Flexible working hours (with the possibility to work from home 1-2 days per week)
- Excellent development and career opportunities, including access to over 5,000 e-learning programs
- Excellent canteen, on-site hairdresser/barber, fitness facilities, and regular social activities
- Competitive parental leave and child sick day policies, plus access to a global parent network
- Opportunity to be part of a global HR team focused on knowledge sharing and innovation
- Employment in a global, innovative company with over 130 years of history

**Application**

Job Description

## En ADT, líder mundial en sistemas de seguridad, buscamos un Técnico de Campo para unirse a nuestro equipo en Capital Federal.

## Serás el responsable de instalar, mantener y reparar sistemas de seguridad en diversas instalaciones, garantizando la satisfacción del cliente y el óptimo funcionamiento de los equipos. Este puesto es fundamental para asegurar que nuestros clientes estén protegidos con la tecnología más avanzada.

## Responsabilidades:

- ## Instalar sistemas de alarma, cámaras de seguridad (CCTV) y controles de acceso, siguiendo los estándares de calidad y seguridad establecidos.

- ## Realizar mantenimiento preventivo y correctivo, asegurando el correcto funcionamiento de todos los sistemas de seguridad instalados.

- ## Atender los llamados de servicio, realizar diagnósticos y resolver problemas técnicos en el campo.

- ## Documentar las intervenciones realizadas, incluyendo reportes de servicios y materiales utilizados.

- ## Colaborar con el equipo de soporte técnico para mejorar los procesos y soluciones ofrecidas a los clientes.


## Requisitos:

- ## Analítico Secundario en mano o de estudios superiores. (excluyente)

- ## Experiencia mínima de 1 año en instalación y mantenimiento de sistemas de seguridad, preferiblemente en alarmas y CCTV. (deseable)

- ## Licencia de conducir con una antigüedad mayor a 2 años. (excluyente)

- ## Disponibilidad para trabajar de Lunes a Viernes de 9 a 18 hs, con posibilidad de extensión.


## Ofrecemos:

- ## Un ambiente de trabajo dinámico y colaborativo.

- ## Oportunidades de desarrollo profesional y capacitación continua.

- ## Contrato directo con la multinacional, bono mensual variable y paquete de beneficios LifePlus.

- ## Vehículo y herramientas de trabajo brindadas por la empresa para el desempeño de tus funciones.

Job Description

### 🚨 ¡ÚNETE A ADT SECURITY SERVICES! 🚨

**Buscamos Agentes de Monitoreo de Alarmas** para nuestra central ubicada en **Providencia, Santiago**.

Si tienes vocación de servicio, atención al detalle y te interesa el mundo de la seguridad, ¡esta oportunidad es para ti!

### 🛠️ ¿Qué harás?

Serás responsable de **gestionar las señales recibidas en nuestra central de monitoreo**, siguiendo los protocolos establecidos para proteger a nuestros clientes.

### 📋 Condiciones del cargo:

- **Jornada de 45 horas semanales**

- **Trabajo 100% presencial**

- **Contrato a plazo fijo con opción a indefinido**

- **Turnos rotativos en modalidad 6x1 y 5x2**, con horarios como:

  - Mañana: 06:30 / 07:00 hrs

  - Tarde: 13:30 / 14:00 / 12:00 / 12:30 hrs

  - Noche: 21:30 / 22:00 hrs

    _(Contamos con traslado corporativo para turnos con ingreso o salida entre las 22:00 y 07:00 hrs)_

### ✅ Requisitos:

- Enseñanza media completa

- Experiencia en atención al cliente

- Manejo de PC a nivel usuario


### 🎁 Beneficios:

- Contrato directo con ADT

- Excelente ambiente laboral

- Estabilidad y desarrollo profesional


### 📍 Ubicación: Providencia, Santiago

¡Sé parte de una empresa líder en seguridad electrónica y haz la diferencia cada día!

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

Sie möchten Verantwortung übernehmen, Ihr technisches Know-how einbringen und in einem internationalen Umfeld durchstarten? Werden Sie Teil unseres Neuanlagen-Teams der Gebäudeautomation in **Kesselsdorf** und gestalten Sie die Zukunft der Gebäudetechnik mit!

Nach einer umfassenden Einarbeitung übernehmen Sie selbstständig Projekte, arbeiten eng mit unseren Kunden und Projektleitern zusammen und sorgen für die reibungslose technische Umsetzung.

## **Ihre Aufgaben**

- **Programmierung und Konfiguration** von MSR-Anlagen (z. B. Gebäudeleittechnik-Systeme wie ADS / ADX / OWS, Feldregler und Automatisierungssysteme mit einfachen Regelkreisen).

- **Enge Zusammenarbeit** mit dem Projektleiter zur Sicherstellung aller technischen Anforderungen.

- **Inbetriebnahme** der Anlagen beim Kunden, inklusive Übergabe und Einweisung für den weiteren Betrieb.


## **Ihr Profil**

- Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung in **Automatisierungstechnik**, **Betriebstechnik**, **MSR-Technik** oder eine vergleichbare technische Qualifikation.

- Erfahrung in der **Inbetriebnahme** von MSR-Anlagen.

- Gute Programmierungskenntnisse wünschenswert.

- Ausgeprägte **Kommunikationsfähigkeiten** und Teamorientierung.


## **Das bieten wir Ihnen**

- **Attraktive Vergütung**: Leistungsgerechte Bezahlung, angelehnt an den IG Metall-Tarifvertrag.

- **Flexibles Arbeiten**: 35-Stunden-Woche, 30 Tage Urlaub und keine Kernarbeitszeit – Sie gestalten Ihren Arbeitstag!

- **Firmenfahrzeug** zur privaten Nutzung.

- **Umfassendes Onboarding**: Ein 2-tägiges Willkommensprogramm und ein strukturierter 10-Wochen-Onboarding-Programm

- **Weiterentwicklung**: Zugang zu einer internen Trainingsbibliothek und fachspezifischen Kursen nach Bedarf.

- **Gesundheit und Mobilität**: Günstige Konditionen für ein Firmen-Fahrrad-Leasing.

- **Absicherung**: Umfassende Unfallversicherung, die auch privat greift.

- **Vergünstigungen**: Corporate Benefits und Altersvermögenswirksame Leistungen.


## **Werden Sie Teil unseres Teams!**

Bewerben Sie sich jetzt online über den Button „Jetzt bewerben“.

**Fragen?**

Kontaktieren Sie Frau Elisabeth Seidel unter **0174 / 730 8558**.

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

**Johnson Controls**is a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings. With operations in over 150 countries, we are committed to driving innovation and excellence in building technologies and integrated solutions. Our people are at the heart of our success, and we are passionate about attracting and developing top talent across the globe. We are looking for a **STIVA 1 Training & HPT Coordinator.**

**What will you do?**

The Training & HPT Coordinator oversees the plant’s overall training and development framework, ensuring that employees receive effective onboarding, ongoing skills growth, and access to the resources needed for successful performance. The role manages key training environments and facilitates communication channels that support learning and engagement. It also guides trainer coordination, monitors progress through established indicators, supports continuous improvement methodologies, and helps strengthen organizational culture through integration and branding initiatives.

**How will you do it?**

- Develop and sustaining the training system (on boarding) for newly hired personnel (administrative and operational).

- Develop, coordinate and execute the plant level skill assessment plan.

- Design, implement and manage training room & communication room.

- Coordinate trainer activities.

- Ensure the training indicators (% training time, % multi-skills, % administrative training, HPT Scorecard).

- Ensure the implementation of JCMS MP's (related to training and HPT).

- Coordinate the implementation of branding based on JCMS requirements.

- Schedule integration events (Salary & hourly).


**What are we looking for?**

- Engineering Degree.

- Prior experience a similar position.

- Personal management.

- Leadership.

- English.

- Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.

- Comfortable talking in front of a group as well as one-on-one with an individual.

- Knowledge of various teaching methods and approaches.

- Excellent organizational skills.

- Able to give and receive feedback effectively.


**Whatdowe offer?**

- Comprehensive benefits package.

- Opportunities for professional growth and development.

- Supportive and inclusive work environment.

- Commitment to work-life balance and employee well-being.

- Engagement in meaningful projects that make a difference.


**Johnson Controls does not request pregnancy or HIV testing as a condition for hiring, continued employment, or promotion**,in accordance withits commitment to labor equality and non-discrimination.

Job Description

**What You Will Do**

Johnson Controls is looking for a Production Engineer to join our Gaziemir manufacturing plant. This role reports directly to the Production Manager and plays a key part in driving operational excellence on the shop floor.

**How You Will Do**

- Support daily production to meet safety, quality, delivery, and cost targets
- Improve manufacturing processes and lead problem‑solving activities
- Coordinate engineering changes and new product introductions
- Drive Lean/continuous improvement initiatives
- Collaborate with quality, maintenance, and planning teams to ensure smooth operations

**What We Look For**

- Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical or Industrial Engineering (required)
- 2–5 years of experience in production/manufacturing engineering
- Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills
- Experience with Lean, Kaizen, or Six Sigma is a plus
- Good command of English and strong communication skills
- Proactive, hands‑on, solution‑oriented mindset

Job Description

**Salesforce Test Analyst**

**Overview:**Johnson Controls isseekinga talented and motivated Salesforce Test Analyst to join our growing team. This individual will play a key role in ensuring the quality and performance of our Salesforce applications, focusing on both manual and automated testing processes. The role involves collaboration with both onshore and offshore teams in an Agile environment, working closely with Product Managers, Product Owners, developers, and other stakeholders to design, execute, andmaintaintest cases for Salesforce solutions.

**Responsibilities:**

- Develop detailed test plans, test cases, and test scripts based on functional requirements, business use cases, and user stories.


- Create andmaintaintest data for both functional and integration testing.


- Collaborate with product management and technical teams toidentifytesting needs and scenarios.


- Execute manual testing of Salesforce applications, including UI, functional, and regression testing.


- Design, develop, and execute automated test scripts using Salesforce testing tools such as Selenium,Worksoft,BrowserStack,LambdaTest.


- Perform integration testing to ensure smooth communication between Salesforce and third-party systems (e.g., ERP, payment gateways).


- Identify, document, and track defects in tools like Azure Boards or Jira, ensuringtimelyresolution.


- Re-test fixed defects and verify that solutions meet acceptance criteria.


- Conduct performance testing tovalidatethe scalability of Salesforce applications using tools like JMeter or LoadRunner.


- Perform API testing to verify integrations with external systems using tools like Postman or SoapUI.


- Participate in Agile sprint cycles to ensuretimelydelivery of high-quality Salesforce solutions.


- Contribute to the improvement of testing processes and best practices within the team.


- Provide regular test status reports and defect metrics to management.


**Qualifications:**

- 3-5years of experience testing all phases of QA efforts for medium to large enterprise Salesforce implementation projects, specifically in Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, and Experience Cloud.


- Experience with manual testing of Salesforce projects using Agile Scrummethodology.


- Familiarity with testing, including validation of screens and user interfaces in Sales Cloud and Commerce Cloud environments.


- Effective communication skills to share testing knowledge and strategies in a collaborative atmosphere, leading and influencing positive quality results among testing teams.


- Proven experience in planning, preparing, and executing functional, integration, and user acceptance testing, including end-to-end test scenarios across multiple systems and batch processes.


- Demonstrated experience as an IT Quality Assurance professional for small to large projects.


- Experience in developing test strategies and managing all aspects of software testing and defect management.


- Proficiencyin risk-based testing approaches todeterminetestingscope.


- Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, team-oriented environment.


- Experience with Salesforce (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud) isa must; Commerce Cloud experience is a plus.


- Experiencein automation and device simulationtoolssuch as Selenium,Worksoft,BrowserStack,LambdaTest.


- Experience with performance testing tools (e.g., JMeter, LoadRunner) is a plus.


- Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to interact with technical and non-technical stakeholders.


- Salesforce certifications (e.g., Administrator, Platform App Builder) are highly preferred.

Job Description

**What you will do**

The Service Engineer will be reporting directly to the Senior Project Manager and will be based in Dubai. As part of the Security systems Aftermarket team you will install, service, repair and carry out preventative maintenance on the Security systems.

You will be supporting the Service & Maintenance team on designing and execution of projects. As a member of this team, you will work within a dynamic environment that provides a platform for you to develop your technical and project management skills.

**How you will do it**

As a Service Engineer you will use your strong Security systems technical knowledge to execute site surveys and carry out full dilapidation reports, inspecting all refrigeration components and recommending system enhancements, upgrades and replacements as necessary.  As an experienced Service Engineer, you will;

• Ensure efficient and cost-effective delivery of Inspection, Testing and Maintenance of Security systems (CCTV, Access control, ANPR, facial recognition, intercom, barriers) and other monitoring systems as per the scope of Services.

• Plan, coordinate and supervise the activities and resources for day-to-day activities covered under the contract agreement.

• Coordinate closely with the Head office support departments in a planned manner to get the required support in time.

• Provide technical advice and assistance and makes recommendations based upon field observation and maintenance needs.

• Ensure consistent implementation of work methods and procedures used in maintenance and engineering and recommends improvements.

• Advise on availability of materials, spares, consumables including tools for smooth execution of activities.

• Prepare and review various performance reports such as Preliminary Failure and Root Cause Analysis Etc. Monitor the effectiveness of PM programs and recommends changes as required.

• Execute project with due attention for quality, health and safety and environment and Liaise with the Company Quality & HSE manager on all aspects of quality and safety.

• To ensure that work once started is carried out as planned, and that relevant legislation and regulations are observed on site.

• Send reports as per agreed scope of Services for the works planned and completed.

**What we look for**

**Required**

- Bachelors Degree or Diploma in Electrical /Electronic engineering or equivalent


- Minimum of 5 years’ experience in servicing, maintenance, troubleshooting of Various Security Systems


- Experience in handling reports & communications in a concise way


- Experience in servicing and maintenance of Access Control, CCTV, Intercom, Gate Barrier, Intrusion Systems, etc


- Proficient in reading and understanding blueprints, diagrams, submittals and specifications used in installation activities


- Proven ability to produce high quality and impactful professional documents for clients and internal use.


- Clear understanding of Contractual documents/obligations and preparation of official communications with Client/ Vendors.


- Excellent client-facing and internal communication skills.

Job Description

**Tube Fitter Apprentice**

**Location: San Antonio ,TX**

**Shift: 2nd Shift (Monday-Saturday: 3:30PM-2AM)**

**Pay: $17.00/hour + 2nd Shift Premium**

**Summary**

Join the Johnson Controls team as a Tube Fitter Apprentice at our San Antonio, TX plant. This role is an excellent opportunity for individuals looking to gain hands-on experience in the manufacturing industry. You will work under the guidance of experienced professionals while developing essential skills in tubing installation and electrical systems.

**Essential Job Functions**

- Bend, form, and install all tubing on units.

- Perform tubing layout of units according to blueprints in shop packets.

- Identify and install valves and/or devices on units.

- Some knowledge of electrical systems required.


**Marginal Functions**

Perform housekeeping and other duties as required.

**Physical/Mental Requirements**

- Lifting 50 lbs. (occasionally), standing (constantly), walking (frequently), bending (frequently), climbing (frequently), crawling (occasionally), reaching (frequently), grasping (frequently), sitting (occasionally), connecting (frequently), pulling (frequently), reading prints (frequently), and exercising judgment (frequently).

- Must understand and follow safety rules, regulations, policies, and procedures.

- Ability to follow written and oral instructions.

- Vision corrected to 20/20.

- Handling equipment (frequently).


**Environmental Factors**

Manufacturing environment – Exposure to noise, dirt, dust, cold/heat (indoor), and cold/heat (outdoor). Job hazards of working around heavy equipment and machinery.

**Tools and Equipment Used**

Tape measure, small hand tools, power tools, and drills.

**Qualifications**

- Possess a complete understanding of Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&ID) on electrical drawings as applicable.

- Ability to use hand tools, electrical equipment, and measuring tape.

- Capability to maintain a clean and safe work area and/or equipment as required.

- Active participation in a Continuous Improvement Team is a condition of employment.


The above description covers the most significant duties performed but does not exclude other occasional work assignments necessitated by business reasons which may affect production/operations to some degree.

Job Description

**Johnson Controls**is a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings. With operations in over 150 countries, we are committed to driving innovation and excellence in building technologies and integrated solutions. Our people are at the heart of our success, and we are passionate about attracting and developing top talent across the globe. We are looking for a **CM Engineer PLM/SAP.**

**What will you do?**

- Manages own workload effectively and efficiently.

- Expands technical contribution and encourages knowledge collaboration between teams.

- Fosters Business Intelligence & other developers to grow and mentor to help ensure overall solutions are delivered on a timely manner.

- Provides training for members of the team and ensures team effectiveness.

- May provide informal guidance and support to colleagues with less experience.


**How will you do it?**

- Determining business needs via consultation, business analysis, and targeted observations.

- Assessing existing IT infrastructure and recommending improvements.

- Developing and configuring customized SAP solutions.

- Deploying SAP solutions and ensuring smooth system integration.

- Troubleshooting and resolving issues to optimize performance.

- Providing SAP system support and end-user training.

- Promoting leveraging IT integration.

- Performing rigorous documentation of SAP processes and presenting progress reports.

- Ensuring compliance with industry regulations/audits.

- Keeping up to date on the latest SAP/PLM offerings and developments.

- Overseeing the engineering change process and Material Master Data Changes.

- Overseeing the holistic alignment from EBoMs to MRP MBoMs documenting any exceptions.

- Receive and review engineer changes, report any discrepancies to Design team.

- Review the work instructions that need to be validated.

- Create Factory Change Notice Implementation Plans.

- Participate in VAVE projects.

- Support production for BoM discrepancies.

- Execute massive changes for BoMs, Material Master, and routings.

- Generate the weekly Engineering changes report and distribute via-email to all involved.

- Verify production floor implementation.

- Scheduling annual Material Master Data audits within Engineering and MRP databases.


**What are we looking for?**

- Bachelor's or higher degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.

- +3 years of experience in PLM, product development, or a related field.

- Proven track record of implementing and managing PLM solutions in a manufacturing environment.

- Strong knowledge of PLM tools (preferably Windchill) and methodologies.

- Excellent teamwork and collaboration skills.

- Effective communication skills with the ability to influence and collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.

- Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

- +3 years of experience with SAP.

- Proficient in Microsoft Office applications (EXCEL, WORD, PowerPoint)

- Fluent in Spanish/English


**Whatdowe offer?**

- Comprehensive benefits package.

- Opportunities for professional growth and development.

- Supportive and inclusive work environment.

- Commitment to work-life balance and employee well-being.

- Engagement in meaningful projects that make a difference.

Job Description

**Salesforce Test Analyst**

**Overview:**Johnson Controls isseekinga talented and motivated Salesforce Test Analyst to join our growing team. This individual will play a key role in ensuring the quality and performance of our Salesforce applications, focusing on both manual and automated testing processes. The role involves collaboration with both onshore and offshore teams in an Agile environment, working closely with Product Managers, Product Owners, developers, and other stakeholders to design, execute, andmaintaintest cases for Salesforce solutions.

**Responsibilities:**

- Develop detailed test plans, test cases, and test scripts based on functional requirements, business use cases, and user stories.


- Create andmaintaintest data for both functional and integration testing.


- Collaborate with product management and technical teams toidentifytesting needs and scenarios.


- Execute manual testing of Salesforce applications, including UI, functional, and regression testing.


- Design, develop, and execute automated test scripts using Salesforce testing tools such as Selenium,Worksoft,BrowserStack,LambdaTest.


- Perform integration testing to ensure smooth communication between Salesforce and third-party systems (e.g., ERP, payment gateways).


- Identify, document, and track defects in tools like Azure Boards or Jira, ensuringtimelyresolution.


- Re-test fixed defects and verify that solutions meet acceptance criteria.


- Conduct performance testing tovalidatethe scalability of Salesforce applications using tools like JMeter or LoadRunner.


- Perform API testing to verify integrations with external systems using tools like Postman or SoapUI.


- Participate in Agile sprint cycles to ensuretimelydelivery of high-quality Salesforce solutions.


- Contribute to the improvement of testing processes and best practices within the team.


- Provide regular test status reports and defect metrics to management.


**Qualifications:**

- 3-5years of experience testing all phases of QA efforts for medium to large enterprise Salesforce implementation projects, specifically in Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, and Experience Cloud.


- Experience with manual testing of Salesforce projects using Agile Scrummethodology.


- Familiarity with testing, including validation of screens and user interfaces in Sales Cloud and Commerce Cloud environments.


- Effective communication skills to share testing knowledge and strategies in a collaborative atmosphere, leading and influencing positive quality results among testing teams.


- Proven experience in planning, preparing, and executing functional, integration, and user acceptance testing, including end-to-end test scenarios across multiple systems and batch processes.


- Demonstrated experience as an IT Quality Assurance professional for small to large projects.


- Experience in developing test strategies and managing all aspects of software testing and defect management.


- Proficiencyin risk-based testing approaches todeterminetestingscope.


- Ability to work effectively in a collaborative, team-oriented environment.


- Experience with Salesforce (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Experience Cloud) isa must; Commerce Cloud experience is a plus.


- Experiencein automation and device simulationtoolssuch as Selenium,Worksoft,BrowserStack,LambdaTest.


- Experience with performance testing tools (e.g., JMeter, LoadRunner) is a plus.


- Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to interact with technical and non-technical stakeholders.


- Salesforce certifications (e.g., Administrator, Platform App Builder) are highly preferred.

Job Description

We are seeking an experienced Electrical Component Installer, 2nd Shift to join our federal team based at York, PA.

We're keeping the Navy cool. Literally. Our team at the Grantley Plant custom designs, develops and manufactures complex chilled water HVAC and refrigeration equipment for Navy vessels around the world. This position consists of performing operations required to plan, lay out, assemble, wire and test complex electrical systems on special engineered chiller systems.  Operations include working from drawings, orders, schematics, specifications, codes, instructions.  Make layouts of conduit and cable runs, calculate, make necessary bends, cut to length, flare or thread.  Wire in high voltage motors, check motor protection, mount, connect control panels, starters.  Make operational tests.  Check, operate, test safety devices.  Interlocks, system gauging controls, relay sequencing, repair, and replace components.  Terminate and code wires.

Use steel tape, electrical measuring instruments, hand, hand power tools, ladder, and appropriate material handling equipment.

Pay Rate: $30.32 per hour, plus a $.0.70/hour Shift differential. (Increased 9/2025)

Bonus Eligible: Yes

Shift: 2nd Shift hours, Monday-Friday (3:00 pm – 11:30pm)

Benefits: Eligible for benefits on first day of employment

Employment type: Full Time, Union position

\*Learn more about Navy Systems: [Link Here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AD9TF3ANMxU&t=2s)

As an Electrical Component Installer, you will:

- Perform routine installation and checkout on a variety of electrical systems utilizing codes, company procedures, schematics, and/or engineering drawings
- Installs raceways, cables and wiring to meet local or national codes, standards and customer system requirements.
- Make layouts of conduit and cable runs, calculate, make necessary bends, cut to length, flare or thread.
- Wire in low to high voltage motors, check motor protection, mount, connect control panels, starters.
- Make operational tests. Check, operate, test safety devices. Interlocks, system gauging controls, relay sequencing, repair, and replace components.
- Terminate and code wires.
- Use steel tape, electrical measuring instruments, hand, hand power tools, ladder, and appropriate material handling equipment.
- Mounts and terminates controls equipment as needed.
- Adheres to Johnson Controls safety programs and policies
- Performs other related duties as assigned

Required Qualifications:

- Demonstrated ability to read and interpret electrical schematics, blueprints, and/or engineering drawings.
- Possesses working knowledge of local electrical codes.
- Experienced in scope determination of installation materials.
- Able to work independently or part of a team.

Preferred Education:

- Electrical apprenticeship (preferred) but not required.


Who We Are

At Johnson Controls (NYSE:JCI), we are One Team working collaboratively to create purposeful solutions that make a difference in the world. We are a Fortune 500 company with more than 100,000 employees worldwide offering the world\`s largest portfolio of building technology products, solutions and services. As a member of our Federal Systems team, your work matters. We value and recognize your contributions and want to help you succeed. We invest in our employees, provide opportunities for growth and advancement, and foster a culture of inclusion and respect.

To learn more about who we are and what we do, please check out our [Take a Journey](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GvGYkasvEwM) video.

Recently, Johnson Controls has been recognized by several organizations for leadership in Environment, Sustainability and Governance, as well as innovations in smart building pl

Job Description

**Be part of the future!**

We are one team, dedicated to working collaboratively to create the purposeful solutions that propel the world forward. We hope you will join our diverse team of top people – you bring your talent, and we’ll give you the space and opportunities to grow and succeed.  We are committed to make a difference.

**What we offer:**

- Competitive Starting Pay
- Paid Training
- Global Advancement Opportunities
- Company Vehicle (as applicable)
- Referral Bonuses
- Comprehensive Benefits
  - Medical/Dental/Vision insurance
  - Health Savings Account (HSA)
  - Life Insurance
  - 401(k) savings plan with company match
  - Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
  - Employee Assistance Program
  - Wellness Program
  - And More!

**What you will do**

Perform service and repairs on Portable Fire Extinguishers, Kitchen Hood Systems & Suppression Gas Systems in addition to other related building system products at customer sites.  Service, troubleshoot and install many different manufacturers including Simplex and ANSUL.  Read and interpret blueprints, diagrams, submittals, specifications, software/systems programs, schematics, and operational product manuals. Manage daily service call, customer activity and program system products. You will work with clean agent systems, dry chemical systems, and special hazards.

**How you will do it**

- Assist customers with all questions and concerns in a timely and professional manner.

- Participation in an on-call rotation for Emergency Service.

- Performs inspections of Suppression Gas Systems based on contract requirements.

- Inspect customer systems

- Use of Smart Phone and Equipment

- May include government work


**What we look for**

**Required**

- 4+ years of experience

- Excellent communication and organizational skills

- Outstanding Customer facing service and problem-solving skills

- PC Skills including Microsoft Windows, Excel, Word, and Outlook


**Preferred**

- Any Manufacturer certifications is a plus; ANSUL, Kiddie PyroChem

- Local and State license if required.


HIRING HOURLY RANGE: $24-34 (Hourly rate to be determined by the education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities of the applicant, internal equity, and alignment with market data.) This position includes a competitive benefits package. The posted salary range reflects the target compensation for this role. However, we recognize that exceptional candidates may bring unique skills and experiences that exceed the typical profile. If you believe your background warrants consideration beyond the stated range, we encourage you to apply. To

support an efficient and fair hiring process, we may use technology assisted tools, including artificial intelligence (AI), to help identify and evaluate candidates. All hiring decisions are ultimately made by human reviewers. For details, please visit the About Us tab on the Johnson Controls Careers site at https://jobs.johnsoncontrols.com/about-us

Job details

**Employment Type**:

Full-Time

**Location**:

Johannesburg, Gauteng, South Africa

**Job Category**:

Risk / Health, Safety & Env

**Job Number**:

WD30260376

[Apply Now](https://jobs.johnsoncontrols.com/job/WD30260376#)

[Apply now](https://apply.johnsoncontrols.com/johnson-controls/apply/job_requisition_id/WD30260376?)

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

**What you will do**

EHS Manager is a key role, reporting to Sr. EHS Manager MEA and responsible for overseeing EHS activities across South Africa Region. The primary function of an EHS (Environmental, Health, and Safety) Manager is to:

- Enforce the OHS Act to ensure the workplace complies with the regulations laid out in the Occupational Health and Safety Act 85 of 1993 to safeguard employees, the environment, and the organization

- Implement ISO 14001/ 45001 Management system

- Compile and maintain safety files for all contracts as per legal / contractual requirement and assists branches with updated baseline safety files for use as templates

- Develop, review and implement EHS training program for employees & contractors

- Undertake various risk assessments, develop, review and implement SOP’s,

- review and assign various legal appointments and ensure training for legal appointments is completed.

- Development, review and implementation of EHS inspections and audits for all legal compliance. Prepare reports on various audits / inspection findings and follow up for closeout

- Coordinate and lead SHE committee meetings.

- Managing incident and accident investigations

- Preparing and maintaining all safety reports and documentation

- Compile, maintain and enforce safety systems and procedures across all company operations and assisting all branches in implementing a safe working environment


You will be based in Johannesburg and will be part of a project execution team and shall be responsible for implementing safety and environmental standards within organization.

**How will you do it**

EHS Manager primary responsibility is to ensure a safe and healthy work environment by conducting safety inspections and audits, establishing and enforcing safety guidelines, ensuring compliance, conducting risk assessments, and maintaining adequate EHS Records complying ISO 14001 / ISO 45001 Standard requirements. You play a crucial role in fostering a culture of safety awareness, promoting JCI Zero Harm Vision and Life Saving Rules (LSR) among employees. He shall focus on developing a program preventing accidents, injuries, and illnesses by identifying hazards, developing safety policies & procedure and providing training.

**What we look for**

**Required**

- **Bachelor’s degree/ Diploma in occupational health and safety or Environmental Science or Engineering**

- **Accredited health and safety certificate**, often at NQF Level 5, such as the SAMTRAC program.

- **Min of 5 years EHS experience**.

- **A valid driver’s license** (required) and willing to Travel to various sites

- **Lead Auditor in ISO 14001/45001 and strong knowledge of relevant ISO standards.**

- Result orientated, tenacious, self-motivated, a customer focused

- Excellent Interpersonal skills and communication skills.

- Problem Solving, Collaboration, Analyzing and Reporting.

[Apply now](https://apply.johnsoncontrols.com/johnson-controls-es/apply/job_requisition_id/WD30261434?)

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

**Principales Responsabilidades:**

Resolución de disputas (clientes nacionales e internacionales)

Realizar actividades del proceso de cobranza administrativa, conciliaciones bancarias, gestión de cobranza, tramites fiscales.

Actualizar y depurar la cartera de los clientes.

Gestionar la cartera de clientes activos mediante correo electrónico o vía telefónica (facturas, estados de cuenta, gestión de cartera) base de datos y plataformas internas.

Realizar visitas personales a los clientes para llevar a cabo negociaciones directas de pago

Manejo de portales empresariales

Dar soluciones inmediatas como reprogramación, refinanciación o cobranza judicial de los créditos vencidos que tiene problemas de flujo o incobrabilidad.

Mantener indicadores óptimos de morosidad, gestionando adecuadamente el nivel de provisiones de la cartera asignada.

Seguimiento y resolución de notas de crédito, refacturación o amortización.

Plan de recuperación de cartera vencida, seguimiento overdue, reestructuración y conciliación de cuentas.

Reportes de antigüedad de saldos y cartera

Seguimiento y resolución de disputas, reunión semanal

Cobranza clientes 150 días

Seguimiento a devoluciones de clientes

Desbloqueo de clientes en sharepoint y customer Master

**Requerimientos:**

Conocimiento en cuentas por cobrar, de preferencia experiencia con otros países

Ingles intermedio

Licenciatura en administración o contaduría

Resolución de problemas

Trabajo en equipo

Comunicación efectiva

Enfoque en trato con el cliente

Job Description

**What you will do**

施主・ビルオーナー、ゼネコン・サブコン・設計事務所への提案営業

- ビルディングオートメーションシステムの提案


- 中央監視、セキュリティ、HVAC&R、省エネルギー、ファシリティマネジメント等各種ソリューションの提案


- 新築・改修・リニューアル工事の提案


- メカニカルサービスの提案


- 設計打ち合わせ・見積り作成


**How you will do it**

- 既存顧客とのリレーション強化


- 新規顧客開拓および立ち上げ


**What we look for**

**Required**

- 建築業界での営業経験


- サブコンに対する営業経験


- 施主・オーナー（デベロッパー・鉄道・病院等）営業の経験


- 中央監視装置・自動制御機器の営業または施工経験


- 空調・プラントにおける営業または施工経験


- ソリューション営業経験


- 空調、電気、衛生などの建築設備業界の経験・知識


**Preferred**

- エネルギー管理業務またはESCO業務経験・知識


- 空調、電気、衛生などの建築設備業界の経験・知識


- 設備の設計・施工経験者尚可


- 電気工事施工管理技士


- 管工事施工管理技士


- 英語力（尚可-外資アカウントセールスチームあり）


- 計装士


- エネルギー管理士


- 認定ファシリティマネジャー

Job Description

**Salesforce Developer**

**Overview:**

Johnson Controls isseekinga skilled and motivated**Salesforce Developer**to join our dynamic Product Team. In this role, you will design, build, enhance, andmaintainour enterprise**B2B Commerce**application within the Salesforce platform. The ideal candidate brings strongexpertisein Salesforce development and a demonstrated ability to deliver scalable,highquality, and maintainable solutions. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in an agile environment to translate business needs into effective Salesforce functionality.

**Responsibilities:**

- Develop, configure, and deploy robust Salesforce applications and solutions tailored to business requirements.


- Collaborate with Product Owners, Technical Leads, and other stakeholders to gather, analyze, and define technical requirements.


- Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code using**Apex, Lightning Web Components (LWC), Aura, Visualforce,Flow** and related Salesforce technologies.


- Participate in code reviews to ensure adherence to best practices, coding standards, and platform governance limits.


- Integrate Salesforce withthird-partysystems using**REST/SOAP APIs**and**event-driven architectures**.


- Conduct unit testing, debugging, and performance tuning to ensure functionality and reliability.


- Maintain and enhance existing Salesforce applications by addressing defects, technical debt, and enhancement requests.


- Stay current with Salesforce releases, new features, and emerging technologies; recommend improvements where applicable.


**Qualifications:**

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field, or equivalent experience.


- Minimum of**3years ofhands-onSalesforce development experience**, including Apex, LWC,Aura,Visualforce,and Flow.


- Proven experience building and managing**Experience Cloud sites using the LWR framework**(required).


- Knowledge of**Salesforce Commerce Cloud,Coveo, andAgentforce**is an advantage.


- Strong understanding of Salesforce data models, standard and custom objects, security, and sharing models.


- Experience with Salesforce integrations using**REST/SOAP APIs**, event-driven patterns,**SSO, Named Credentials, Connected Apps, Canvas Apps**, andiframe.


- Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodologies and tools such as**Jira**or**Azure Boards**.


- Solid front-end skills with**JavaScript, HTML, CSS, SLDS**, and the Lightning Component Library.


- Knowledge of Salesforce best practices, design patterns, and development lifecycle.


- Experience with**Git**and CI/CD pipelines.


- Salesforce Certifications (e.g.,**Platform Developer I**,**Platform** **Administrator**) are highly preferred.


- Excellent analytical andproblem-solvingskills with strong attention to detail.


- Effective communication skills to collaborate with both technical and nontechnical stakeholders.


- Self-starter with the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

**What you will do**

As the Regional Procurement Manager, you will be the main procurement contact for your assigned Regions.  You will support and manage the selection of 3rd party subcontractors used on JCI projects within your Regions and will work with Sales, Engineering & Operations teams in full support of projects from inception through execution.  You will drive best practices, ensure proper quoting, supplier rationalization, contract execution and lead supplier negotiations upon project award.  You will develop and lead cost saving initiatives in collaboration with category managers and branch teams.  Activities include supplier development, contract management, maintain existing and develop new supplier relationships, supply base rationalization, price negotiations, trade working capital optimization and improve communication between procurement and local branch installation and service offices.  You will directly engage with the Regional Leadership to ensure that you are an active member of their leadership teams and all Regional and branch offices within your areas are aware of your activities and pro-actively working with you to achieve common goals and objectives.

**This position offers remote flexibility; however, applicants must be based in the Northeast region. ​**

**How you will do it**

- Develop, align, and implement multiple supply chain strategies to deliver the most cost-effective methods for field teams to procure subcontract labor from suppliers.

- Ensure branch installation and service teams are efficiently executing our supply chain strategies allowing our extended field procurement team to influence a greater percentage of spend to increase year over year savings.

- Ensure we have the proper qualified suppliers in each market and take leverage full JCI spend to drive cost savings.

- Meet or exceed individual and team profit plan goals related to: Cost Savings, TWC, quality, supplier rationalization, supplier diversity spend and other agreed objectives.

- Provide input to category management team regarding the unique/specific regional needs during category strategy development.

- Create personal development plans for team members to ensure continuous evolution and development of the team and ensure full alignment with JCI’s overall strategies.

- Work with suppliers and internal teams and act as escalation point to ensure continuity of supply to support the business unit requirements.

- Collaborate with field offices within assigned Areas to identify new/critical products and services to expand the line card product of field offerings.

- Ensure branches are utilizing approved subcontractors and material suppliers as per policy / work instruction.

- Draft and monitor appropriate controls to ensure processes and procedures are performed in a compliant manner.

- Develop appropriate dashboards, metrics and measures to identify ongoing areas for improvement across all facets of the procurement operation.

- Establish annual goals for Field Procurement Managers on your team providing support, guidance and clear expectations in alignment with business priorities to ensure the success of your team, overall procurement and the business.

- Ensure that quality and delivery requirements are met. Work JCI legal department on the execution of supplier contracts to ensure contracts are issued in accordance with corporate terms and conditions.

- Work with regional teams to report project based diverse sourcing opportunities.

- Align procurement metrics with the Area leadership teams and communicate regularly with internal customers.


**What we look for**

**Required:**

- Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience

- 5-8 years of procurement

Job Description

**Be part of the future!**

We are one team, dedicated to working collaboratively to create the purposeful solutions that propel the world forward. We hope you will join our diverse team of top people – you bring your talent, and we’ll give you the space and opportunities to grow and succeed.  We are committed to make a difference.

**What we offer**

- Competitive Starting Pay
- Paid Training
- Global Advancement Opportunities
- Company Vehicle (as applicable)
- Referral Bonuses
- Comprehensive Benefits
  - Medical/Dental/Vision insurance
  - Health Savings Account (HSA)
  - Life Insurance
  - 401(k) savings plan with company match
  - Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
  - Employee Assistance Program
  - Wellness Program
  - And More!

**What you will do**

Performs assigned system commissioning using Johnson Controls configuration and commissioning tools. Troubleshoots and resolves basic HVAC mechanical, electrical, and controls problems.  Responds to basic warranty calls. Provide our customers with the highest level of service to solve building inefficiencies and resolve service related items. As a trusted advisor to our customers you will provide solutions and outcomes at the time of service.

**How you will do it**

Loads system-level controller software.

Performs basic commissioning and system diagnostics from system-level controllers to end devices (i.e. sensors, actuators, etc) and completes all required commissioning documentation.

Keeps management and JCI contractor or customer informed of job progress and issues.

Calibrates systems requiring basic electronic test equipment.

Completes administrative tasks including but not limited to time sheets, expense reports, and on-site documentation.

Documents changes and provides information for as-built documentation.

Communicates with the JCI contractor or customer upon arrival and before leaving the work site.

Follows all safety standards and attends required safety training.  High degree of regard to employee and subcontractor safety.

Provide recommendations and solutions to ensure facility is running efficiently to include upselling opportunities.

Provide quotes to support recommendations and solutions.

**What we look for**

**_Required_**

Vocational School four year program or an Associate degree in electronics, mechanical systems, computer technology, air conditioning or similar field plus three years of industry experience or 1 year experience in servicing electronic and/or mechanical systems in the HVAC industry.

Generally required knowledge includes HVAC controls systems, facilities management systems, site preparation, peripheral equipment installation and servicing techniques.

Effective interpersonal skills to properly represent Johnson Controls to customers and other outside contacts in an ethical and professional manner.

Ability to explain technical information to technical and non-technical people.  Leadership skills to support the activities of Mechanics working on non-complex, non-critical Building Automation equipment on a job site.

PC experience required and the ability to program HVAC related software.

HIRING HOURLY RANGE: $23.56- 32.21(Hourly rate to be determined by the education, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities of the applicant, internal equity, and alignment with market data.) Job Description

**Ihr Aufgabenfeld:**

- Durchführung von Wartungen, Inbetriebnahmen, Störungsbeseitigungen, Reparaturen und Änderungen an stationären Feuerlöschanlagen, insbesondere an Wasserlöschanlagen (Sprinkler, Sprühwasser, Hochdruckwassernebel)

- Beratung von Bestandskunden zu Serviceleistungen

- Dokumentation

- Teilnahme am Rufbereitschaftsdienst


**Wie werden Sie Ihre Aufgaben angehen:**

Unsere Sicherheit liegt in Ihren Händen. Als **Kundendienstmonteur (m/w/d) Feuerschutz** planen Sie bei uns eigenständig Ihren Tagesablauf. Wir bieten Ihnen eine moderne IT-Ausstattung sowie ein vollausgerüstetes Kundendienstfahrzeug. Sie sind ein Teamplayer mit guter Kommunikationsfähigkeit und serviceorientiertes Arbeiten ist für Sie selbstverständlich.

**Ihre Qualifikationen:**

- Abgeschlossene Berufsausbildung im technischen Bereich: z.B. als Mechatroniker, Gas-, Wasserinstallateur, Anlagenmechaniker für Sanitär-, Heizungs-, Klimatechnik

- Berufliche Erfahrung im stationären Brandschutz- und Brandmeldebereich wünschenswert

- Kenntnisse der einschlägigen Richtlinien VDE, DIN, VdS sind von Vorteil

- Selbständiges und serviceorientiertes Arbeiten


**Was wir Ihnen bieten:**

**Unsere Kultur:** Eine kollegiale Atmosphäre in einem internationalen Konzernumfeld

**Ihre Vergütung:** Leistungsgerecht und marktkonform

**Ihre Arbeitszeit:** 35 Stunden mit 30 Tagen Urlaub – Fahrtzeit gilt bei uns auch als Arbeitszeit)

**Ihr Arbeitsort:** Mobil, flexibel. beim Kunden vor Ort

**Ihre Ausstattung:** Moderne IT-Ausstattung sowie ein vollausgerüstetes Kundendienstfahrzeug

**Ihre Karriere:** Johnson Controls steht für den **Fortschritt** und unterstützt **IHRE** Karriere mit ganz speziell auf **SIE** ausgerichteten Karriereplänen

**Ihre Weiterbildung:** Neue Produktlösungen und zukunftsweisenden Technologien wecken auch ihr Interesse? Wir bieten Ihnen die Möglichkeit der Erweiterung ihrer Kenntnisse auf fachlicher Ebene auch im Bereich der Sicherheitstechnik

**Ihre Benefits:** Nutzen Sie interessante Angebote von Corporate Benefits, gerne unterstützt Johnson Controls auch Ihre ehrenamtlichen Tätigkeiten

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung am besten über unser Online Portal.

Job Description

**Be part of the future!**

We are one team, dedicated to working collaboratively to create the purposeful solutions that propel the world forward. We hope you will join our diverse team of top people – you bring your talent, and we’ll give you the space and opportunities to grow and succeed.  We are committed to make a difference.

**What we offer**

- Competitive Starting Pay
- Paid Training
- Global Advancement Opportunities
- Company Vehicle (as applicable)
- Referral Bonuses
- Comprehensive Benefits
- Medical/Dental/Vision insurance
- Health Savings Account (HAS)
- Life Insurance
- 401(k) savings plan with company match
- Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
- Employee Assistance Program
- Wellness Program
- And More!

**What you will do**

Inspectors are responsible for conducting routine inspections, testing, service, and preventative maintenance on Electrical, Sprinkler or Suppression life safety product lines as well as similar competitive manufacturers’ product lines. Work with low voltage wiring and associated devices for the operation of low voltage equipment. Complete Service Acknowledgements with proper coding through communication devices such as laptop computers and/or hard copy. Follow and maintain a highly structured inspection schedule. Input, retrieve and archive inspection documents via laptop computer. Complete assigned inspections on time. Perform other duties as assigned.

**How you will do it**

- Sprinkler and Suppression Inspectors will perform Inspections under Sprinkler and Suppression contracted service lines.
- Check reports and maintain archives-inspection and service reports
- Supervise or coordinate with JCI personnel to complete inspections
- Supervise and maintain inspection schedules, trackers, and service calls
- Work with JCI Service regarding parts and materials
- Maintain a supply of parts and materials
- Maintain coordination with JCI employees including TSM, Managers, CCR, ESERV, Technicians, Inspectors, Schedulers, IT, Xaap portal.
- Some documented experience in low voltage electrical systems.
- Experience in reading and interpreting blueprints, diagrams, submittals, specifications, programs schematics, and operational/product manuals.
- Experience with hand tools and multi-meter usage.
- Demonstrates an aptitude for troubleshooting systems and performing necessary small repairs.

**What we look for**

**_Required_**

- Five to ten years documented experience in low voltage electrical systems and Fire and /or Life Safety Systems experience required.
- Basic computer skills. Proficient using iPhone, apps and Laptops.
- Demonstrates an aptitude for troubleshooting systems.
- Ability to follow verbal and written instructions.
- Capable of performing physical labor to include carrying and moving equipment and tools up to 50-70 lbs.
- Able to work in unusual and sometimes difficult position such as climbing ladders, scaffolding, and high lift equipment up to 40 feet, working within ducts, crawl spaces, above ceilings, etc.
- Ability to work flexible hours including weekdays and weekends to meet customer requirements.
- Willing to travel out of town including overnight lodging.
- Demonstrate a high level of customer service.
- Ability to work well with others.
- Self-starter that can work with minimal supervision.
- Ability to adhere to, implement, and follow safety guidelines, and procedures at all times.
- Strong organizational skills, positive attitude, and an ability to learn quickly.
- Possess a valid driver’s license and driving record that meets company requirements.
- Able to obtain and retain any licenses that are required by National, State, and Local codes.
- High School Diploma or equivalent.
- Successful completion of the Inspector E

Job Description

**What you will do**

We are seeking for a Product and Design Engineer who will be a member of our Izmir team and will directly report to our Engineering Manager. At this position, you will assist production line to prepare engineering and production bill of materials according to customer requirements.

As Product and Design Engineer, you will transfer new designs from global design center to Izmir factory production line, also apply modification and improvements on the products.

It requires knowledge on 3D software (Creo Parametric) and ERP software.

**How you will do it**

- Support on department on activities coordination

- Prepare the information for transfer product from global engineering to local factory.

- Release drawings and engineering change release

- Development of products according to standards

- Assists in identifying specification issues or interpretation discrepancies and providing solutions to resolve.

- Develops documentation for projects.

- Release drawings and engineering change coordination, to distribute information with the internal departments.

- Carry out product definitions including BOM

- Orders and engineering preparation

- Prepare VA activities

- Responsible for creating and maintaining the technical information in the factory

- CE PED certification and Eurovent support

- Support factory departments to prepare all necessary info to complete the source of the components

- Support procurement on the specification understand

- Prepare cost impact analysis to quantify cost savings or other customer benefits.

- Assists in technical or financial bench marking as needed

- Teaches and reinforces methodology and skills used to collect, analysis and present designs, scopes and cost estimates

- Line support for production from engineering point of view.


**What we look for**

**Required**

- Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering

- 3D Creo experience

- Design experience

- Fluent in English, both written and verbal


**Preferred**

- Experience in HVAC industry

- Windchill

- SAP – BOM

Job Description

En Johnson Controls, transformamos los entornos donde las personas viven, trabajan, aprenden y juegan. Desde la optimización del rendimiento de los edificios hasta la mejora de la seguridad y el aumento de la comodidad, impulsamos los resultados que son más importantes. Dedicados a proteger el medio ambiente, cumplimos nuestra promesa en sectores como la atención de salud, la educación, los centros de datos y la manufactura. Gracias a un equipo mundial de 100.000 expertos en más de 150 países y más de 130 años de innovación, somos la fuerza detrás de la misión de nuestros clientes.

**Descripción / Responsabilidades:**

Johnson Controls requiere contratar un(a) **Vendedor (a) de Servicios Especialista en Refrigeración** para la Región Metropolitana de Chile.

**Funciones principales:**

- Gestionar una cartera de clientes, profundizándola y fidelizándola, al igual que prospectar nuevos clientes para la compañía.

- Visitar en terreno a los clientes, manteniéndose actualizado de sus requerimientos y resolviendo todas las necesidades que se presenten.

- Manejar los aspectos técnicos de los productos que comercializa, generando expertise para abordar a los clientes de manera técnica y comercial.

- Realizar estudios técnicos para la aplicación de productos, elaborar propuestas económicas y presupuestos.

- Brindar soporte técnico a los proyectos en ejecución para la validación de factibilidad técnica, diseño y puesta en marcha de sistemas de Refrigeración.


**Requisitos:**

- Grado académico: Ingeniería en Refrigeración y Climatización, Ingeniería Mecánica, Ingeniería Industrial, o carreras afines.

- Mínimo 3–5 años de experiencia en ventas técnicas de Refrigeración y Climatización

- Experiencia trabajando con clientes industriales, de alimentos, constructoras, mineras, entre otras.

- Conocimiento en selección de equipos, normativas y estándares de climatización.

- Manejo de Sales Force y manejo nivel medio de Excel

- Licencia de conducir clase B.

- Orientación al Cliente, Proactivo, con habilidades de liderazgo y manejo de personal.


**Condiciones:**

- Lugar de trabajo: Renca, Santiago.

- Turno: 5x2 100% presencial

- Viajes esporádicos y planificados dentro del país.


¿Estás interesada/o en una compañía que considera la equidad de género e inclusión como una de sus prioridades más importantes? Tenemos diversas oportunidades para ti, postula con nosotros.
