Job Description

**What will you do**

Under general direction, acts as the on-site project leader to plan, execute, and complete fire alarm, access control, CCTV, intrusion alarm and intercom system projects with assigned customers. Performs or delegates tasks as required to execute and fully complete assigned projects including: hardware design, system programming, installation coordination, system and network commissioning and project closeout. Responsible for the overall financial results of assigned projects including: costs, project billings, and collections. Maintains an effective balance between customer satisfaction and project financial results. Actively pursues selling change orders. Coordinates communication with the customer during all phases of the project. Ensures proper execution of warranty. Provides work direction to subcontractors, electrical installers, technicians, designers, and administration as necessary. Ensures work performed is in compliance with state, local and Federal legal requirements and operates on the job with the highest of ethics. Adheres and ensures Johnson Controls staff and subcontractors adhere to all safety standards.

**How will you do it**

- Completes typical installation hardware design and software programming using established standards as required. Provides detailed information to communicate design and operation to customers, Johnson Controls staff and subcontractors.
- Actively pursues additional work through change orders. Evaluates the contractual scope of work and the impact of client issued bulletins, field directives and/or scheduling changes. Communicates both technical and business related issues with the clients.
- Manages the selection, ordering, budget and delivery schedule of materials to be procured for the projects assigned.
- Secures pricing and availability from outside vendors and suppliers, mechanical and electrical subcontractors including scope, terms and conditions. May request several bids in pricing process. Oversees subcontractor payment and billing processes.
- Provides jobsite coordination for panel and field device physical locations. Ensures installation is in accordance with Johnson Controls literature and project requirements.
- Manages and completes the loading, device verification, and commissioning of all systems and controllers as required. Validates complete system functionality and resolves issues with subcontractors and others to ensure proper operation. Provides accurate project as-built and commissioning documentation.
- Manages costs, billings and collections. Completes project billings in a timely and accurate format to the client. Maintains profitability goals and positive cash flow.
- Provides coaching, mentoring and technical assistance to System Technicians, Electrical Installers and System Designers. Provides technical assistance to subcontractors. Ensures that delegated tasks are done accurately, on-time, billed, within budget and within scope of the contract.
- Effectively communicates the status of projects to management as required and provides monthly forecasts of revenue, costs, and gross margin.
- Sends warranty letter to JCI customer upon substantial completion of project and ensures proper execution of warranty.
- Develops project plans and coordinates the required resources to ensure timely and cost effective installation and completion of assigned projects.
- Performs site-specific training for owner/operator on the total system in full or with assistance. Ensures owner/operator has been trained. Provides proper documentation and manuals for system operation.
- Develops and maintains viable long-term relationships with contractors, clients, consultants and subcontractors. Att

Job Description

**Johnson Controls**is a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings. With operations in over 150 countries, we are committed to driving innovation and excellence in building technologies and integrated solutions. Our people are at the heart of our success, and we are passionate about attracting and developing top talent across the globe. We are looking for a **Technical Engineer Contact Center.**

**What will you do?**

The **Technical Engineer – Contact Center** plays a key role in designing, implementing, supporting, and optimizing the technologies that enable our customer experience operations. You will work closely with cross‑functional teams—including IT, Customer Service, Vendor Partners, and Operations—to ensure our contact center platforms are reliable, scalable, and continuously improving.

**How will you do it?**

- Provide advanced technical support for contact center infrastructure (voice, chat, IVR, ACD, integrations, call recording, etc.).

- Troubleshoot Tier 2/Tier 3 issues, perform root‑cause analysis, and implement long‑term fixes.

- Configure, maintain, and optimize contact center platforms (routing logic, workflows, user provisioning, telephony settings).

- Monitor system performance and proactively identify issues before they impact operations.

- Participate in or lead deployments of new features, upgrades, migrations, and technology enhancements.

- Support integrations with CRMs, ticketing systems, reporting tools, and automation solutions.

- Create detailed technical documentation and collaborate with stakeholders on solution design.

- Analyze call flows and performance metrics to recommend operational and technical improvements.


**What are we looking for?**

- Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Engineering, Telecommunications, or related field.

- +3 years of experience in a technical support, systems engineering, or contact center engineering role.

- Experience with at least one major contact center platform (e.g., Genesys Cloud, Cisco UCCE, Avaya Aura, Five9, NICE inContact, Amazon Connect).

- Knowledge of network fundamentals, VoIP, SIP, routing, and telephony concepts.

- Familiarity with APIs, scripting, or automation (Python, PowerShell, JSON) is a plus.

- Understanding of workforce management, call routing strategies, and reporting analytics.

- Experience integrating CCaaS solutions with CRM or ITSM platforms.

- Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently.

- Customer‑centric mindset with a focus on reliability and quality.


**Whatdowe offer?**

- Comprehensive benefits package.

- Opportunities for professional growth and development.

- Supportive and inclusive work environment.

- Commitment to work-life balance and employee well-being.

- Engagement in meaningful projects that make a difference.


**Johnson Controls does not request pregnancy or HIV testing as a condition for hiring, continued employment, or promotion**,in accordance withits commitment to labor equality and non-discrimination.

Job Description

**Location:** San Antonio, TX

**Shift:** 2nd Shift \| Full-Time **Monday-Saturday: 3:30PM-2AM**

**Pay:** $17.00/hour + 2nd Shift Premium

**Summary**

Join the Johnson Controls team as an Insulator at our San Antonio, TX plant. This role is an excellent opportunity for individuals looking to gain hands-on experience in the manufacturing industry. You will work under the guidance of experienced professionals while developing essential skills with wrapping units and measuring and cutting material.

**Essential Job Functions**

- Wrap units with insulation material.

- Cut and measure material for placement on unit.

- Fit, adjust, install, and fasten unit or component parts to unit.


**Marginal Functions**

Perform housekeeping and other duties as required.

**Physical/Mental Requirements**

- Lifting 50 lbs. (occasionally), standing (constantly), walking (frequently), bending (frequently), climbing (frequently), crawling (occasionally), reaching (frequently), grasping (frequently), sitting (occasionally), connecting (frequently), pulling (frequently), reading prints (frequently), and exercising judgment (frequently).

- Must understand and follow safety rules, regulations, policies, and procedures.

- Ability to follow written and oral instructions.

- Vision corrected to 20/20.

- Handling equipment (frequently).


**Environmental Factors**

Manufacturing environment – Exposure to noise, dirt, dust, cold/heat (indoor), and cold/heat (outdoor). Job hazards of working around heavy equipment and machinery.

**Tools and Equipment Used**

Tape measure, small hand tools, power tools, crane, and brazing torch.

**Qualifications**

- Knowledge of hand tools

- Ability to read a measuring tape

- Good hand dexterity required to fit and assemble product

- Some judgment required

- Perform math at a level normally acquired through completion of high school

- Ability to maintain clean and safe work area and/or equipment as required.

- Depending on the type of Assembler, this person must be able to qualify in the following process:

  - Insulation PSI testing
- Capability to maintain a clean and safe work area and/or equipment as required.

- As a condition of employment, active participation in a Continuous Improvement Team is a condition of employment.


The above description covers the most significant duties performed but does not exclude other occasional work assignments necessitated by business reasons which may affect production/operations to some degree.

Job Description

**What you will do**

The Technical Support Engineer provides advanced technical assistance for Johnson Controls’ UL‑listed fire detection products across the Middle East region. The role supports partners, installers, service teams, and end customers through troubleshooting, commissioning support, product expertise, and ensuring compliance with UL and local Civil Defense requirements. This position plays a key role in maintaining product reliability, customer satisfaction, and brand reputation.

**How will you do**

Technical Support & Troubleshooting

- Provide remote and onsite technical support for JCI UL fire detection panels, detectors, modules, and networked systems.

- Diagnose complex hardware, software, and network-related issues, ensuring timely resolution.

- Support system commissioning, configuration, programming, cause‑and‑effect development, and integration with BMS and third‑party interfaces.

- Escalate critical issues to global support and R&D when required.


Product Expertise & Knowledge Management

- Serve as a subject matter expert for UL product lines (e.g., Zettler Profile, FireClass UL, Tyco UL detection portfolio).

- Maintain up‑to‑date knowledge of product releases, firmware updates, and configuration tools.

- Develop and update technical bulletins, troubleshooting guides, FAQs, and application notes.


Training & Enablement

- Deliver technical training to distributors, partners, JCI field teams, and key customers.

- Support certification programmes for installers and maintenance teams.

- Conduct workshops, demonstrations, and product seminars across the Middle East.


Compliance & Documentation

- Ensure system designs and configurations comply with UL standards and local authority requirements (Dubai Civil Defense, Abu Dhabi Civil Defense, etc.).

- Review design submittals, wiring diagrams, and cause‑and‑effect matrices.

- Prepare detailed technical reports and failure analysis documentation.


Field Support & Site Engagement

- Provide onsite support for major project installations, acceptance testing, and troubleshooting.

- Assist sales and project teams with technical evaluations, product selection, and solution design.

- Participate in customer meetings to resolve technical concerns.


Continuous Improvement

- Gather field feedback and communicate product performance issues to engineering teams.

- Recommend enhancements to improve product reliability and customer experience.

- Contribute to knowledge-base development to streamline future support.


**What we look for**

**Required**

- Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics, Mechatronics, or related field.

- 3–5+ years of experience in fire detection systems, preferably UL‑listed systems.

- Strong understanding of fire alarm network architecture, loop design, and life‑safety system principles.

- Hands‑on experience with commissioning, programming, and troubleshooting fire detection systems.

- Familiarity with UL864, NFPA 72, and Middle East Civil Defense requirements.

- Excellent communication and customer‑facing skills.


**Preferred**

- Experience with Johnson Controls / Tyco / Zettler UL fire detection platforms.

- Knowledge of BMS integration protocols (Modbus, BACnet, OPC).

- Experience delivering technical training.

- Arabic language skills (optional but beneficial).

Job Description

Job Description

**Why work at Johnson Controls**

Johnson Controls is powered by your talent. We are the power behind the customer mission. Together we are building a world that is safe, comfortable, and sustainable. Our diverse global team of 117,000 experts in more than 150 countries creates innovative, integrated solutions to make cities more connected, buildings more intelligent and environments more comfortable and secure.

**What you will do**

This Skilled Fire Alarm Installerposition involves installing, troubleshooting, commissioning, and servicing Simplex Fire Alarm Systems.  Including fire alarm device replacement/repair, resolving low voltage circuit troubles and system testing. Organize and effectively utilize technical resources, information, material, and support to assure efficient execution of all assigned tasks.

**How you will do it**

- Execute projects on time and within allocated installation hours.

- Work with the project manager to resolve problems that impair installation activities.

- Must be able to work on multiple projects simultaneously.

- Read and interpret blueprints, diagrams, submittals, specifications, schematics, and operational product manuals.

- Coordinate the installation activities with all trades to optimize installation time.

- Attend all required site meetings.

- Instruct and train customers on functional operation of the equipment/system.

- Conduct simulations and answer questions.

- Exhibit excellent customer service skills by consistently communicating with the district staff and customers to ensure customer satisfaction.

- Complete project documentation in a timely manner.

- Adhere to all OSHA and Johnson Controls safety policies and procedures.

- Perform other duties as assigned.


**What we look for**

**Required**

- High School Diploma or Equivalent

- Five (5) years documented experience in low voltage electrical systems, including Fire and Life Safety systems.

- Experience in reading and interpreting blueprints, submittals, and operational/product manuals.

- Experience with hand tools test equipment, multi-meter usage and commissioning devices.

- Able to obtain and retain any licenses that are required by National, State, and local codes.

- Capable of performing physical labor to include carrying and moving equipment and tools up to 50-70 lbs.

- Able to work in unusual and sometimes difficult position such as climbing ladders, scaffolding and high lift equipment up to 40 feet, working within crawl spaces and above ceilings, etc.

- Ability to work flexible hours including weekends to meet customer requirements.

- Demonstrate a high level of customer service.

- Ability to lead & work well with team members.

- Ability to adhere to, implement, and always follow safety guidelines and procedures.

- Strong organizational skills, positive attitude, and an ability to learn quickly.

- Possess a valid driver’s license and driving record that meets company requirements.

- Able to pass a pre-employment background and drug test.


**Preferred**

- NICET Level II certification.

- Local State licenses.

- Knowledge of national and local Fire Codes

Job Description

Content and Data Migration Specialist:

Experience: 3 -10 years

Overview:

Johnson Controls is seeking an experienced Content & Data Migration Specialist to join our Global Marketing IT team. In this role, you'll be responsible for migrating Product content from various sources into our PIM tool (InRiver) as part of our global digital transformation initiatives. You will have previous experience with content & data migrations. In this role you will collaborate with multiple teams, stakeholders, and business units to ensure a smooth, accurate, and timely migration while maintaining high data integrity. A key focus will be on reducing manual effort by leveraging automation and GenAI tools to optimize workflows. The ideal candidate will adopt an automation-first mindset, using GenAI and other tools to boost efficiency, minimize manual tasks, accelerate the migration process, and deliver cost-effective, high-quality results. You'll work with cross-functional teams to ensure the migrated content is accurate, optimized for the new platform, and aligns with business requirements.

**Key Responsibilities:**

1.    Sitecore Content Migration:

a.    Lead and manage the migration of content, resources, metadata etc. from multiple data sources into JCI PIM solution, ensuring consistency, accuracy, and alignment with business requirements.

b.    Execute the end-to-end migration process, including content extraction, transformation and loading while maintaining data quality and integrity throughout.

c.    Work with content owners, business analysts, and development teams to understand legacy data structures and translate them into PIM-compatible formats.

d.    Develop and document migration workflows and strategies to ensure smooth and efficient content transfers.

2.    Collaboration with Cross-Functional Teams:

a.    Partner with global marketing teams, content owners, product owners, IT teams, and development teams to define content migration requirements and processes.

b.    Serve as a key point of contact for migration-related queries, providing regular updates to stakeholders and addressing any concerns during the process.

c.    Collaborate with Scrum teams, business analysts, and developers to ensure seamless integration of migrated content into PIM solution.

3.    Content Data Analysis & Optimization:

a.    Conduct thorough data analysis alongside senior data analyst to assess legacy content for quality, structure, and relevance before migration, ensuring that it meets the standards required by PIM solution.

b.    Develop and execute content mapping strategies to ensure content from legacy systems is appropriately structured and categorized in PIM solution.

c.    Work with quality assurance (QA) team to ensure that checks are made after migration to ensure content is ready to transfer to downstream systems.

4.    Automation & Tool Development:

a.    Leverage existing automation tools, scripts, and migration utilities to streamline and expedite content migration processes.

b.    Develop and maintain migration templates, tools, and scripts to automate routine tasks and ensure consistency in large-scale content transfers.

c.    Monitor the migration process for performance and efficiency, identifying opportunities for improvement and optimizing workflows.

5.    Content Governance & Best Practices:

a.    Ensure content governance best practices are followed during migration.

b.    Advocate for PIM’s best practices in content management and assist teams in adhering to the guidelines for content creation and delivery.

6.    Training & Documentation:

a.    Provide training and guidance to other team members involved in the migration process, ensuring consistent application

[Apply now](https://jcifederal.bamboohr.com/careers/1477?)

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

We are seeking a Project Manager for our federal team supporting a few project sites in the Washington D.C. Metro area. This role will primarily work out of our office on the Fort Myer military base in Arlington, VA. Our team is primarily responsible for install and retrofit projects related to HVAC mechanical equipment and HVAC Controls (Building Automation Systems) systems, and other relevant equipment and systems.

As part of a Fortune 500 company, Johnson Controls Federal Systems (JCFS) is a specialized team serving as a trusted partner to the federal government. We help modernize U.S. military installations, Department of Defense and other federal agency facilities to be smarter, more resilient, efficient, sustainable, and secure.

Pay range: $110,000 - $140,000 base salary plus bonus incentive. Negotiable based on experience.

Bonus eligible: Yes

Benefits: Eligible for benefits on first day of employment

Paid Time Off: 3 weeks paid vacation, 5 sick days, 3 floating holidays, and 10 standard holidays (6.5 weeks in total)

Travel Required: None, all work is based at this location

As a Project Manager, you will:

- Serve as a primary leader for execution on a retrofit and installation projects, primarily related to the HVAC mechanical equipment, BAS/Controls (Direct Digital Control (DDC) systems), and other MEP related equipment and systems. Develops project schedules and executes according to plan.
- Supervises the field construction process of assigned projects in accordance with the plans and specifications, to ensure that each phase of the work is completed on schedule, within the budget, and with the quality workmanship expected by the company and the customer
- Manages budgets and completes forecasting in conjunction with Operations Manager and Regional Manager.
- Manage the selection, scope of work, procurement, quality and payment of project materials, vendors and subcontractors
- Negotiates, prepares and issues subcontracts
- Ensures that assigned projects are done accurately, on-time, billed within budget and within scope of the contract.
- Adheres to and ensures project personnel and subcontractors adhere to all safety standards, internal policies, and US Army Corps of Engineers requirements regarding health and safety.
- Understand project and contract scopes; actively pursues growth opportunities that will help the customer achieve desired outcomes
- Help prepare and approve cost estimates, contract terms & conditions, and technical responses to client requests for information, bids, and proposals
- Ensures Technicians, subcontractors and other site personnel complete assigned work per established standards, and thoroughly communicate the services performed and completion criteria to customers
- Complete timely and accurate project billing to maintain profitability goals and positive cash flow
- Analyze financial reporting and project schedule to proactively address potential problems. Effectively communicates project progress, issues and financial status to leadership as required
- Provide periodic project status updates, and monthly revenue, cost, and gross margin forecasts
- Manage risk, establish project recovery plans, and resolve disputes to minimize escalation
- Ensure project document controls and deliverables comply with contract requirements and Johnson Controls standards
- Manages the selection, ordering, and delivery schedule of materials to be procured for the projects assigned
- Coordinates customer-training requirements

Required Qualifications:

- Project management or service management experience in the building construction industry for commercial or industrial project sites, preferably with commercial or industrial HVAC mechanical, HVAC controls/BAS, MEP, or other related equipment and systems.
- Able to execute projects of higher project scope and contract

[Apply now](https://apply.johnsoncontrols.com/johnson-controls/apply/job_requisition_id/WD30260250?)

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

**What you will do**

Despatcher is a key role reporting to the Business Support  & Operations Excellence Director and is responsible for organizing technicians’ field work.  He/she is the right hand of Operations Manager on the ground, taking care of the scheduling and monitoring team performance and job delivery.

As a member of this team, you will work within a vibrant environment that provides a platform for you to use your experience to generate satisfactory results.

**How you will do it**

As a Despatcher, your responsibilities will be:

- Scheduling jobs for around 40 technicians and dispatching vehicles or equipment to appropriate locations according to predetermined schedules, customer requests or immediate needs.
- Creating jobs in the system and communicating with the Operations team.
- Coordinating with multiple departments and customers for successful job execution.
- Following up on timesheet submission, making sure that all 40 technicians have submitted their timesheets and service reports on a weekly basis.
- Following up on contract billing and ensuring  it is done accurately and timely.
- Contributing to process improvement initiatives
- Analyzing and consolidating various reports using Excel

**What we look for**

**Required:**

- University degree or diploma
- Ability to deal with multiple urgent situations, and co-ordinate for quick resolution without supervision
- High personal discipline and ability to monitor and drive team performance
- Attention to detail
- Good Excel and numerical skills
- Work experience of min. 3-5 years in a similar role

Job Description

Wir suchen aktuell in der Region Nord-Ost-Deutschland einen**Sales Operations Mitarbeiter (m/w/d).**

**Ihre Aufgaben:**

• Regelmäßige Überprüfung der Pipeline mit den Teilregionen, um Datenqualität und Fortschritt der Chancen sicherzustellen.

• Unterstützung der regionalen Manager durch Pflege sauberer Pipelines und Identifikation von Lücken oder Engpässen.

• Zusammenarbeit mit Vertriebsteams zur Entwicklung neuer Kampagnenregeln und Verfeinerung der Zielgruppenlogik.

• Analyse von Kampagnenergebnissen (z. B. Konversionsraten, Kampagnenleistung, Ablehnungsgründe) und Identifikation von Verbesserungsmöglichkeiten.

• Vertrauensvoller Partner für regionale Vertriebsteams, um Leads voranzubringen und Chancen zu konvertieren.

**Ihre Qualifikationen:**

• Erfahrung in Vertriebsoperationen, Vertriebsadministration oder Sales Enablement.

• Vertrautheit mit ERP-Systemen und geschäftsrelevanten Feldern für die Installationsbasis.

• Starke analytische Fähigkeiten zur Interpretation von Kampagnendaten und Ableitung umsetzbarer Verbesserungen.

• Erfahrung oder Bereitschaft zur Schulung in Lead-Generierungsplattformen (z. B. Installationsbasis).

• Ausgezeichnete Kommunikations- und Kooperationsfähigkeiten über regionale und funktionsübergreifende Teams hinweg.

• Ehrgeiz, sich in eine breitere Vertriebsmanagement- oder strategische Enablement-Rolle weiterzuentwickeln.

**Was wir Ihnen bieten:**

**Unsere Kultur:** Eine kollegiale Atmosphäre in einem internationalen Konzernumfeld

**Ihre Vergütung:** Leistungsgerecht und marktkonform

**Ihre Einarbeitung:** Umfangreich und intensiv – ein detaillierter Einarbeitungsplan an Ihre Erfahrung angepasst

**Ihre Weiterbildung:** Nutzen Sie unsere interne Trainingsbibliothek und fachspezifische Kurse je nach Bedarf

**Ihre Absicherung:** Eine Unfallversicherung 24/7, die im beruflichen und privaten Umfeld greift

**Ihre Benefits:** Nutzen Sie interessante Angebote von Corporate Benefits, gerne unterstützt Johnson Controls auch Ihre ehrenamtlichen Tätigkeiten oder unterstützt Sie mit der Zahlung von vermögenswirksamen Leistungen

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftige Bewerbung am besten über unser Online Portal.

Job Description

**What you will do**

As the Project Engineer – HVAC Systems, you will play a pivotal role in driving the growth of our HVAC business. Based in Dubai, you will be part of a dynamic team, responsible for managing the design and execution of projects. Your key responsibilities will include:

•            Monitoring assigned engineering projects, QA/QC policies, performance standards, and specifications.

•            Interacting daily with clients to understand their needs and represent the company in the field.

**How you will do it**

Your expertise in large project executions and in-depth knowledge of HVAC systems solutions in the Middle East region will be crucial. You will:

•            Lead a team of field technicians and site engineers to complete and deliver security system projects.

•            Monitor and control project cost, time, and scope.

•            Prepare project progress reports and present them to internal and external stakeholders.

•            Understand and monitor various financial metrics of the projects, ensuring timely submission of billing invoices and facilitating payment collection.

•            Communicate formally (letters) and informally (verbally) with clients.

•            Prepare technical submittals, drawings, method statements, etc., as per project requirements.

•            Prepare and comply with project quality plans and inspection & test plans, ensuring full compliance with specifications and client requirements.

•            Initiate change processes to manage variations as per contract terms.

•            Ensure key milestone dates are met and develop plans to bring projects back on track if delays occur.

•            Manage all allocated staff and resources to complete projects on time and within budget.

•            Develop and train allocated staff.

•            Ensure compliance with company and client QHSE policies/procedures.

•            Liaise with QA/QC and HSE managers on all aspects of quality and HSE.

•            Monitor and control all aspects of specialist sub-contractors, including finalizing subcontract agreements, certifying subcontract invoices, and resolving disputes.

•            Ensure subcontractors follow all HSE guidelines as per company and customer policies.

•            Manage and archive all project data as per company guidelines.

•            Work with various internal and external stakeholders to ensure successful project execution.

**What we look for**

**Required**

- Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering or Similar Discipline from a reputable university.
- Minimum of 5 years project engineering experience in execution of HVAC systems involving Chillers, AHUs, FCUs etc.
- Good interpersonal, negotiation, team management and influencing skills.
- Excellent analytical, strategic conceptual thinking and planning, project costing and execution skills.
- Determine cost estimate targets during the design and development process, recommending cost effective solutions.
- Proven ability to produce high quality and impactful professional documents for clients and internal use.
- Clear understanding of Contractual documents/obligations and preparation of official communications with Client/ Vendors.
- Excellent client-facing and internal communication skills.
- Fluency in the English language is essential and excellent mail etiquettes.
- Decision Making Skills.

You have successfully copied the job URL to clipboard!

Job Description

**What will you do**

As a Project Engineer – Telecom, you will be involved in managing client relationships and project execution for telecom system installations in Qatar. You will lead teams, manage budgets, oversee technical designs, communicate with clients, and mitigate risks while ensuring quality assurance throughout the project lifecycle

**How will you do:**

You will be involved in,

**Stakeholder & Client Management** \- Act as the main contact for clients and contractors. Lead meetings, resolve issues, and manage change requests.

**Project Initiation** \- Review scope and goals with Sales. Align project with client specs, HSE standards, and international codes. Prepare execution plan.

**Project Planning** \- Create detailed plans with timelines, resources, and budgets. Coordinate schedules and ensure alignment with contract terms.

**Team Leadership** \- Lead engineering and site teams. Assign tasks, monitor progress, and ensure smooth communication.

**Budget & Cost Control** \- Manage budgets, track costs, and approve purchases. Forecast costs and handle scope changes.

**Technical Oversight** \- Review and approve designs. Ensure proper integration of telecom systems and resolve technical issues.

**Client Communication** \- Provide regular updates and reports. Manage expectations and ensure satisfaction.

**Risk Management** \- Identify risks, assess impact, and implement mitigation plans.

**Quality Assurance** \- Ensure compliance with standards. Oversee inspections, testing, and commissioning.

**Documentation & Reporting** \- Maintain project records, prepare reports, and ensure audit-ready documentation.

**Procurement & Vendor Management** \- Select vendors, manage procurement timelines, and ensure compliance with specs.

**Installation Supervision** \- Oversee telecom system installation in challenging environments. Coordinate with other disciplines.

**Regulatory & Safety Compliance** \- Ensure compliance with industry and safety standards. Coordinate permits and inspections.

**Testing & Handover** \- Lead FAT/SAT and commissioning. Approve test procedures and manage client handover.

**Project Closure** \- Finalize handover, conduct reviews, and support warranty phase. Capture lessons learned.

**What we look for:**

**Required:**

- Degree in Electrical/ Electronics Engineering or similar.
- 10+ years of experience in low voltage, security, and telecom systems.

**Preferred:**

- Experience with JCI/TYCO UAE and Oil & Gas projects.
- PMP, PRINCE2, or similar certifications are a plus.
- Background in Telecom System Integration (TSI) is an advantage.
- Experience in EPC/FEED and ADNOC projects is beneficial.
- Strong knowledge of low current systems: Access Control, CCTV, LAN, VOIP, WiFi, Fire Detection, Intercom, PAGA, Radio (UHF/VHF), etc.
- Skilled in project planning, costing, and execution.
- Excellent communication, negotiation, and team management skills.
- Able to prepare professional documents and client communications.
- Good understanding of contracts and vendor coordination.

Job Description

**Johnson Controls**is a global leader in smart, healthy, and sustainable buildings. With operations in over 150 countries, we are committed to driving innovation and excellence in building technologies and integrated solutions. Our people are at the heart of our success, and we are passionate about attracting and developing top talent across the globe. We are looking for a **Salesforce Sr. Developer.**

**What will you do?**

Johnson Controls is seeking a talented and motivated Salesforce Developer to join our dynamic team to manage the B2B Commerce salesforce portal. The ideal candidate will have a strong foundation in Salesforce development and a proven ability to create scalable and maintainable solutions on the Salesforce platform.

This role will involve working closely with cross-functional teams in an agile environment to meet business requirements and deliver high-quality Salesforce applications.

**How will you do it?**

- Develop and implement robust Salesforce applications and solutions tailored to business needs.

- Collaborate with Product Owners, Technical Leads, and other stakeholders to gather and define requirements.

- Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code using Apex, Visualforce, Aura, Lightning Web Components, and other Salesforce technologies.

- Participate in code reviews, providing and receiving constructive feedback to ensure high code quality and adherence to best practices. Integrate

- Salesforce with third-party applications and services using REST/SOAP APIs.

- Conduct unit testing and debugging to ensure the functionality and performance of developed solutions.

- Maintain and enhance existing Salesforce applications, addressing any issues or enhancements as needed.

- Stay up to date with Salesforce releases and new features, providing recommendations for improvements and enhancements.

- Assist in the creation and maintenance of technical documentation, including design specifications and user guides.


**What are we looking for?**

- Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.

- Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in Salesforce development, including experience with Apex, Lightning Web Components, and Visualforce. Experienced in creating and managing Experience Cloud Sites.

- Extensive experience in building webpages in VF Pages, Aura, LWC, JavaScript, CSS, and HTML.

- Strong understanding of Salesforce objects, data models, and security features.

- Strong understanding of Salesforce objects, data models, and security features.


**Whatdowe offer?**

- Comprehensive benefits package.

- Opportunities for professional growth and development.

- Supportive and inclusive work environment.

- Commitment to work-life balance and employee well-being.

- Engagement in meaningful projects that make a difference.


**Johnson Controls does not request pregnancy or HIV testing as a condition for hiring, continued employment, or promotion**,in accordance withits commitment to labor equality and non-discrimination.

# Chase Leadership    Development Program

We’re looking for strategic, analytical, and innovative individuals who are eager to work collaboratively to solve real issues our customers face.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You’ll contribute to innovative projects while gaining deep insight into the Consumer & Community Banking business. You’ll have the opportunity to support various strategic projects, like using data analytics to measure operational effectiveness, driving product development, leading project management efforts, evaluating business strategies, or developing innovative solutions to key business cases.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

You should have exceptional analytical, interpersonal, project management, and problem-solving skills.

### What we offer

During this nine-week program, you will support a business unit within Chase, such as Card Services, Consumer Banking, or Home Lending.

#### **On-the-job experience**

Your work may include using data analytics to measure operational effectiveness, driving product development through the lifecycle – testing, iterating, measuring, and marketing new features; leading project management efforts for high visibility of our brands, evaluating and quantifying business strategies in line with regulatory controls, or developing innovative solutions to key business cases by collaborating across business, product areas, and functions.

#### **Training**

We’ll complement your work experience with training designed to improve your leadership, management, technical, and professional skills. Additionally, you’ll benefit from one-on-one coaching, and have access to networking events and a speaker series.

#### **Career Progression**

High-performing interns who successfully complete the program may receive an offer of employment into our full-time rotational program.

# Design Development Program

Launch your career by joining our Experience Design team, focusing on design or research as you learn, grow, and contribute to JPMorganChase’s portfolio of work.

This program will guide you as you create dynamic visual solutions, research the needs of our users, and drive design thinking on matters that add value to our business and have real-life impact on our customers.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/design-dev-fulltime#first-apply-now-link)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

In this two-year program, under the guidance of user experience and research leads, you will have the opportunity to design and redesign product experiences, participate in user research to better understand user needs and behaviors,  and engage in usability testing to see your solutions at work. You’ll focus on ideation and prototyping, working creatively to explore different design options.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We are committed to hiring candidates from various backgrounds and experiences. We’re looking for passionate, dynamic, and motivated individuals with an interest and background in user experience design, interaction design,  or user research.

We are looking for people who are open to collaborating with others and demonstrate excellent teamwork skills.

The full-time program is available for candidates pursuing a bachelor's degree in a relevant design discipline or those who have obtained their foundational technology background in non-traditional ways, including, but not limited to, technology bootcamps or workforce development programs.

#### **Key Skills**

We’re looking for individuals who understand the basics of adjacent skills, such as user research, UX writing,  and prototyping.

Qualified candidates display foundational communication and presentation skills, and can justify their design decisions.

### What we offer

In this full-time position as a UI/UX Designer or Researcher, you will have the opportunity to learn, develop your career, and gain new skills. During your time in the program, you will grow with support from your peers, managers, mentors, and program managers.

#### **On-the-job experience**

This two-year program will support new UI/UX talent by providing a welcoming and diverse environment to learn and grow in the financial technology space. You will contribute to meaningful projects, develop your business acumen, and make fulfilling connections with our team members.

#### **Training**

You’ll begin with an orientation at one of our main offices. Over the course of the program, you’ll have access to professional and leadership development training, networking, business deep dives,  and one-on-one mentorship.

#### **Career progression**

After successfully completing the full-time program, highly motivated analysts have the opportunity to move into full-time associate positions.

# Securities Services Leadership Program

This summer analyst program is the gateway into our two-and-a-half-year rotational program that offers the opportunity to develop a comprehensive, end-to-end knowledge of the Securities Services business. You will spend your summer in one of our key business areas.

If you receive an offer to join full-time, you'll participate in three 10-month rotations, covering areas such as Client Services, Product Development, and Product Management. This program is designed to establish a strong foundation in the key disciplines of Securities Services so that you will be able to take on significant responsibilities and projects within the business upon completion of the program.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/sslp-summer-analyst#first-apply-now-link)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You will develop your analytical, technical, and leadership skills while gaining hands-on project management experience in your role. You will work with seasoned managers in your location and have exposure to global teams across all areas of Securities Services. The full-time program also gives you the opportunity to experience business trips in an office outside of your base location.

Additionally, you will have regular engagement and networking opportunities with business leaders, the Analyst & Associate Development Council, and council members. You also have the support of a sponsor, program manager, as well as your direct manager.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for future leaders of the business, with the vision and initiative to develop solutions and drive change. You should have a strong interest in finance, project management, emerging technologies, data governance and analytics, and the use of digital tools to drive innovation. Our business is constantly evolving so we rely on strong team players who are proactive, responsive, and can thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

#### **Key skills**

We’re looking for individuals with excellent project management and problem-solving skills as well as strong interpersonal skills. You will be working with teams across multiple lines of business in many different countries, so strong communication skills are a must. You also will need the ability to collect and synthesize large amounts of detailed information and use it to propose and deliver innovative solutions. We want people who value continuous learning and look forward to opportunities that challenge them to grow.

### What we offer

In this program, you’ll get an in-depth look into one of the business areas of Securities Services, while gaining exposure to the full end-to-end business.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You'll work with experienced managers and team members on priority initiatives that span across all aspects of Securities Services. You will have the opportunity to work across business areas within the Commercial and Investment Bank and colleagues around the globe.

#### **Training**

As a summer intern, curated programming will provide you with a comprehensive overview of the business and access to all of our Securities Services University courses.

As a full-time analyst, you will cultivate and hone new skills through an ongoing training curriculum and program analysts will participate in a two-week bootcamp. You will have the unique opportunity to participate in leadership trainings designed for your analyst class.

#### **Career progression**

Upon successful completion of the summer program, top performing interns may receive an offer to join our full-time Securities Services Leadership Program.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/image-sslp-summer-analyst.jpg)

title: J.P. Morgan Investment Banking Full-Time Analyst | JPMorgan Chase & Co.

[![Close Icon](https://www.chase.com/etc/designs/chase-ux/css/img/icon_close.svg)](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/investment-banking-fulltime-analyst#)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/investment-banking-fulltime-analyst#main)

# Investment Banking Analyst Program

Start your career working alongside the top professionals in the industry to come up with solutions that shape the global economy. Investment banking teams partner with corporate clients to achieve their strategic goals. Whether it’s raising capital for an expansion or buying another company, investment bankers provide innovative and creative financial solutions for all clients’ needs. You’ll be part of a global, fast-paced team that does business with more than 90% of Fortune 500 companies.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

You’ll join one of our Advisory, Capital Markets, and Coverage (Industry), groups, developing meaningful solutions for clients or executing high-value transactions. You will attain the skills and experience to become a thoughtful, strategic advisor. You’ll learn the nature of client service and how we build long-term relationships by putting their needs first, while developing the technical acumen to continue a career in investment banking. You’ll also write reports, build updated financial models, and support multi-billion dollar transactions.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We value diversity of thought, backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We’re looking for those who have innovative ideas and entrepreneurial qualities, and enjoy tackling new challenges and solving intellectual problems.

We’re looking for motivated, resourceful, analytical people who are ready to contribute from the beginning. If you have exceptional interpersonal and communication skills and thrive in a dynamic, collaborative environment, then this is the role for you.

#### **Key skills**

Strong analytical, quantitative, and interpretative skills will help you thrive in our dynamic and collaborative work environment. You should also be adaptable, flexible, and resilient, able to effectively navigate and contribute to various situations.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

Our investment bankers are focused on developing long-term relationships and crafting strategies that help our clients grow and innovate. Our Advisory, Capital Markets, and Coverage (Industry) groups provide sound advice, offer clients access to funding, and execute transactions—all while helping clients manage risks in today’s complex environment.

Advisory groups work with corporations and institutions in all industries and partners across the bank to advise on mergers, acquisitions, and solutions to problems with multifaceted, complex and unique features. Our advisory teams play a crucial role in helping clients navigate complex transactions and achieve their strategic goals.

Capital Markets groups serve clients holistically in partnership with the Industry Coverage and Mergers and Acquisitions (M&A) groups. The firm offers a wide range of global services, from origination and structuring to financing and syndication.

Our Coverage teams provide in-depth, industry-specific expertise and regional market acumen—this is how we serve the evolving needs of global clients across a broad range of sectors, including Consumer & Retail, Energy & Infrastructure, Financial Institutions, Financial Investors, Healthcare, Industrials, Media & Communications, Public Sector, Real Estate, and Technology.

#### **Training**

You'll spend weeks with our leaders and top consultants learning about our products, services and business practices through interactive learning tools, case studies and modeling sessions. We provide extensive pre-training coursework to build your financial and economic expertise.

#### **Career progression**

High-performing analysts may have the opportunity to advance in a variety of roles with the firm.

Where to learn more

Explore life at JPM with this free & self-paced virtual experience. To learn more and register, visit the [Investment Banking page](https://www.theforage.com/virtual-internships/prototype/YD2kY95RQxQtXxFTS/JPM-IB-Virtual-Experience-Program "investment banking") on Forage.

\*Registration or completion of Forage virtual experience programs is optional and will not impact consideration or hiring decisions.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/investment-banking-fulltime-analyst.jpg)

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/hr-summer-analyst#main)

# Human Resources Analyst Development Program

Our people are our greatest asset and investing in attracting, retaining, and developing them is a top priority. Working in Human Resources is more than payroll and benefits; we have programs to recruit and train talent, as well as help them build fulfilling, rewarding careers. We’re constantly adding new initiatives to create engaged employees who are developing new skills. Joining our community means impacting millions of people and making sure we have the right talent to innovate for the future.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the Program

### What you’ll do

Experience the breadth of human resources strategies at one of the world’s largest financial services firms in this nine-week program. You’ll develop core skills that can be applied across the firm as you build a powerful network.

### Who we’re looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for highly motivated, analytical, and passionate people who will challenge the way we do things and who aren’t intimidated to provide feedback so we can improve.

You should be flexible, adaptable, and enjoy working with people. We’re always looking for innovative ways to improve our processes and engage employees, so bring your best ideas.

#### **Key skills**

Those with strong project management, communication, interpersonal, and strategic thinking skills will do well on our teams. You should be collaborative, open to new challenges, a natural problem-solver, and take ownership of your work.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

You’ll work on projects in areas such as compensation, recruiting, performance management, employee relations, and more. Your tasks might include compiling data packages for senior leaders and collaborating on succession plans.

#### **Training**

The program begins with orientation, followed by on-the-job training. Over the course of the summer, you’ll also have access to networking, senior speakers, and continued professional development.

#### **Career progression**

High performing interns who successfully complete the program may receive full-time offers within the bank.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/wealth-management-summer.jpg)

### Who we’re looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for highly motivated, analytical, and passionate people who will challenge the way we do things and who aren’t intimidated to provide feedback so we can improve.

You should be flexible, adaptable, and enjoy working with people. We’re always looking for innovative ways to improve our processes and engage employees, so bring your best ideas.

#### **Key skills**

Those with strong project management, communication, interpersonal, and strategic thinking skills will do well on our teams. You should be collaborative, open to new challenges, a natural problem-solver, and take ownership of your work.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

#### **Training**

The program begins with orientation, followed by on-the-job training. Over the course of the summer, you’ll also have access to networking, senior speakers, and continued professional development.

#### **Career progression**

High performing interns who successfully complete the program may receive full-time offers within the bank.

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/markets-seasonal-analyst#main)

# Markets Seasonal Analyst Internship Program

Become an expert in financial markets while working in a dynamic, collaborative and supportive team when you join Global Markets, Sales & Research. You’ll research and find exciting opportunities for our clients, help them execute complex transactions and develop deep relationships while gaining a thorough understanding of markets through data analysis.

Our Global Markets, Sales & Research group works collaboratively to help clients achieve their goals. Sales is in touch with institutional clients about insights and strategic ideas that are executed by traders. All of this is informed by comprehensive research into financial markets and companies that allows us to give the best possible advice.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/markets-seasonal-analyst#first-apply-now-link)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

Working on one of our dynamic teams, your day-to-day will vary based on your team and what’s going on in the markets. You will use your passion and critical thinking skills to solve real business problems, work on complex mathematical models and cutting-edge methodologies, and analyze data  to prepare innovative insights for clients.

You’ll also write reports, build updated financial models, and support multi-billion dollar transactions.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for inquisitive, creative thinkers who can react quickly to changing markets. We value diversity of perspectives, so we’re looking for people with different backgrounds, degrees, and experiences. Those who work well in team environments and are able to build long-term relationships with colleagues and clients do well here.

#### **Key skills**

You should have strong critical thinking and communication skills along with excellent analytical, quantitative, and interpersonal skills. You’ll need to be able to work independently and with our teams to produce accurate, detailed work under pressure. Experience with Java, C++, Python, or Matlab is a plus.

### What we offer

Our seasonal internship program runs from three to six months during your academic year, and is available in specific European locations.

You will learn about global market strategies and asset classes through ongoing education and hands-on experience.  Guided by industry leaders, you’ll work on trading projects and gain exposure to our best-in-class client franchises.

#### **On-the-job experience**

You will spend your time exploring the sophisticated financial solutions we deliver across asset classes. You’ll gain experience in proposing trade ideas, managing risk, and working with clients, all while being part of a “desk.” You’ll learn about the solutions and products we offer for clients to manage any market conditions.

#### **Training**

The program will begin with classroom training covering the technical and practical skills that will help you flourish.

#### **Career Progression**

Depending on performance, you may be offered the opportunity to join our traditional summer internship program or as a full time employee after university.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/markets-seasonal-analyst.jpg)

### What you'll do

Working on one of our dynamic teams, your day-to-day will vary based on your team and what’s going on in the markets. You will use your passion and critical thinking skills to solve real business problems, work on complex mathematical models and cutting-edge methodologies, and analyze data  to prepare innovative insights for clients.

You’ll also write reports, build updated financial models, and support multi-billion dollar transactions.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for inquisitive, creative thinkers who can react quickly to changing markets. We value diversity of perspectives, so we’re looking for people with different backgrounds, degrees, and experiences. Those who work well in team environments and are able to build long-term relationships with colleagues and clients do well here.

#### **Key skills**

You should have strong critical thinking and communication skills along with excellent analytical, quantitative, and interpersonal skills. You’ll need to be able to work independently and with our teams to produce accurate, detailed work under pressure. Experience with Java, C++, Python, or Matlab is a plus.

### What we offer

Our seasonal internship program runs from three to six months during your academic year, and is available in specific European locations.

You will learn about global market strategies and asset classes through ongoing education and hands-on experience.  Guided by industry leaders, you’ll work on trading projects and gain exposure to our best-in-class client franchises.

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/research-technologies-summer-associate-program#main)

# Research Technologies Summer Associate Program

Our team cultivates investigations into emerging technology areas, produces research solutions, and contributes to the firm's intellectual property (IP). We partner with internal project teams, senior management, and external communities to advance research in a way that is beneficial to the firm's technical teams. The team is comprised of innovative problem-solvers with a passion for turning scientific research into commercial value.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

As a Research Technologies Summer Associate, you'll collaborate on research projects relating to quantum computing algorithms and applications, specifically for optimization, AI, cryptography, and finance, with an aim to develop novel research solutions to problems faced by internal teams. You will work with other researchers to document your findings in scientific papers and contribute to JPMorganChase’s IP by pursuing necessary protections of generated IP.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We are seeking colleagues with excellent analytical, quantitative, and problem-solving skills and demonstrated research ability. We value strong communication skills and the ability to present findings to a non-technical audience.

#### **Key skills**

- Experience with quantum computing algorithms and applications
- Excellent knowledge of Python programming
- Working knowledge of common research evaluation frameworks and techniques

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

Your professional growth and development will be supported throughout the internship program via project work related to your academic and professional interests, mentorship, interaction with senior stakeholders, and more.

#### **Training**

Through research and hands-on work experience, you'll develop solutions and technology that drive innovation and help our stakeholders improve and protect our customer and client experiences every day.

#### **Career progression**

The specialized knowledge and skills gained through the program will prepare you for a successful career at the firm. High-performing interns who successfully complete the program may receive an offer.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/inside-the-prog.jpg)

### What you'll do

As a Research Technologies Summer Associate, you'll collaborate on research projects relating to quantum computing algorithms and applications, specifically for optimization, AI, cryptography, and finance, with an aim to develop novel research solutions to problems faced by internal teams. You will work with other researchers to document your findings in scientific papers and contribute to JPMorganChase’s IP by pursuing necessary protections of generated IP.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We are seeking colleagues with excellent analytical, quantitative, and problem-solving skills and demonstrated research ability. We value strong communication skills and the ability to present findings to a non-technical audience.

#### **Key skills**

- Experience with quantum computing algorithms and applications
- Excellent knowledge of Python programming
- Working knowledge of common research evaluation frameworks and techniques

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

Your professional growth and development will be supported throughout the internship program via project work related to your academic and professional interests, mentorship, interaction with senior stakeholders, and more.

#### **Training**

Through research and hands-on work experience, you'll develop solutions and technology that drive innovation and help our stakeholders improve and protect our customer and client experiences every day.

#### **Career progression**

The specialized knowledge and skills gained through the program will prepare you for a successful career at the firm. High-performing interns who successfully complete the program may receive an offer.

# Aspiring Professionals Program

We’ve partnered with the Social Mobility Foundation to bring students to our London and Glasgow offices for an overview of our business areas, opportunities to showcase a project you will work on, and practical skills development, such as CV and interview techniques.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the Program

### What you’ll do

Join us for a two-week, in-person, work placement in our London or Glasgow office to experience what a career in financial services or technology might look like. You will have an opportunity to not only learn about our firm and gain invaluable employability skills, but also to have your very own JPMorganChase mentor to help support you throughout the week and after the program as you begin to consider your future options.

### Who we’re looking for

We’re looking for inquisitive minds with enthusiasm to learn. You should have excellent communication skills, the ability to learn quickly, be determined, intelligent, and passionate about a career in financial services.

### What we offer

During this two-week program, you’ll experience what it’s like working in our London or Glasgow offices and gain an understanding of our lines of business. We’ll show you how we serve clients and how all our businesses work together to help them meet their financial goals. You’ll have the opportunity to work with a mentor, meet with senior leaders, experience our world-class training, and develop skills that will serve as an important foundation for your future career.

title: Chase Digital Development Programme
raw_text:
We and our partners use cookies and other tools for advertising, to help stop fraud, and for other purposes. By using this site, you agree to how your information is used as outlined in our [Privacy Policy](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/legal/privacy).

[Skip to main content](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/chase-digital-development-programme-fulltime#main)

# Chase Digital Development Programme

We know that people want great value combined with an excellent experience, from a bank they can trust. That's why we're revolutionizing mobile banking, by creating seamless digital journeys that our customers will love. For us, that means keeping ourselves customer-obsessed and always being open to trying new things. Above all, it's about working with people who are passionate about building the bank of the future.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/chase-digital-development-programme-fulltime#first-apply-now-link)

## Apply now



Please note, only locations where this program is currently open will be displayed. If you're not able to find your desired location, please follow us on Facebook or Linkedin for program application updates.


- [**2026 Chase Digital Development Programme – Full-time Analyst (London)** \\
\\
Deadline: February 15, 2026\\
\\
Europe, Middle East, & Africa\\
\\
London\\
\\
Apply now](https://jpmc.fa.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX/job/210705365)
- [**2026 Chase Digital Development Programme – Summer Internship (London)** \\
\\
Deadline: February 15, 2026\\
\\
Europe, Middle East, & Africa\\
\\
London\\
\\
Apply now](https://jpmc.fa.oraclecloud.com/hcmUI/CandidateExperience/en/sites/CX/job/210702292)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

Our programme gives you the tools and experience you need to become a skilled Product Manager, by empowering you to harness insights and help us build innovative customer experiences in a full-time, two-year programme. Your time will be made up of three eight-month rotations, so you can see what goes into developing intuitive experiences, from start to finish.

As a Product Manager, you'll get involved with every part of the process. From coming up with fresh ideas, penning strategies and iterating designs with our developers, to implementing features with our engineers, and looking at marketing, data and servicing with our customer experience team – you'll see exactly what it takes to get genius ideas into the hands of our customers.

### Who we're looking for

We understand that everyone's unique – and that diversity of thought, experience, and backgrounds is what makes a good team great. By bringing people with different points of view together, we can represent everyone and truly reflect the communities we serve. Specifically for this programme, we're looking for graduates in their final year, or who graduated less than a year ago.

Fintech is a fast-paced, ever-evolving world. So, we like working with people who are adaptable, open to new things, and don't mind making mistakes. To help us tackle problems together, we also look for skills like critical thinking and problem-solving, as well as having a curious mindset and being a great communicator.

So that you can get the most out of the programme, having a motivated, resourceful spirit is key, as well as a hunger to learn more about technology and financial services. If you're a graduate with fresh ideas and an entrepreneurial mindset, we'd love to hear from you.

### What we offer

#### **Collaborative culture**

By joining the Chase Digital Development Programme, you'll be part of a team that’s friendly, committed, hard-working, and growth-orientated. Over two years, we'll show you how to build new products and journeys, all while balancing business innovation with what our customers need—and making our mark as an exciting player in fintech.

#### **International growth**

Since we launched a digital bank in the UK, we're planning our next phase of growth, including developing new products and expanding into new markets – so it's an incredibly exciting time to jump on board.

#### **Career development**

You'll work with teams across lots of different areas of the business, and have access to a huge range of skill sets, leadership styles, and opportunities to learn.

Through this network, we'll help you evolve your professional skills with mentorship, training, and plenty of hands-on experience. You'll also spend time learning from your peers as you support each other to grow.

#### **Long term opportunities**

After the programme, we'll work together to find the best fit for you. We're currently expanding our European footprint – so if you have a second language, be sure to let us know.

![](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/content/dam/jpmorganchase/images/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/chase-digital-development-programme-fulltime.jpg)

# Global Private Bank Associate Program

For more than 200 years, we have been working with innovators, entrepreneurs, industry leaders, and their families to help them achieve their unique ambitions, secure their legacies, and make a difference in the world. That means providing meaningful, tailored advice now and across generations. The Private Bank helps clients plan, invest, borrow, and bank to create the life and legacy they envision.

[Apply now](https://www.jpmorganchase.com/careers/explore-opportunities/programs/wealth-management-summer-associate#first-apply-now-link)

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

In this internship program, you will focus on business generation and client engagement. You'll gain exposure to every facet of global private banks, including investments, lending, banking, and trust and estate planning.

The course of the internship will run over nine weeks in the North America and Asia Pacific locations.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We're looking for highly motivated individuals who are entrepreneurial with a desire to build a business. You should be a self-starter who thrives and excels in a competitive industry. You should also have good judgment, discretion when working with confidential information, and integrity.

#### **Key skills**

You'll need to have excellent communication and presentation skills, strong quantitative and organizational skills, a passion for investing, and the ability to multitask. We're looking for those who focus on the client experience and will work tirelessly on the client's behalf.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

Private banking is a relationship. By understanding our clients and their finances, we are able to deliver highly customized and comprehensive solutions in order to help them achieve their goals. Throughout the program, you will work with a team of advisors and specialists to help bring in new clients and serve existing customers.

#### **Training**

The program begins with a five-day orientation that introduces you to the firm and our Global Private Bank business. It also includes skills development sessions in markets, sales, and client management.

#### **Career progression**

This internship program prepares you for your next career step. Top performers who successfully complete the program may receive an offer of full-time employment.

# Commercial & Investment Bank Innovation Development Program

Launch a product management career with a high-impact rotational experience. You’ll support innovative products and solutions, while developing the skills needed to lead at scale.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

Over nine weeks, you'll gain hands-on product management experience through immersive training, real-world projects, and close mentorship.

You’ll rotate through two product teams within the Global Bank, gaining exposure to different products, client segments, and product lifecycle stages.

During the internship, you’ll explore areas like product strategy, data and analytics, change management, and business transformation. Whether you’re helping to draft product requirements, analyzing customer behavior, or supporting a feature launch, you’ll be part of a collaborative, tight-knit community within one of the most respected firms in the industry.

### Who we're looking for

We're looking for individuals with an entrepreneurial mindset and a passion for driving business transformation and product development.

Ideal candidates are excellent communicators, intellectually curious, and are great problem solvers. They possess a strong work ethic, are self-starters who drive projects forward and meet deadlines.

#### **Opportunity to drive impact**

Advise senior leaders and deliver key product capabilities and strategic insights to enhance the customer experience.

Develop valuable critical thinking and analytical skills through program and on-the-job training, with a variety of post-program opportunities.

Gain exposure to a full range of banking products & services, and develop solutions for domestic and international clients.

# Commercial Real Estate Program

Our Commercial Real Estate group works with developers, investors, owners, companies, investment funds, government agencies, community development financial institutions, and both for-profit and not-for-profit organizations to build the future of our communities. We’re focused on developing client relationships where we build the future together.

Applications are currently closed

## Inside the program

### What you'll do

In this full-time role, you’ll will gain a unique perspective on all aspects of commercial real estate. You will collaborate with colleagues, investors, owners, and other organizations to deliver best-in-class financial solutions, plus have the opportunity to develop a strong foundation of financial skills, including how to structure and evaluate projects while building lasting client relationships.

### Who we're looking for

#### **Valued qualities**

We’re looking for well-rounded, self-motivated critical thinkers who have demonstrated an interest in real estate and have experience in finance, accounting, real estate, economics, and/or public policy.

#### **Key skills**

You should have strong interpersonal and analytical skills with sharp attention to detail. Coursework or previous internship experience in real estate, finance, accounting, or economics is a plus.

### What we offer

#### **On-the-job experience**

Develop and hone your financial real estate  skills within one of three distinct commercial real estate lines of business. You’ll gain experience in financial statement analysis and modeling, crafting credit memos, cash flow analysis, credit structuring and more.

#### **Training**

Following intensive training and orientation, you’ll work alongside our banking team to provide support to our clients and gain exposure to all facets of commercial real estate banking.

#### **Career progression**

Upon successful completion of the program, high-performing analysts may be considered for additional opportunities or advancement within the firm.

# Financial Services Apprenticeship
